label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sponsor Device Identifier
cdisc_notes: A sequence of characters used by the sponsor to uniquely identify a specific device. Used to represent a device associated in some way with the adverse event. SPDEVID values are defined in the Device Identifiers (DI) domain.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external identifier such as a serial number on an SAE reporting form.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. It may be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number on an Adverse Events CRF page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Reported Term for the Adverse Event
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the event.

label: Modified Reported Term
cdisc_notes: If AETERM is modified to facilitate coding, then AEMODIFY will contain the modified text.

label: Lowest Level Term
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived text description of the lowest level term.

label: Lowest Level Term Code
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived code for the lowest level term.

label: Dictionary-Derived Term
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived text description of AETERM or AEMODIFY. Equivalent to the Preferred Term (PT in MedDRA). The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to map the terms utilizing the external codelist element in the Define-XML document.

label: Preferred Term Code
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived code for the preferred term.

label: High Level Term
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived text description of the high level term for the primary system organ class (SOC).

label: High Level Term Code
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived code for the high level term for the primary SOC.

label: High Level Group Term
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived text description of the high level group term for the primary SOC.

label: High Level Group Term Code
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived code for the high level group term for the primary SOC.

label: Category for Adverse Event
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records. Examples: "BLEEDING", "NEUROPSYCHIATRIC".

label: Subcategory for Adverse Event
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of adverse event. Example: "NEUROLOGIC".

label: Pre-Specified Adverse Event
cdisc_notes: A value of "Y" indicates that this adverse event was prespecified on the CRF. Values are null for spontaneously reported events (i.e., those collected as free-text verbatim terms).

label: Body System or Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Dictionary derived. Body system or organ class used by the sponsor from the coding dictionary (e.g., MedDRA). When using a multi-axial dictionary such as MedDRA, this should contain the SOC used for the sponsor's analyses and summary tables, which may not necessarily be the primary SOC.

label: Body System or Organ Class Code
cdisc_notes: Dictionary derived. Code for the body system or organ class used by the sponsor. When using a multi-axial dictionary such as MedDRA, this should contain the SOC used for the sponsor's analyses and summary tables, which may not necessarily be the primary SOC.

label: Primary System Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived text description of the primary SOC. Will be the same as AEBODSYS if the primary SOC was used for analysis.

label: Primary System Organ Class Code
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived code for the primary SOC. Will be the same as AEBDSYCD if the primary SOC was used for analysis.

label: Location of Event
cdisc_notes: Describes anatomical location relevant for the event (e.g., "ARM" for skin rash).

label: Severity/Intensity
cdisc_notes: The severity or intensity of the event. Examples: "MILD", "MODERATE", "SEVERE".

label: Serious Event
cdisc_notes: Is this a serious event? Valid values are "Y" and "N".

label: Action Taken with Study Treatment
cdisc_notes: Describes changes to the study treatment as a result of the event. AEACN is specifically for the relationship to study treatment. AEACNOTH is for actions unrelated to dose adjustments of study treatment. Examples of AEACN values include ICH E2B values: "DRUG WITHDRAWN", "DOSE REDUCED", "DOSE INCREASED", "DOSE NOT CHANGED", "UNKNOWN" and "NOT APPLICABLE".

label: Other Action Taken
cdisc_notes: Describes other actions taken as a result of the event that are unrelated to dose adjustments of study treatment. Usually reported as free text. Example: "TREATMENT UNBLINDED. PRIMARY CARE PHYSICIAN NOTIFIED".

label: Action Taken with Device
cdisc_notes: An action taken with a device as the result of the event. The device may or may not be a device under study.

label: Causality
cdisc_notes: Records the investigator's opinion as to the causality of the event to the treatment. ICH does not establish any required or recommended terms for non-device relatedness. ICH E2A and E2B examples include (up-cased here for alignment to SDTM conventions) terms such as "NOT RELATED", "UNLIKELY RELATED", "POSSIBLY RELATED", "RELATED", but these example terms do not establish any conventions or expectations. Controlled terminology may be defined in the future. Check with regulatory authority for population of this variable.

label: Relationship of Event to Device
cdisc_notes:
A judgment as to the likelihood that the device caused the adverse event. The relationship is to a device identified in the data (i.e., has an SPDEVID). The device may be ancillary or under study. 
 Terminology: 
 * In the EU, follow the European Commission Guidelines on Medical Devices, Clinical Investigations: SAE Reporting (MEDDEV 2.7/3) (e.g., Not Related, Unlikely, Possible, Probable, Causal Relationship), with device-specific definitions. 
 * No required Controlled Terminology in US.

label: Relationship to Non-Study Treatment
cdisc_notes: Records the investigator's opinion as to whether the event may have been due to a treatment other than study drug. May be reported as free text. Example: "MORE LIKELY RELATED TO ASPIRIN USE".

label: Pattern of Adverse Event
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate the pattern of the event over time. Examples: "INTERMITTENT", "CONTINUOUS", "SINGLE EVENT".

label: Outcome of Adverse Event
cdisc_notes: Description of the outcome of an event.

label: Involves Cancer
cdisc_notes: Was the serious event associated with the development of cancer? Valid values are "Y" and "N". This is a legacy seriousness criterion. It is not included in ICH E2A or E2B.

label: Congenital Anomaly or Birth Defect
cdisc_notes: Was the serious event associated with congenital anomaly or birth defect? Valid values are "Y" and "N".

label: Persist or Signif Disability/Incapacity
cdisc_notes: Did the serious event result in persistent or significant disability/incapacity? Valid values are "Y" and "N".

label: Results in Death
cdisc_notes: Did the serious event result in death? Valid values are "Y" and "N".

label: Requires or Prolongs Hospitalization
cdisc_notes: Did the serious event require or prolong hospitalization? Valid values are "Y" and "N".

label: Is Life Threatening
cdisc_notes: Was the serious event life-threatening? Valid values are "Y" and "N".

label: Occurred with Overdose
cdisc_notes: Did the serious event occur with an overdose? Valid values are "Y" and "N". This is a legacy seriousness criterion. It is not included in ICH E2A or E2B.

label: Other Medically Important Serious Event
cdisc_notes: Do additional categories for seriousness apply? Valid values are "Y" and "N".

label: Needs Intervention to Prevent Impairment
cdisc_notes: Records whether medical or surgical intervention was necessary to preclude permanent impairment of a body function, or to prevent permanent damage to a body structure, with either situation suspected to be due to the use of a medical product. This variable is used in conjunction with the other "seriousness" variables (e.g., fatal, life-threatening). It is part of the US federal government definition of a serious adverse event; see 21 CFR Part 803.3(w)(3).

label: Unanticipated Adverse Device Effect
cdisc_notes:
Any serious adverse effect on health or safety or any life-threatening problem or death caused by or associated with a device, if that effect, problem, or death was not previously identified in nature, severity, or degree of incidence in the investigational plan or application (including a supplementary plan or application), 
 or 
 any other unanticipated serious problem associated with a device that relates to the rights, safety, or welfare of subjects. (21 CFR Part 812.3(s)). 
 This variable applies only to serious AEs and should hold collected data; if the value is derived, it should be held in ADaM.

label: Rel of AE to Non-Dev-Rel Study Activity
cdisc_notes:
The investigator's opinion as to the causality of the event as related to other protocol-required activities, actions, or assessments (e.g., medication changes, tests/assessments, other procedures). The relationship is to a protocol-specified, non-device-related activity where the device is identified in the data (i.e., has an SPDEVID). The device may be ancillary or under study. 
 Terminology: 
 * In the EU, follow the European Commission Guidelines on Medical Devices, Clinical Investigations: SAE Reporting (MEDDEV 2.7/3) (e.g., Not Related, Unlikely, Possible, Probable, Causal Relationship), with device-specific definitions. 
 * No required Controlled Terminology in US.

label: Rel of AE to Device-Related Procedure
cdisc_notes:
The investigator's opinion as to the likelihood that the device-related study procedure (e.g., implant/insertion, revision/adjustment, explant/removal) caused the AE. The relationship is to a device-related procedure where the device is identified in the data (i.e., has an SPDEVID). The device may be ancillary or under study. 
 Terminology: 
 * In the EU, follow the European Commission Guidelines on Medical Devices, Clinical Investigations: SAE Reporting (MEDDEV 2.7/3) (e.g., Not Related, Unlikely, Possible, Probable, Causal Relationship), with device-specific definitions. 
 * No required Controlled Terminology in US.

label: Concomitant or Additional Trtmnt Given
cdisc_notes: Was another treatment given because of the occurrence of the event? Valid values are "Y" and "N".

label: Standard Toxicity Grade
cdisc_notes: Toxicity grade according to a standard toxicity scale (e.g., Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events, CTCAE). Sponsors should specify the name of the scale and version used in the metadata (see assumption 7d). If value is from a numeric scale, represent only the number (e.g., "2", not "Grade 2").

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the adverse event. Examples: "SCREENING", "TREATMENT", "FOLLOW-UP".

label: Start Date/Time of Adverse Event
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of the adverse event represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: End Date/Time of Adverse Event
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the adverse event represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Start of Adverse Event
cdisc_notes: Study day of start of adverse event relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Day of End of Adverse Event
cdisc_notes: Study day of end of event relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Duration of Adverse Event
cdisc_notes: Collected duration and unit of an adverse event. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived from start and end date/times. Example: "P1DT2H" (for 1 day, 2 hours).

label: Start Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the observation as being before, during, or after the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics.

label: End Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Describes the end of the event relative to the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point (RFSTDTC) and a discrete ending point (RFENDTC) of the trial. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the event as being before or after the reference time point defined by variable AEENTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description of date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the reference point referred to by AEENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25", "VISIT 2".

label: Reasonable Possibility AE Caus by Device
AE Related to Disease Under Study
AE Onset Status on Day of IP Admin
AE Caused Subject to Withdraw from Study
Date/Time AE Met Criteria for Serious AE
Date Investigator Aware of Serious AE
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID, or UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number from the procedure or test page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains.This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains.This will usually be a many-to-one relationship.

label: Reported Agent Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim medication name that is either preprinted or collected on a CRF.

label: Modified Reported Name
cdisc_notes: If AGTRT is modified to facilitate coding, then AGMODIFY will contain the modified text.

label: Standardized Agent Name
cdisc_notes: Standardized or dictionary-derived text description of AGTRT or AGMODIFY. Equivalent to the generic medication name in WHO Drug. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to map the terms utilizing the external codelist element in the Define-XML document. If an intervention term does not have a decode value in the dictionary, then AGDECOD will be left blank.

label: Category for Agent
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of agent. Examples: "CHALLENGE AGENT", "PET TRACER".

label: Subcategory for Agent
cdisc_notes: Further categorization of agent.

label: AG Pre-Specified
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate whether ("Y"/null) information about the use of a specific agent was solicited on the CRF.

label: AG Occurrence
cdisc_notes: When the use of specific agent is solicited, AGOCCUR is used to indicate whether ("Y"/"N") use of the agent occurred. Values are null for agents not specifically solicited.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question about a prespecified agent was not answered. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Procedure Agent Not Collected
cdisc_notes: Describes the reason a response to a question about the occurrence of a procedure agent was not collected. Used in conjunction with AGSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Indication
cdisc_notes: Denotes the indication for the intervention (e.g., why the therapy was taken or administered).

label: Agent Class
cdisc_notes: Drug class. May be obtained from coding. When coding to a single class, populate with class value. If using a dictionary and coding to multiple classes, follow guidance in Section 4.2.8.3, Multiple Values for a Non-result Qualifier Variable, or omit AGCLAS.

label: Agent Class Code
cdisc_notes: Class code corresponding to AGCLAS. Drug class. May be obtained from coding. When coding to a single class, populate with class code. If using a dictionary and coding to multiple classes, follow guidance in Section 4.2.8.3, Multiple Values for a Non-result Qualifier Variable, or omit AGCLASCD.

label: Dose per Administration
cdisc_notes: Amount of AGTRT taken.

label: Dose Description
cdisc_notes: Dosing amounts or a range of dosing information collected in text form. Units may be stored in AGDOSU. Examples: "200-400", "15-20".

label: Dose Units
cdisc_notes: Units for AGDOSE and AGDOSTXT. Examples: "ng", "mg", "mg/kg".

label: Dose Form
cdisc_notes: Dose form for AGTRT. Examples: "TABLET", "AEROSOL".

label: Dosing Frequency per Interval
cdisc_notes: Usually expressed as the number of repeated administrations of AGDOSE within a specific time period. Example: "ONCE".

label: Total Daily Dose
cdisc_notes: Total daily dose of --TRT using the units in --DOSU. Used when dosing is collected as Total Daily Dose.

label: Intended Dose Regimen
cdisc_notes: Text description of the (intended) schedule or regimen for the Intervention. Example: "TWO WEEKS ON, TWO WEEKS OFF".

label: Route of Administration
cdisc_notes: Route of administration for AGTRT. Example: "ORAL".

label: Lot Number
cdisc_notes: Lot number for the intervention described in --TRT.

label: Location of Administration
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of an intervention, such as an injection site. Example: ARM for an injection.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing laterality of intervention administration. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing directionality of intervention administration. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Fasting Status
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify fasting status. Valid values include "Y", "N", "U" or null if not relevant.

label: Treatment Vehicle
cdisc_notes: Vehicle for administration of treatment, such as a liquid in which the treatment drug is dissolved. Example: "SALINE".

label: Treatment Vehicle Amount
cdisc_notes: Amount of the prepared product (treatment + vehicle) administered or given. Note: should not be diluent amount alone.

label: Treatment Vehicle Amount Units
cdisc_notes: Units for the prepared product (treatment + vehicle). Examples: "mL", "mg".

label: Reason for Dose Adjustment
cdisc_notes: Describes reason or explanation of why a dose is adjusted. Examples "ADVERSE EVENT", "INSUFFICIENT RESPONSE", "NON-MEDICAL REASON".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. \n 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. \n 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the agent administration started.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the agent administration started.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation.

label: Start Date/Time of Agent
cdisc_notes: The date/time when administration of the treatment indicated by AGTRT and the dosing variables began.

label: End Date/Time of Agent
cdisc_notes: The date/time when administration of the treatment indicated by AGTRT and the dosing variables ended.

label: Study Day of Collection
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of Start of Agent
cdisc_notes: Study day of start of agent relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Day of End of Agent
cdisc_notes: Study day of end of agent relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Duration of Agent
cdisc_notes: Collected duration for an agent episode. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived from start and end date/times.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See --TPTNUM and --TPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned Elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (--TPTREF) such as "Previous Dose" or "Previous Meal". This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by --ELTM, --TPTNUM, --TPT, --STINT, and --ENINT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Start Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Describes the start of the agent relative to sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point (represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics). If information such as "PRIOR", "ONGOING", or "CONTINUING" was collected, this information may be translated into AGSTRF. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Describes the end of the agent relative to the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point (represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics). If information such as "PRIOR", "ONGOING", or "CONTINUING" was collected, this information may be translated into AGENRF. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: Start Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the agent as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable AGSTTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: Start Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the reference point referred to by AGSTRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-15", "VISIT 1".

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the agent as being before or after the reference time point defined by variable AGENTPT. Identifies the end of the agent as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable AGENTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the reference point referred to by AGENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25", "VISIT 2".

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Associated Persons Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier for a single associated person, a group of associated persons, or a pool of associated persons.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject (or within a parameter, in the case of the Trial Summary domain). May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1. Should be assigned to be in a consistent chronological order.

label: Reported Term for the Medical History
cdisc_notes: Topic variable for an event observation, which is the verbatim or pre-specified name of the event.

label: Medical History Event Pre-Specified
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate whether the event described by --TERM was pre-specified on a CRF. Value is Y for pre-specified events, null for spontaneously reported events.

label: Medical History Occurrence
cdisc_notes: Used to record whether a pre-specified event occurred when information about the occurrence of a specific event is solicited.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Related Subject
cdisc_notes: Identifier for a related subject or pool of subjects. RSUBJID may be populated with the USUBJID of the related subject or the POOLID of the related pool. RSUBJID will be null for data about associated persons who are related to the study but not to any study subjects.

label: Subject, Device, or Study Relationship
cdisc_notes: If RSUBJID is populated, describes the relationship of the associated person(s) identified in APID to the subject or pool identified in RSUBJID. If RDEVID is populated, describes the relationship of the associated person(s) identified in APID to the subject or pool identified in RDEVID. If RSUBJID and RDEVID are null, SREL describes the relationship of the associated person(s) identified in APID to the study identified in STUDYID.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sponsor Device Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for a device.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject. May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external identifier for the specimen affected or created by the event.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Optional sponsor-defined reference number. Example: Line number on a CRF page.

label: Reported Term for the Biospecimen Event
cdisc_notes: Topic variable for an event observation, which is the verbatim or pre-specified name of the event.

label: Modified Reported Term
cdisc_notes: If the value for BETERM is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Dictionary-Derived Term
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived text description of BETERM or BEMODIFY, if applicable.

label: Category for Biospecimen Event
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Example: COLLECTION, PREPARATION, TRANSPORT.

label: Subcategory for Biospecimen Event
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of BECAT values.

label: Anatomical Location of Event
cdisc_notes: Describes the anatomical location relevant for the event (e.g. BRAIN, LUNG).

label: Accountable Party
cdisc_notes: Party accountable for the transferable object (e.g. specimen) as a result of the activity performed in the associated BETERM variable. The party could be an individual (e.g., subject), an organization (e.g., sponsor), or a location that is a proxy for an individual or organization (e.g., site). It is usually a somewhat general term that is further identified in the BEPRTYID variable.

label: Identification of Accountable Party
cdisc_notes: Identification of the specific party accountable for the transferable object (e.g. Specimen) after the action in BETERM is taken. Used in conjunction with BEPARTY.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Should be an integer.

label: Date/Time of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Date and time of specimen collection.

label: Start Date/Time of Biospecimen Event
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of the event.

label: End Date/Time of Biospecimen Event
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the event.

label: Study Day of Start of Biospecimen Event
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of start of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Biospecimen Event
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Duration of Biospecimen Event
cdisc_notes: Collected duration and unit of a biospecimen event. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived from start and end date/times. Example: P1DT2H (for 1 day, 2 hours).

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sponsor Device Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for a device.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness within a dataset for a subject. May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier.

label: Biospecimen Test Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short character value for BSTEST used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical format to a horizontal format. The short value can be up to 8 characters. Examples: VOLUME, RIN.

label: Biospecimen Test Name
cdisc_notes: Long name for BSTESTCD. Examples: Volume, RNA Integrity Number.

label: Category for Biospecimen Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Example: MEASUREMENT, QUALITY.

label: Subcategory for Biospecimen Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of BSCAT values.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Unit for BSORRES. Examples: mg, mL.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from BSORRES in a standard format or standard units. BSSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in BSSTRESN.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from BSSTRESC. BSSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized unit used for BSSTRESC and BSSTRESN.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a test was not done, or was attempted but did not generate a result. Should be null or have a value of NOT DONE.

label: Reason Test Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with BSSTAT when value is NOT DONE.

label: Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: Name or identifier of the vendor (e.g., laboratory) that provided the test results.

label: Specimen Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: SERUM, PLASMA, URINE, SOFT TISSUE.

label: Anatomical Region of Specimen
cdisc_notes: Defines the specific anatomical or biological region of a tissue, organ specimen or the region from which the specimen is obtained, as defined in the protocol, such as a section or part of what is described in the BSSPEC variable. Examples: CORTEX, MEDULLA, MUCOSA.

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: Defines the condition of the specimen. Examples: HEMOLYZED, ICTERIC, LIPEMIC.

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: SPECTROPHOTOMETRY, ELECTROPHORESIS.

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally-derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Should be an integer.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the assessment was made.

label: Date/Time of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Date and time of specimen collection.

label: Study Day of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Study day of specimen collection relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See BSTPTNUM and BSTPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numerical version of BSTPT used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (BSTPTREF). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date time variable, but an interval, represented as ISO duration.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Name of the fixed reference point referred to by BSELTM, BSTPTNUM, and BSTPT. Examples: PREVIOUS DOSE, PREVIOUS MEAL.

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by BSTPTREF.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to link together a block of related records for a subject within a domain.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external identifier (e.g., lab specimen ID, UUID for an ECG waveform or medical image).

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Reported Term for the Clinical Event
cdisc_notes: Term for the medical condition or event. Most likely preprinted on CRF.

label: Modified Reported Term
cdisc_notes: If the value for --TERM is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Lowest Level Term
cdisc_notes: MedDRA Lowest Level Term.

label: Lowest Level Term Code
cdisc_notes: MedDRA Lowest Level Term code.

label: Dictionary-Derived Term
cdisc_notes: Controlled terminology for the name of the clinical event. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to map the terms utilizing the external codelist element in the Define-XML document.

label: Preferred Term Code
cdisc_notes: MedDRA Preferred Term code.

label: High Level Term
cdisc_notes: MedDRA High Level Term from the primary path.

label: High Level Term Code
cdisc_notes: MedDRA High Level Term code from the primary path.

label: High Level Group Term
cdisc_notes: MedDRA High Level Group Term from the primary path.

label: High Level Group Term Code
cdisc_notes: MedDRA High Level Group Term code from the primary path.

label: Category for the Clinical Event
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records.

label: Subcategory for the Clinical Event
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of the condition or event.

label: Clinical Event Pre-specified
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate whether the event in CETERM was prespecified. Value is "Y" for prespecified events and null for spontaneously reported events.

label: Clinical Event Occurrence
cdisc_notes: Used when the occurrence of specific events is solicited, to indicate whether or not a clinical event occurred. Values are null for spontaneously reported events.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: The status indicates that a question from a prespecified list was not answered.

label: Reason Clinical Event Not Collected
cdisc_notes: Describes the reason clinical event data was not collected. Used in conjunction with CESTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Body System or Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived. Body system or organ class that is involved in an event or measurement from a standard hierarchy (e.g., MedDRA). When using a multi-axial dictionary such as MedDRA, this should contain the SOC used for the sponsor's analyses and summary tables, which may not necessarily be the primary SOC.

label: Body System or Organ Class Code
cdisc_notes: MedDRA System Organ Class code corresponding to --BODSYS assigned for analysis.

label: Primary System Organ Class
cdisc_notes: MedDRA primary System Organ Class associated with the event.

label: Primary System Organ Class Code
cdisc_notes: MedDRA primary System Organ Class code.

label: Location of Event
cdisc_notes: Describes anatomical location relevant for the event. Example: "ARM" for skin rash.

label: Severity/Intensity
cdisc_notes: The severity or intensity of the event. Examples: "MILD", "MODERATE", "SEVERE".

label: Causality
cdisc_notes: Records the investigator's opinion as to the causality of the event to the treatment. ICH E2A and E2B examples include "NOT RELATED", "UNLIKELY RELATED", "POSSIBLY RELATED", "RELATED". Controlled Terminology may be defined in the future. Check with regulatory authority for population of this variable.

label: Pattern of Event
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate the pattern of the event over time. Examples: "INTERMITTENT", "CONTINUOUS", "SINGLE EVENT".

label: Outcome of Event
cdisc_notes: Description of the outcome of an event. Examples: "RECOVERED/RESOLVED", "FATAL".

label: Concomitant or Additional Trtmnt Given
cdisc_notes: Was another treatment given because of the occurrence of the event? Valid values are "Y", "N", and null.

label: Standard Toxicity Grade
cdisc_notes: Toxicity grade according to a standard toxicity scale (e.g., Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) v3.0). Sponsor should specify name of the scale and version used in the metadata. If value is from a numeric scale, represent only the number (e.g., "2", not "Grade 2").

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the clinical event started.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the clinical event.

label: Date/Time of Event Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time for the clinical event observation represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Start Date/Time of Clinical Event
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of the clinical event represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: End Date/Time of Clinical Event
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the clinical event, represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Event Collection
cdisc_notes: Study day of clinical event collection, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics. This formula should be consistent across the submission.

label: Study Day of Start of Event
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of start of the clinical event expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Event
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of the clinical event expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Collected Duration
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of an event, intervention, or finding. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

label: Start Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Describes the start of the clinical event relative to the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point (represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics). \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Describes the end of the event relative to the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point (represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics). \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: Start Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the observation as being before or after the reference time point defined by variable CESTTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: Start Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by --STRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-15", "VISIT 1".

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable CEENTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the reference point referred to by CEENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25", "VISIT 2".

label: Event Sent to Committee for Furth Assmnt
Event Identified During Unsch. Outpt Vis
MRS at Curr Vis or 90 D After Cerebr Ev
Any Prophyla Med. Prior Study Drug Infus
Maximum Intensity after end of eDiary
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sponsor Device Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for a device.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID, or UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. Example: a number preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or record identifier defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: line number on a concomitant medication page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Reported Name of Drug, Med, or Therapy
cdisc_notes: Verbatim medication name that is either preprinted or collected on a CRF.

label: Modified Reported Name
cdisc_notes: If CMTRT is modified to facilitate coding, then CMMODIFY will contain the modified text.

label: Standardized Medication Name
cdisc_notes: Standardized or dictionary-derived text description of CMTRT or CMMODIFY. Equivalent to the generic drug name in WHO Drug. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to map the terms utilizing the external codelist element in the Define-XML document. If an intervention term does not have a decode value in the dictionary, then CMDECOD will be left blank.

label: Category for Medication
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of medications/treatment. Examples: "PRIOR", "CONCOMITANT", "ANTI-CANCER MEDICATION", "GENERAL CONMED".

label: Subcategory for Medication
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of medications/treatment. Examples: "CHEMOTHERAPY", "HORMONAL THERAPY", "ALTERNATIVE THERAPY".

label: CM Pre-specified
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate whether ("Y"/null) information about the use of a specific medication was solicited on the CRF.

label: CM Occurrence
cdisc_notes: When the use of a specific medication is solicited. CMOCCUR is used to indicate whether ("Y"/"N") use of the medication occurred. Values are null for medications not specifically solicited.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question about the occurrence of a prespecified intervention was not answered. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Medication Not Collected
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with CMSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Indication
cdisc_notes: Denotes why a medication was taken or administered. Examples: "NAUSEA", "HYPERTENSION".

label: Medication Class
cdisc_notes: Drug class. May be obtained from coding. When coding to a single class, populate with class value. If using a dictionary and coding to multiple classes, then follow Section 4.2.8.3, Multiple Values for a Non-result Qualifier Variable, or omit CMCLAS.

label: Medication Class Code
cdisc_notes: Class code corresponding to CMCLAS. Drug class. May be obtained from coding. When coding to a single class, populate with class code. If using a dictionary and coding to multiple classes, then follow Section 4.2.8.3, Multiple Values for a Non-result Qualifier Variable, or omit CMCLASCD.

label: Dose per Administration
cdisc_notes: Amount of CMTRT given. Not populated when CMDOSTXT is populated.

label: Dose Description
cdisc_notes: Dosing amounts or a range of dosing information collected in text form. Units may be stored in CMDOSU. Examples: "200-400", "15-20". Not populated when CMDOSE is populated.

label: Dose Units
cdisc_notes: Units for CMDOSE, CMDOSTOT, or CMDOSTXT. Examples: "ng", "mg", "mg/kg".

label: Dose Form
cdisc_notes: Dose form for CMTRT. Examples: "TABLET", "LOTION".

label: Dosing Frequency per Interval
cdisc_notes: Usually expressed as the number of repeated administrations of CMDOSE within a specific time period. Examples: "BID" (twice daily), "Q12H" (every 12 hours).

label: Total Daily Dose
cdisc_notes: Total daily dose of CMTRT using the units in CMDOSU. Used when dosing is collected as total daily dose. Total dose over a period other than day could be recorded in a separate supplemental qualifier variable.

label: Intended Dose Regimen
cdisc_notes: Text description of the (intended) schedule or regimen for the Intervention. Example: "TWO WEEKS ON, TWO WEEKS OFF".

label: Route of Administration
cdisc_notes: Route of administration for the intervention. Examples: "ORAL", "INTRAVENOUS".

label: Location of Administration
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of an intervention, such as an injection site. Example: ARM for an injection.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing laterality of the intervention administration. Examples: "LEFT", "RIGHT".

label: Treatment Vehicle
cdisc_notes: Vehicle for administration of treatment, such as a liquid in which the treatment drug is dissolved. Example: "SALINE".

label: Treatment Vehicle Amount
cdisc_notes: Amount of the prepared product (treatment + vehicle) administered or given. Note: should not be diluent amount alone.

label: Treatment Vehicle Amount Units
cdisc_notes: Units for the prepared product (treatment + vehicle). Examples: "mL", "mg".

label: Reason for Dose Adjustment
cdisc_notes: Describes reason or explanation of why a dose is adjusted. Examples: "ADVERSE EVENT", "INSUFFICIENT RESPONSE", "NON-MEDICAL REASON".

label: Reason the Intervention Was Discontinued
cdisc_notes: When dosing of a treatment is recorded over multiple successive records, this variable is applicable only for the (chronologically) last record for the treatment.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the medication administration started. Null for medications that started before study participation.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the medication administration. Null for medications that started before study participation.

label: Start Date/Time of Medication
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of the medication administration represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: End Date/Time of Medication
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the medication administration represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Start of Medication
cdisc_notes: Study day of start of medication relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Day of End of Medication
cdisc_notes: Study day of end of medication relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Duration
cdisc_notes: Collected duration for a treatment episode. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived from start and end date/times.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken, as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See CVTPTNUM and CVTPTREF.

label: Start Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Describes the start of the medication relative to sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point (represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics). If information such as "PRIOR" was collected, this information may be translated into CMSTRF. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Describes the end of the medication relative to the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point (represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics). If information such as "PRIOR", "ONGOING, or "CONTINUING" was collected, this information may be translated into CMENRF. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: Start Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the medication as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable CMSTTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: Start Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by CMSTRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-15", "VISIT 1".

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the medication as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable CMENTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by CMENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25", "VISIT 2".

label: Related Domain Abbreviation

label: Sequence Number

label: Link ID

label: Link Group ID

label: Identifying Variable

label: Identifying Variable Value
cdisc_notes: Value of identifying variable of the parent record(s). Used only when individual comments are related to domain records. Null for comments collected on separate CRFs.

label: Comment Reference

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Example: "INVESTIGATOR".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST", "RADIOLOGIST 1", "RADIOLOGIST 2".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Date/Time of Comment
cdisc_notes: Date/time of comment on dedicated comment form. Should be null if this is a child record of another domain or if comment date was not collected.

label: Study Day of Comment
cdisc_notes: Study day of the comment, in integer days. The algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in the Demographics (DM) domain.

label: Study Identifier

label: Domain Abbreviation

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
