label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external specimen identifier.

label: Test or Examination Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in CPTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in CPTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). CPTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "MONO", "MNS".

label: Name of Measurement, Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Long name for CPTESTCD. For cell phenotyping, the name (often abbreviated) of the cell population, as it is generally accepted by the scientific community, is populated (rather than a colloquial designation based on a primary marker, e.g., TLym Help rather than CD4). When the test is for a sublineage which can only be identified by specifying additional markers (i.e., has not been given a name) or which is further restricted to a subpopulation based on a particular cell state (e.g., activated, proliferating, apoptotic), the Sublineage Marker String (CPSBMRKS), Cell State (CPCELSTA), and Cell State Marker String (CPCSMRKS) variables are additionally populated and the value in CPTEST is suffixed with "Sub" to denote that it is a subset of the population identified in CPTEST (e.g., Monocytes Sub). \n The value in CPTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters.

label: Category
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values across subjects. Examples: "IMMUNOPHENOTYPING", "CELL FUNCTION", "TARGET ENGAGEMENT".

label: Subcategory
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of CPCAT.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for CPORRES. Examples: "10^6/L", "%", "MESF".

label: Result Scale
cdisc_notes: Classifies the scale of the original result value with respect to whether the result is quantitative, ordinal, nominal, or narrative.

label: Result Type
cdisc_notes: Classifies the kind of result (i.e., property type) originally reported for the test. Examples: "NUMBER CONCENTRATION", "NUMBER FRACTION", "RATIO".

label: Collected Summary Result Type
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate the type of collected summary result. This includes source summary results collected on a CRF or provided by an external vendor (e.g., central lab). If the summary result is derived using individual source data records, this summary result should be represented in ADaM. If a sponsor has both a collected summary result and a derived summary result, the collected summary result should be represented in SDTM and the derived summary result should be represented in ADaM.

label: Reference Range Lower Limit in Orig Unit
cdisc_notes: Lower end of reference range for continuous measurement in original units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Reference Range Upper Limit in Orig Unit
cdisc_notes: Upper end of reference range for continuous measurement in original units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Result or Finding in Standard Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from CPORRES in a standard format or in standard units. CPSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in CPSTRESN.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from CPSTRESC. CPSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized unit used for CPSTRESC or CPSTRESN.

label: Reference Range Lower Limit-Std Units
cdisc_notes: Lower end of reference range for continuous measurements for CPSTRESC/CPSTRESN in standardized units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Reference Range Upper Limit-Std Units
cdisc_notes: Upper end of reference range for continuous measurements in standardized units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Reference Range Indicator
cdisc_notes: Indicates where the value falls with respect to reference range defined by CPORNRLO and CPORNRHI, CPSTNRLO and CPSTNRHI, or by CPSTNRC. Examples: "NORMAL", "ABNORMAL", "HIGH", "LOW". Sponsors should specify in the study metadata (Comments column in the Define-XML document) whether CPNRIND refers to the original or standard reference ranges and results. CPNRIND should not be used to indicate clinical significance.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that the test was not performed or that it was attempted but did not generate a result. Should be null if a result exists in CPORRES.

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Describes why a test was not performed, e.g., "BROKEN EQUIPMENT", "SUBJECT REFUSED", "SPECIMEN LOST". Used in conjunction with CPSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: The name or identifier of the laboratory that performed the test.

label: LOINC Code
cdisc_notes: Code for the test from the LOINC code system. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to map the terms utilizing the Define-XML external codelist attributes.

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: The physical state or quality of a specimen for an assessment. Example: "CLOTTED".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Example: "FLOW CYTOMETRY".

label: Analysis Method
cdisc_notes: Analysis method applied to obtain a summarized result. Analysis method describes the method of secondary processing applied to a complex observation result.

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally-derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record. The value should be "Y" or null. Records that represent the average of other records, or do not come from the CRF, or are not as originally received or collected are examples of records that might be derived for the submission datasets. If CPDRVFL = "Y", then CPORRES may be null, with CPSTRESC and (if numeric) CPSTRESN having the derived value.

label: Clinically Significant, Collected
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate whether a collected observation is clinically significant based on judgement.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Date/time of specimen collection represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Collection
cdisc_notes: Study day of specimen collection, measured in integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC value in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a specimen is to be taken, as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point (i.e., to the value in CPTPTREF). Example: "1 hour post".

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numerical version of CPTPT to aid in sorting. When CPTPT is represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point (i.e., to the value in CPTPTREF), the values in CPTPTNUM should be assigned in ascending order relative to the value in CPTPTREF. For example, records for time points where CPTPT = "5 minutes post", 1 hour post", and "4 hours post" could be represented in CPTPTNUM as "1", "2", and "3", which maintains the order between CPTPT and CPTPTNUM with respect to the fixed time point reference in CPTPTREF.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time relative to the planned fixed reference value in CPTPTREF, represented in ISO 8601 duration format. Examples: "-PT15M" to represent 15 minutes prior to the reference time point indicated by CPTPTREF, "T8H" to represent 8 hours after the reference time point represented by CPTPTREF.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Descriptive name of the fixed reference point referred to by CPTPT, CPTPTNUM, and CPELTM. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by CPTPTREF.

label: Antibody Clone Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifies the antibody clone (e.g., supplier-provided catalog name) used to confer specificity for the binding agent specified in CPBDAGNT.

label: Binding Agent
cdisc_notes: The textual description of the agent that is binding to the entity in the CPTEST variable. The CPBDAGNT variable is used to indicate that there is a binding relationship between the entities in the CPTEST and CPBDAGNT variables, regardless of direction. \n The binding agent may be, but is not limited to, a test article; a portion of a test article; a substance related to a test article; an endogenous molecule; an allergen; an infectious agent; or a reagent (e.g., primary antibody) that confers the binding specificity for the measurement defined in CPTEST when it is needed to uniquely identify the test.

label: Cell State
cdisc_notes: A textual description of a subset of the cell population identified in CPTEST based on a particular functional and/or biological state (e.g., "ACTIVATED", "PROLIFERATING", "SENESCENT"). When populated, the values in CPCELSTA and CPSMRKS, in combination with the values in CPTEST and CPSBMRKS, fully describe the cell population being measured.

label: Test Condition Agent
cdisc_notes: The textual description of the agent, if applicable, used to impose the condition identified in CPTSTCND. For example, records might be produced for the same assay run under stimulating (CPTSTCND value = "STIMULATED") conditions produced by different stimulating agents (e.g., phorbol myristate acetate, concanavalin A, PHA-P, TNF-alpha, Ionomycin, candida antigen).

label: Cell State Marker String
cdisc_notes: Identifies the marker(s) or indicator(s) used to define the cell state (i.e., the value in CPCELSTA). \n For example, when Ki67 expression is used to determine that a cell population is in a proliferating state (i.e., CPCELSTA value="PROLIFERATING"), the value "Ki67+" in CPCSMRKS indicates that positive expression of Ki67 was used to define the population as proliferating. Similarly, a value of "Ki67-" in CPCSMRKS would indicate that lack of expression of Ki67 defined the "NON-PROLIFERATING" cell state in CPCELSTA. The CPCSMRKS value is useful for quickly determining which marker(s) were used to classify (i.e., operationally define) a cell population based on a functional/biological state.

label: Gate Definition
cdisc_notes: The text string identifying the set of parameters and the order in which they are applied to define the gating strategy. In practice, a series of 2-dimensional sub-gates based on different cell characteristics (i.e., markers/indicators/physical properties) are most often combined until the cell population of interest is sufficiently resolved (i.e., electronically isolated) from other cell populations contained within the specimen. \n For complex analyses, differences in gating strategies can produce subtle differences in results obtained for a test. To ensure nuances important for accurately interpreting the data are accounted for and which arise from the use of different gating strategies, it is often necessary to qualify the test in terms of the gating strategy. For some purposes, however, and at the discretion of the sponsor, only the ultimate or penultimate gate is identified. When specifying the gating strategy in CPGATDEF, each sub-gate should be listed in the order it was applied and separated from the next sub-gate using the pipe/vertical line ("|") character.

label: Gate
cdisc_notes: The sponsor-defined name assigned to a gate. Gates are electronic (i.e., a device setting or software-defined) boundaries set by a user to virtually parse a specimen into discrete populations based on a set of defined characteristics (e.g., presence, absence, or intensity of expression of various markers; physical size; internal complexity or granularity). Gates are used to constrain data collection or analysis to a specific cell population or region of interest within the specimen.

label: Marker String
cdisc_notes: The text string identifying the full set of markers/indicators used by the laboratory to operationally define the complete test based on the combination of CPTEST, CPSBMRKS, and CPCELSTA. Because laboratories often use different markers/indicators to identify a cell population, the relationship between a named cell population in CPTEST (as combined with CPSBMRKS and CPCELSTA values) and the set of markers used to identify that population is many-to-one. To ensure nuances important for accurately interpreting the data are accounted for and which arise from the use of different sets of markers, it is necessary to operationally define the test in terms of the complete set of markers/indicators used to perform that test.

label: Sublineage Marker String
cdisc_notes: Used to further subset the cell population identified in CPTEST based on the use of additional marker(s) that define a sublineage. The value in CPSBMRKS is used in combination with values in CPTEST and CPCELSTA to fully describe the cell population being measured. As such, it is an essential component of the full test name. \n For example, three unnamed sublineages of monocytes have been identified as: CCR2+CD16-, CCR2-CD16+, and CCR2+CD16+. Whereas the entire monocyte cell population can be defined as CD14+ cells, the additional CCR2 and CD16 markers are used to differentiate one sublineage from another. As none of these sublineages have been given names, they are only known by the CCR2 and CD16 marker combinations. By associating the CPTEST value of "Monocytes Sub" with, for example, a value of "CCR2+CD16-" in CPSBMRKS, the full test is defined to be the CCR2+CD16- monocyte subpopulation.

label: Sponsor Test Description
cdisc_notes: Sponsor's description of a test. The variable is intended to contain highly structured test description metadata used by a sponsor to unambiguously define (label) a test. Such values generally reside in a sponsor/laboratory test metadata repository. CPSPTSTD is not intended for unstructured (spontaneous) free text. \n An example of appropriate usage is when it is necessary to include identifying information for a target cell population on which a test is conducted when the target population is not part of the test name, e.g., tests for quantitative expression of a particular marker on a specific cell population.

label: Test Condition
cdisc_notes: Identifies any planned condition imposed by the assay system on the specimen at the time the test is performed. --TSTCND is generally used to distinguish between two or more records where the same assay is performed under varying (as opposed to fixed) conditions, usually for the purpose of making a comparison. For example, when the same assay (identified in --TEST) is performed under stimulated and non-stimulated conditions, the --TSTCND variable is used distinguish between the records.

label: Test Panel
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined textual description used to group tests run together as part of a test panel. Can be used with --GRPID to ensure that relationships between associated tests are accurately identified.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject. May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. Example: a preprinted line identifier on a CRF.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship.

label: Link Group
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship.

label: Short Name of Cardiovascular Test
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in CVTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in CVTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" would not be valid). CVTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores.

label: Name of Cardiovascular Test
cdisc_notes: Long name For CVTESTCD. The value in CVTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters.

label: Category for Cardiovascular Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values.

label: Subcategory for Cardiovascular Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of CVCAT values.

label: Position of Subject During Observation
cdisc_notes: Position of the subject during a measurement or examination. Examples: "SUPINE", "STANDING", "SITTING".

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. Unit for CVORRES.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived, from CVORRES in a standard format or in standard units. CVSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in CVSTRESN. For example, if various tests have results "NONE", "NEG", and "NEGATIVE" in CVORRES and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in CVSTRESC as "NEGATIVE".

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from CVSTRESC. CVSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for CVSTRESC and CVSTRESN.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question was not asked or a test was not done, or a test was attempted but did not generate a result. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Describes why a measurement or test was not performed (e.g., "BROKEN EQUIPMENT", "SUBJECT REFUSED"). Used in conjunction with CVSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Examples: "HEART", "LEFT VENTRICLE".

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL", "UNILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method used to create the result.

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally-derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that CVBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record (i.e., a record that represents the average of other records, such as a computed baseline). Should be "Y" or null.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", " INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "RADIOLOGIST".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in CVEVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1" or "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the assessment was made.

label: Date/Time of Test
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation.

label: Study Day of Visit/Collection/Exam
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken, as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See CVTPTNUM and CVTPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (CVTPTREF). Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL". This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval, represented as ISO duration.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by CVELTM, CVTPTNUM, and CVTPT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by CVTPTREF.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study within the submission.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as a code from the product packaging (e.g., bottle label, package label, kit label).

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Examples: Line number on the Product Accountability CRF page, a code from the product packaging (e.g., bottle label, package label, kit label).

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship.

label: Short Name of Accountability Assessment
cdisc_notes: Short character value for DATEST used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical format to a horizontal format. The short value can be up to 8 characters and cannot begin with a number or contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "DISPAMT", "RETAMT".

label: Name of Accountability Assessment
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name corresponding to the topic variable of the test or examination used to obtain the product accountability assessment. The value in DATEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Dispensed Amount", "Returned Amount".

label: Category
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Examples: "STUDY MEDICATION", "RESCUE MEDICATION".

label: Subcategory
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization level for a group of related records.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the product accountability assessment as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Unit for DAORRES.

label: Result or Finding in Standard Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all product accountability assessments copied or derived from DAORRES, in a standard format or in standard units. DASTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in DASTRESN.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from DASTRESC. DASTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for DASTRESC and DASTRESN.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a product accountability assessment was not done. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with DASTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than one assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted evaluation. Expected values can include "Y", "N", or null. This is not intended to be an analysis flag to indicate acceptability for a given analysis.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit, based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm (see Section 7.2.1, Trial Arms).

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Date and time of the product accountability assessment represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Visit/Collection/Exam
cdisc_notes: Study day of product accountability assessment, measured in integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. Perhaps preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Examples: Line number on the Drug Accountability page, drug label code.

label: Death Detail Assessment Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in DDTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in DDTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). DDTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "PRCDTH", "SECDTH".

label: Death Detail Assessment Name
cdisc_notes: Long name for DDTESTCD. The value in DDTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Primary Cause of Death", "Secondary Cause of Death".

label: Category
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Examples: "STUDY MEDICATION", "RESCUE MEDICATION".

label: Result or Finding as Collected
cdisc_notes: Result of the test defined in DDTEST, as originally received or collected.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result or finding copied or derived from DDORRES in a standard format.

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding. Examples: "TREATMENT RELATED", "NONTREATMENT RELATED", "UNDETERMINED", "ACCIDENTAL".

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date or start date and time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected (see Section Trial Arms).

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Date/time of collection of the diagnosis or other death assessment data in ISO 8601 format. This is not necessarily the date of death.

label: Study Day of Collection
cdisc_notes: Study day of the collection, in integer days. The algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in the Demographics (DM) domain.

label: MedDRA High Level Group Term Code
MedDRA High Level Group Term Name
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sponsor Device Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for a device

label: Device Events Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence Number given to ensure uniqueness of device records within subject records within a domain. May be any valid number. It should be unique within every subject/device combination. If there is no USUBJID associated with the event, DESEQ should be unique within each SPDEVID.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID, or UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. Perhaps pre-printed on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Note that it does not have to be numeric.

label: Reported Term for Device Event
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the observed event. Examples: "Screw Breakage", "Fissure Formation", "Battery Issue".

label: Modified Device Event Name
cdisc_notes: The modified text for DETERM. If DETERM is modified, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Device Events Dictionary-Derived Term
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived form of the event described in DETERM. Dictionary-derived text description of DETERM or DEMODIFY. The name and version of the dictionary used to map terms must be provided in a Define.XML ExternalCodeList element.

label: Category of Device Event
cdisc_notes: Used to define a categorization level for events. For example, "MALFUNCTION" vs. "CALIBRATION".

label: Subcategory of Device Event
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further category level for events. For example, "EXTERNAL" vs. "INTERNAL".

label: Pre-Specified Device Event
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate whether (Y/null) information about a specific event was solicited on the CRF. For example, DETERM could contain a list of malfunctions that are being specifically evaluated. DEPRESP would identify those (Y), whereas any spontaneous events would have DEPREST null.

label: Device Event Occurrence
cdisc_notes: When information about specific events is solicited, DEOCCUR is used to indicate whether or not (Y/N) a particular pre-specified event occurred. Values are null for events not specifically solicited.

label: Device Event Collection Status
cdisc_notes: The status indicates that the pre-specified question was not answered. For example, if equipment operation requires checking, such as checking an event log to detect events. Capturing that the checks were not completed may be relevant to interpreting the study data.

label: Reason Device Event Not Collected
cdisc_notes: Reason DESTAT was "NOT DONE". This variable should only be used if there are pre-specified events.

label: Device Event Severity
cdisc_notes: Describes the severity of the event, (e.g., the severity of a malfunction).

label: Action Taken with Device
cdisc_notes: Describes Action Taken with respect to the device. Action Taken may include removal, calibration, reprogramming, and so on. Action is usually in response to some event, for example, a subject's adverse event.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date or start date and time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected (see Section Trial Arms).

label: Date of Device Event Data Collection
cdisc_notes: Date the device event information was collected. This may be reported if the event (e.g., malfunction) is discovered on a different date from the event.

label: Start Date/Time of Device Event
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of the device event. If the event happened at a single point in time, DESTDTC is used.

label: End Date/Time of Device Event
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the device event. If an event lasted over a period of time, DEENDTC can be used to capture the end date/time.

label: Study Day of Start of Device Event
cdisc_notes: Study day of Device Event observation, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics.

label: Study Day of Device Event Start
cdisc_notes: Study day of start of Device Event, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics.

label: Study Day of Device Event End
cdisc_notes: Study day of end of Device Event, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFENDTC variable in Demographics.

label: AE Caused by Device Event Flag
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Sponsor Device Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for a device

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness within a parameter within a device (SPDEVID) within dataset. If there is only one value for DIPARMCD for each value of SPDEVID, then DISEQ will be 1 for all records. DISEQ must be a valid number.

label: Device Identifier Element Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the identifier characteristic of the device (e.g., "SERIAL", "MODEL"). A record with DIPARMCD = "DEVTYPE" should be included.

label: Device Identifier Element Name
cdisc_notes: Name of the identifier characteristic of the device. Examples: Serial Number, Model. A record with DIPARM = "DEVTYPE" should be included.

label: Device Identifier Element Value
cdisc_notes: Value for the parameter. When DIPARMCD=DEVTYPE it should use controlled terminology defined by FDA in their Preferred Term codelist. FDA has stated a preference for the Global Medical Device Nomenclature (GMDN), but as of the date of this publication, the GMDN is not freely available to the public. In its Unique Device Identification rule, FDA indicated that GMDN will not be required unless it is available to the public at no cost. There is a lookup tool on the FDA website to map GMDN codes to FDA PT codes.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product. This must be a unique value, and could be a compound identifier formed by concatenating STUDYID-SITEID-SUBJID.

label: Subject Identifier for the Study
cdisc_notes: Subject identifier, which must be unique within the study. Often the ID of the subject as recorded on a CRF.

label: Subject Reference Start Date/Time
cdisc_notes: Reference start date/time for the subject in ISO 8601 character format. Usually equivalent to date/time when subject was first exposed to study treatment. See assumption 9 for additional detail on when RFSTDTC may be null.

label: Subject Reference End Date/Time
cdisc_notes: Reference end date/time for the subject in ISO 8601 character format. Usually equivalent to the date/time when subject was determined to have ended the trial, and often equivalent to date/time of last exposure to study treatment. Required for all randomized subjects; null for screen failures or unassigned subjects.

label: Date/Time of First Study Treatment
cdisc_notes: First date/time of exposure to any protocol-specified treatment or therapy, equal to the earliest value of EXSTDTC.

label: Date/Time of Last Study Treatment
cdisc_notes: Last date/time of exposure to any protocol-specified treatment or therapy, equal to the latest value of EXENDTC (or the latest value of EXSTDTC if EXENDTC was not collected or is missing).

label: Date/Time of First Challenge Agent Admin
cdisc_notes: Used only when protocol specifies a challenge agent to induce a condition that the investigational treatment is intended to cure, mitigate, treat, or prevent. Equal to the earliest value of AGSTDTC for the challenge agent.

label: Date/Time of Last Challenge Agent Admin
cdisc_notes: Used only when protocol specifies a challenge agent to induce a condition that the investigational treatment is intended to cure, mitigate, treat, or prevent. Equal to the latest value of AGENDTC for the challenge agent (or the latest value of AGSTDTC if AGENDTC was not collected or is missing).

label: Date/Time of Informed Consent
cdisc_notes: Date/time of informed consent in ISO 8601 character format. This will be the same as the date of informed consent in the Disposition domain, if that protocol milestone is documented. Would be null only in studies not collecting the date of informed consent.

label: Date/Time of End of Participation
cdisc_notes: Date/time when subject ended participation or follow-up in a trial, as defined in the protocol, in ISO 8601 character format. Should correspond to the last known date of contact. Examples include completion date, withdrawal date, last follow-up, date recorded for lost to follow up, and death date.

label: Date/Time of Death
cdisc_notes: Date/time of death for any subject who died, in ISO 8601 format. Should represent the date/time that is captured in the clinical-trial database.

label: Subject Death Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicates the subject died. Should be "Y" or null. Should be populated even when the death date is unknown.

label: Study Site Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a site within a study.

label: Investigator Identifier
cdisc_notes: An identifier to describe the Investigator for the study. May be used in addition to SITEID. Not needed if SITEID is equivalent to INVID.

label: Investigator Name
cdisc_notes: Name of the investigator for a site.

label: Date/Time of Birth
cdisc_notes: Date/time of birth of the subject.

label: Age
cdisc_notes: Age expressed in AGEU. May be derived from RFSTDTC and BRTHDTC, but BRTHDTC may not be available in all cases (due to subject privacy concerns).

label: Age Units
cdisc_notes: Units associated with AGE.

label: Sex
cdisc_notes: Sex of the subject.

label: Race
cdisc_notes: Race of the subject. Sponsors should refer to the FDA guidance2 regarding the collection of race. See assumption below regarding RACE.

label: Ethnicity
cdisc_notes: The ethnicity of the subject. Sponsors should refer to the FDA guidance1 regarding the collection of ethnicity.

label: Planned Arm Code
cdisc_notes: ARMCD is limited to 20 characters. It is not subject to the character restrictions that apply to TESTCD. The maximum length of ARMCD is longer than for other "short" variables to accommodate the kind of values that are likely to be needed for crossover trials. For example, if ARMCD values for a 7-period crossover were constructed using 2-character abbreviations for each treatment and separating hyphens, the length of ARMCD values would be 20. If the subject was not assigned to a trial arm, ARMCD is null and ARMNRS is populated. \n With the exception of studies which use multistage arm assignments, must be a value of ARMCD in the Trial Arms dataset.

label: Description of Planned Arm
cdisc_notes: Name of the arm to which the subject was assigned. If the subject was not assigned to an arm, ARM is null and ARMNRS is populated. \n With the exception of studies which use multistage arm assignments, must be a value of ARM in the Trial Arms dataset.

label: Actual Arm Code
cdisc_notes: Code of actual arm. ACTARMCD is limited to 20 characters. It is not subject to the character restrictions that apply to TESTCD. The maximum length of ACTARMCD is longer than for other short variables to accommodate the kind of values that are likely to be needed for crossover trials. \n With the exception of studies which use multistage arm assignments, must be a value of ARMCD in the Trial Arms dataset. \n If the subject was not assigned to an arm or followed a course not described by any planned arm, ACTARMCD is null and ARMNRS is populated.

label: Description of Actual Arm
cdisc_notes: Description of actual arm. \n With the exception of studies which use multistage arm assignments, must be a value of ARM in the Trial Arms dataset. \n If the subject was not assigned to an arm or followed a course not described by any planned arm, ACTARM is null and ARMNRS is populated.

label: Reason Arm and/or Actual Arm is Null
cdisc_notes: A coded reason that arm variables (ARM and ARMCD) and/or actual arm variables (ACTARM and ACTARMCD) are null. Examples: "SCREEN FAILURE", "NOT ASSIGNED", "ASSIGNED, NOT TREATED", "UNPLANNED TREATMENT". It is assumed that if the arm and actual arm variables are null, the same reason applies to both arm and actual arm.

label: Description of Unplanned Actual Arm
cdisc_notes: A description of actual treatment for a subject who did not receive treatment described in a planned trial arm.

label: Country
cdisc_notes: Country of the investigational site in which the subject participated in the trial. \n \n Generally represented using ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3. Note that regulatory agency specific requirements (e.g., US FDA) may require other terminologies; in such cases, follow regulatory requirements.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Date/time of demographic data collection.

label: Study Day of Collection
cdisc_notes: Study day of collection measured as integer days.

label: Date of Last Dose from Previous Study
Enrolled in Prior Study within Project
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sponsor Device Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for a device

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence Number given to ensure uniqueness of device records within subject records within a domain. May be any valid number. It should be unique within every subject/device combination.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a device.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external identifier. This could be a scan code or equivalent.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number.

label: Device Property Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in DOTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in DOTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST"). DOTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "SHLFLIFE", "INDC", "COMPOS".

label: Device Property Test Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in DOTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Shelf Life", "Indication for use", "Composition" (of device).

label: Category for Device In-Use
cdisc_notes: Defines a category of related records. For example, it can be used to define the type of property being defined, such as "DIMENSIONS" versus "MATERIAL".

label: Subcategory for Device In-Use
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of a measurement or examination. For example, if DOCAT = "DIMENSION", DOSCAT might be "LENGTH" or "WIDTH" or "THICKNESS".

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the Device Property as originally observed or collected. DOORRES should contain the result or value of the property defined in DOTEST. For example, if DOTEST is LIFE (shelf life), then DOORRES might be 6 (months).

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for DOORRES. Examples: "MONTHS", "cm".

label: Result or Finding in Standard Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for the response assessment, copied, or derived from DOORRES in a standard format or standard units. RSSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format. For Clinical Classifications, this may be a score.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from --STRESC. --STRESN should store all numeric test results or findings. For Clinical Classifications, this may be a score.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for DOSTRESC and DOSTRESN.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the assessment was made.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by --ELTM, --TPTNUM, --TPT, --STINT, and --ENINT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sponsor Device Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for a device

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external identifier.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number on a Disposition page.

label: Reported Term for the Disposition Event
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the event or protocol milestone. Some terms in DSTERM will match DSDECOD, but others, such as "Subject moved", will map to controlled terminology in DSDECOD, such as "LOST TO FOLLOW-UP".

label: Standardized Disposition Term
cdisc_notes: Controlled terminology for the name of disposition event or protocol milestone. Examples of protocol milestones: "INFORMED CONSENT OBTAINED", "RANDOMIZED". There are separate codelists used for DSDECOD where the choice depends on the value of DSCAT. Codelist "NCOMPLT" is used for disposition events, codelist "PROTMLST" is used for protocol milestones, and codelist "OTHEVENT" is used for other events.

label: Category for Disposition Event
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records.

label: Subcategory for Disposition Event
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of DSCAT (e.g., "STUDY PARTICIPATION", "STUDY TREATMENT" when DSCAT = "DISPOSITION EVENT"). The variable may be subject to controlled terminology for other categories of disposition event records.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the event.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of the disposition observation represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Start Date/Time of Disposition Event
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of the disposition event in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Collection
cdisc_notes: Study day of collection of event relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Day of Start of Disposition Event
cdisc_notes: Study day of start of event relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

Strat Factor 1 Value Used for Rand (N)

Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Sponsor Device Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for a device

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence Number given to ensure uniqueness of device records within subject records (if applicable) within a domain. May be any valid number. Should be unique within each SPDEVID.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. Perhaps pre-printed on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Note that it does not have to be numeric.

label: Reported Term for the Tracking Event
cdisc_notes: Verbatim or preprinted term for the activity that occurs. Example: "Shipped", "Returned", "Installed", "Implanted", "Explanted".

label: Modified Reported Term
cdisc_notes: Modified term entered to allow mapping of verbatim term to dictionary term. If DTTERM is modified to facilitate coding, then DTMODIFY will contain the modified text.

label: Standardized Tracking Term
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived text description of DTTERM or DTMODIFY. If an external controlled terminology is used, the name and version of the dictionary used to map terms must be provided in a Define-XML ExternalCodeList element.

label: Category for Device Tracking Event
cdisc_notes: Defines a categorization level for a group of related records. Examples: categorize by tracking event type (e.g., "DOMESTIC" vs. "INTERNATIONAL").

label: Subcategory for Device Tracking Event
cdisc_notes: Defines a further categorization level for a group of related conditions or events.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question was not done or was not answered. Should be null if a result exists in QSORRES.

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Describes why a question was not answered. Used in conjunction with QSSTAT when value is "NOT DONE". Example: "SUBJECT REFUSED".

label: Party Responsible for the Device
cdisc_notes: Person or organization accountable for the device at the conclusion of the action specified in DTTERM. Describes the person or organization that is accountable for the device defined in DTTERM. For example, if DTTERM=SHIPPED, DTPARTY would contain the transfer recipient (e.g., SITE). If DTTERM=IMPLANTED, DTPARTY would contain SUBJECT.

label: Responsible Party Identifier
cdisc_notes: An identifier for the responsible group/role (e.g. site, subject). The value of the responsible party identified in DTPARTY. For example, if DTPARTY is SUBJECT, DTPRTYID would contain the subject number.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date or start date and time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected (see Section Trial Arms).

label: Date/Time of Tracking Event Collection
cdisc_notes: Date/Time the tracking event information was collected. Will generally be the same as the date the tracking event started, but it can differ.

label: Start Date/Time of Tracking Event
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of tracking event. A tracking event (e.g., SHIPPED, RECEIVED) is usually a point in time, which is why only the start date/time is included. If an event occurs over a longer period, the stop date/time may be included from the SDTM model.

label: Study Day of Start of Observation
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of start of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product. Expected in this domain as devices may have settings or uses that either may not involve subjects (e.g., diagnostic tools) or devices that are removed from the study prior to contact with a subject (e.g., device has malfunction).

label: Sponsor Device Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for the device. It must be unique for each tracked unit of the device under study, and can be at whatever level of granularity the device should be identified (e.g., model or serial number, or combination of identifiers).

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence Number given to ensure uniqueness of device records within subject records within a domain. May be any valid number. It should be unique within every subject/device combination.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier for a group or block of related records. Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject or a group of subject related records (e.g., group records specifying all the settings for a specific imaging scan, such as field strength, repetition time and echo time).

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external identifier. This could be a scan code or equivalent.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. Perhaps pre-printed on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database.

label: Device In-Use Test Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in DUTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in DUTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST"). DUTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "COILSTR", "CNTMEDIA".

label: Device In-Use Test Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in DUTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Coil Strength", "Contrast Media".

label: Category for Device In-Use
cdisc_notes: Defines a category of related records. It can be used to define the type of device for which settings are recorded if DI is not used (e.g., if the device is not under study); may also be used to record the type of setting (e.g., "HARDWARE" vs. "SOFTWARE").

label: Subcategory for Device In-Use
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of a measurement or examination. For example, if DUCAT = "SOFTWARE", DUSCAT might be "NOMINAL" or "POST-ADJUSTMENT".

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement as originally received or collected. DUORRES should contain the setting or other device condition in effect at the time the device was used.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for DUORRES. Examples: Tesla, mm.

label: Result or Finding in Standard Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from DUORRES in a standard format or standard units. DUSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in DUSTRESN. For example, if a test has results "NONE," "NEG," and "NEGATIVE" in DUORRES, and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in DUSTRESC as "NEGATIVE".

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format. Copied in numeric format from DUSTRESC. DUSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized unit used for DUSTRESC and DUSTRESN. The unit for standardized results may or may not be the same as for the original results.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: A clinical encounter number. A Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. This value is usually derived.

label: Date/Time Device Used With Test/ Setting
cdisc_notes: Date/time that the device was used with this setting. This is not the date/time that the setting was set on the device, but rather that date/time that a measurement or test was done using that setting.

label: Study Day of Observation
cdisc_notes: Study day of Device-In-Use measurement, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external identifier.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number on a CRF page.

label: Protocol Deviation Term
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the protocol deviation criterion. Example: "IVRS PROCESS DEVIATION - NO DOSE CALL PERFORMED". DVTERM values will map to the controlled terminology in DVDECOD (e.g., "TREATMENT DEVIATION").

label: Protocol Deviation Coded Term
cdisc_notes: Controlled terminology for the name of the protocol deviation. Examples: "SUBJECT NOT WITHDRAWN AS PER PROTOCOL", "SELECTION CRITERIA NOT MET", "EXCLUDED CONCOMITANT MEDICATION", "TREATMENT DEVIATION".

label: Category for Protocol Deviation
cdisc_notes: Category of the protocol deviation criterion.

label: Subcategory for Protocol Deviation
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of the protocol deviation.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the deviation. Examples: "TREATMENT", "SCREENING", "FOLLOW-UP".

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation.

label: Start Date/Time of Deviation
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of deviation represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: End Date/Time of Deviation
cdisc_notes: End date/time of deviation represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Collection
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of Start of Deviation Event
cdisc_notes: Study day of start of event relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Day of End of Deviation Event
cdisc_notes: Study day of end of event relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sponsor Device Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for the device. It must be unique for each tracked unit of the device under study, and can be at whatever level of granularity the device should be identified (e.g., model or serial number, or combination of identifiers).

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence Number given to ensure uniqueness of device records within subject records within a domain. May be any valid number. It should be unique within every subject/device combination.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier that ties together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject. For example, if a device is inserted that delivers radiation, DXGRPID could be used to tie the records for the device and the radiation together for each course of therapy.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. Examples: a number pre-printed on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or record identifier defined in the sponsor's operational database.

label: Name of Device Exposure or Output
cdisc_notes: Name of the device or the exposure outputs that are delivered or administered via the device. This should match the definitions as described in the trial summary domain and/or the protocol. Example: "coronary stent", "extracorporeal shock wave treatment", "hyaluronic acid".

label: Category for Device Exposure
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of device exposures. For example, for a subject who had radiation delivered through an implanted catheter, DXCAT could be used to group the radiation records versus the catheter records.

label: Subcategory for Device Exposure
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of device exposures. If DXCAT captures the radiation versus catheter records (see DXCAT), then DXSCAT might capture the type of catheter if more than one was used.

label: Exposure per Administration
cdisc_notes: Amount of DXTRT administered/delivered per administration. Dose if captured as a numeric value. Dose should only appear once in DXDOSE, DXDOSTXT, or DXDOSTOT.

label: Device Exposure Description
cdisc_notes: Exposure amount or a range of exposure information collected in text form. Units may be stored in DXDOSU. Example: 200-400, 15-20. Dose should only appear once in DXDOSE, DXDOSTXT, or DXDOSTOT.

label: Device Exposure Units
cdisc_notes: Units for DXDOSE, DXDOSTXT, and DXDOSTOT. Examples: "pulses", "ml".

label: Device Exposure Frequency per Interval
cdisc_notes: Exposure frequency per interval. Usually expressed as the number of repeated administrations of DXDOSE within a specific time period. Examples: "CONTINUOUS", "PRN", "Q2M" (every 2 months).

label: Total Daily Device Exposure
cdisc_notes: Total daily exposure of DXTRT using the units in DXDOSU. Total exposure over a period other than day could be recorded in a separate Supplemental Qualifier variable.

label: Intended Device Exposure Regimen
cdisc_notes: Text description of the (intended) schedule or regimen for the Intervention. Examples: "TWO WEEKS ON, TWO WEEKS OFF".

label: Route of Administration
cdisc_notes: Route of administration for DXTRT. Examples: "EXTRACORPOREAL", "INTRA-ARTICULAR, HEMODIALYSIS".

label: Location of Device Exposure
cdisc_notes: Anatomic location of exposure. Examples: "Knee", "Eye".

label: Laterality of Device Exposure
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Reason for Exposure Adjustment
cdisc_notes: Describes reason why the exposure was adjusted from protocol-specified or expected exposure levels. Example: "PAIN".

label: Start Date/Time of Device Exposure
cdisc_notes: Start date and time of exposure. Defined by the sponsor.

label: End Date/Time of Device Exposure
cdisc_notes: End date and time of exposure. Defined by the sponsor.

label: Study Day of Start of Device Exposure
cdisc_notes: Study day of start of exposure relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Day of End of Device Exposure
cdisc_notes: Study day of end of exposure relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Duration of Device Exposure
cdisc_notes: Collected duration for a treatment episode. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived from start and end date/times.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sponsor Device Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for a device.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external identifier (e.g., kit number, bottle label, vial identifier).

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number on a CRF page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related, grouped records across domains.

label: Name of Treatment
cdisc_notes: Name of the intervention treatment known to the subject and/or administrator.

label: Modified Treatment Name
cdisc_notes: If the value for --TRT is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Mood
cdisc_notes: Mode or condition of the record specifying whether the intervention (activity) is intended to happen or has happened. Values align with BRIDG pillars (e.g., scheduled context, performed context) and HL7 activity moods (e.g., intent, event). Examples: "SCHEDULED", "PERFORMED".

label: Category of Treatment
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related ECTRT values.

label: Subcategory of Treatment
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of ECCAT values.

label: Pre-Specified
cdisc_notes: Used when a specific intervention is prespecified. Values should be "Y" or null.

label: Occurrence
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate whether a treatment occurred when information about the occurrence is solicited. ECOCCUR = "N" when a treatment was not taken, not given, or missed.

label: Reason for Occur Value
cdisc_notes: The reason for the value in --OCCUR. If --OCCUR = "N", this is the reason the exposure did not occur.

label: Indication
cdisc_notes: Denotes the indication for the intervention (e.g., why the therapy was taken or administered).

label: Dose
cdisc_notes: Amount of ECTRT when numeric. Not populated when ECDOSTXT is populated.

label: Dose Description
cdisc_notes: Amount of ECTRT when non-numeric. Dosing amounts or a range of dosing information collected in text form. Example: "200-400". Not populated when ECDOSE is populated.

label: Dose Units
cdisc_notes: Units for ECDOSE, ECDOSTOT, or ECDOSTXT.

label: Dose Form
cdisc_notes: Dose form for ECTRT. Examples: "TABLET", "LOTION".

label: Dosing Frequency per Interval
cdisc_notes: Usually expressed as the number of repeated administrations of ECDOSE within a specific time period. Examples: "Q2H", "QD", "BID".

label: Total Daily Dose
cdisc_notes: Total daily dose of ECTRT using the units in ECDOSU. Used when dosing is collected as total daily dose.

label: Intended Dose Regimen
cdisc_notes: Text description of the intended schedule or regimen for the Intervention. Example: "TWO WEEKS ON", "TWO WEEKS OFF".

label: Route of Administration
cdisc_notes: Route of administration for the intervention. Examples: "ORAL", "INTRAVENOUS".

label: Lot Number
cdisc_notes: Lot number of the ECTRT product.

label: Location of Dose Administration
cdisc_notes: Specifies location of administration. Example: "ARM", "LIP".

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing laterality of the intervention administration. Examples: "LEFT", "RIGHT".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL", "UPPER".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing distribution (i.e., arrangement of, apportioning of). Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT".

label: Fasting Status
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify fasting status. Examples: "Y", "N".

label: Pharmaceutical Strength
cdisc_notes: Amount of an active ingredient expressed quantitatively per dosage unit, per unit of volume, or per unit of weight, according to the pharmaceutical dose form.

label: Pharmaceutical Strength Units
cdisc_notes: Unit for ECPSTRG. Examples: "mg/TABLET", "mg/mL".

label: Treatment Vehicle
cdisc_notes: Vehicle for administration of treatment, such as a liquid in which the treatment drug is dissolved. Example: "SALINE".

label: Reason for Dose Adjustment
cdisc_notes: Describes reason or explanation of why a dose is adjusted.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Trial epoch of the exposure as collected record. Examples: "RUN-IN", "TREATMENT".

label: Start Date/Time of Treatment
cdisc_notes: The date/time when administration of the treatment indicated by ECTRT and ECDOSE began.

label: End Date/Time of Treatment
cdisc_notes: The date/time when administration of the treatment indicated by ECTRT and ECDOSE ended. For administrations considered given at a point in time (e.g., oral tablet, pre-filled syringe injection), where only an administration date/time is collected, ECSTDTC should be copied to ECENDTC as the standard representation.

label: Study Day of Start of Treatment
cdisc_notes: Study day of ECSTDTC relative to the sponsor-defined DM.RFSTDTC.

label: Study Day of End of Treatment
cdisc_notes: Study day of ECENDTC relative to the sponsor-defined DM.RFSTDTC.

label: Duration of Treatment
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of administration. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived from start and end date/times.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when administration should occur. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See ECTPTNUM and ECTPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numerical version of ECTPT to aid in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time relative to the planned fixed reference (ECTPTREF). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Name of the fixed reference point referred to by ECELTM, ECTPTNUM, and ECTPT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by ECTPTREF.

label: Start Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the agent as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable ECSTTPT.

label: Start Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the reference point referred to by ECSTRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-15" or "VISIT 1".

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the agent as being before or after the reference time point defined by variable ECENTPT. Identifies the end of the agent as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable ECENTPT.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the reference point referred to by ECENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25" or "VISIT 2".
