cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Unit for GFORRES.

label: Reference Result in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Reference value for the result or finding as originally received or collected. GFORREF uses the same units as GFORRES, if applicable.

label: Result or Finding in Standard Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from GFORRES, in a standard format or in standard units. GFSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in GFSTRESN.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from GFSTRESC. GFSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for GFSTRESC, GFSTRESN, GFSTREFC, and GFSTREFN.

label: Reference Result in Standard Format
cdisc_notes: Reference value for the result or finding copied or derived from GFORREF in a standard format.

label: Numeric Reference Result in Std Units
cdisc_notes: Reference value for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format or in standard units. GFSTREFN uses the same units as GFSTRESN, if applicable.

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question was not asked or a test was not done, or a test was attempted but did not generate a result. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Test Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with GFSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: External File Path
cdisc_notes: The filename and/or path to external data not stored in the same format and possibly not the same location as the other data for a study.

label: Laboratory/Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: Name or identifier of the vendor that provided the test result. When more than 1 vendor is involved in the generation of the result, additional vendors should be represented as supplemental qualifiers.

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Identifies the type of genetic material used for the measurement.

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: The test method by which the examination is performed by the wet lab in order to yield the result reported in the dataset.

label: Run ID
cdisc_notes: A unique identifier for a particular run of a test performed by the wet lab on a particular batch of samples. This identifier can be used to distinguish between records for the same test performed at different times.

label: Analysis Method
cdisc_notes: The method of secondary processing performed by the dry lab to yield the result reported in the dataset.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record (e.g., a record that represents the average of other records such as a computed baseline). Should be "Y" or null.

label: Lower Limit of Quantitation
cdisc_notes: Indicates the lower limit of quantitation for an assay. Units will be those used for GFSTRESU.

label: Repetition Number
cdisc_notes: The instance number of a test that is repeated within a given timeframe for the same test performed by the wet lab.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Should be an integer.

label: Date/Time of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Date and time of specimen collection.

label: Study Day of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See GFTPTNUM and GFTPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numerical version of GFTPT used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (GFTPTREF). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date time variable, but an interval, represented as ISO duration.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Name of the fixed reference point referred to by GFELTM, GFTPTNUM, and GFTPT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by GFTPTREF.

label: Chromosome Identifier
cdisc_notes: The designation (name or number) of the chromosome or contig on which the variant or other feature appears (e.g., "17"; "X").

label: Copy Identifier
cdisc_notes: An arbitrary identifier used to differentiate between copies of a genetic target of interest present on homologous chromosomes.

label: Genetic Location
cdisc_notes: Specifies the location within a sequence for the observed value in GFORRES.

label: Genome Reference
cdisc_notes: An identifier for the genome reference used to generate the reported result. For example, Genome Reference Consortium Human Build 38 patch release 13 may be represented as "GRCh38.p13".

label: Genetic Sub-Region
cdisc_notes: The portion of the locus in which the variation was found. Examples: "Exon 15", "Kinase domain".

label: Inheritability
cdisc_notes: Identifies whether the variation can be passed to the next generation.

label: Published Variant Identifier
cdisc_notes: A unique identifier for the variation that has been publicly characterized in an external database. Examples: "rs2231142", "COSM41596".

label: Sequence Identifier
cdisc_notes: A unique identifier for the sequence used as the reference to identify the genetic variation in the result. Examples: "NM_001234", "ENSG00000182533", "ENST00000343849.2".

label: Genomic Symbol
cdisc_notes: A published symbol for the portion of the genome serving as a locus for the experiment/test.

label: Genomic Symbol Type
cdisc_notes: A description of the type of genomic entity that is represented by the published symbol in GFSYM.

label: Method of Genomic Test Other Specify
If CEBPA, specify in-frame bZIP CEBPA
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external healthcare encounter identifier.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number on a Healthcare Encounters CRF page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Healthcare Encounter Term
cdisc_notes: Verbatim or preprinted CRF term for the healthcare encounter.

label: Dictionary-Derived Term
cdisc_notes: Dictionary or sponsor-defined derived text description of HOTERM or the modified topic variable (HOMODIFY).

label: Category for Healthcare Encounter
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-related values.

label: Subcategory for Healthcare Encounter
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of HOCAT values.

label: Pre-Specified Healthcare Encounter
cdisc_notes: A value of "Y" indicates that this healthcare encounter event was prespecified on the CRF. Values are null for spontaneously reported events (i.e., those collected as free-text verbatim terms).

label: Healthcare Encounter Occurrence
cdisc_notes: Used when the occurrence of specific healthcare encounters is solicited, to indicate whether an encounter occurred. Values are null for spontaneously reported events.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: The status indicates that the prespecified question was not answered.

label: Reason Healthcare Encounter Not Done
cdisc_notes: Describes the reason data for a prespecified event were not collected. Used in conjunction with HOSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the healthcare encounter. Examples: "SCREENING", "TREATMENT", "FOLLOW-UP".

label: Date/Time of Event Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of the healthcare encounter.

label: Start Date/Time of Healthcare Encounter
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of the healthcare encounter (e.g., date of admission).

label: End Date/Time of Healthcare Encounter
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the healthcare encounter (e.g., date of discharge).

label: Study Day of Event Collection
cdisc_notes: Study day of event collection relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Day of Start of Encounter
cdisc_notes: Study day of the start of the healthcare encounter relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Day of End of Healthcare Encounter
cdisc_notes: Study day of the end of the healthcare encounter relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Duration of Healthcare Encounter
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of the healthcare encounter. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived from the start and end date/times. Example: "P1DT2H" (for 1 day, 2 hours).

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See --TPTNUM and --TPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned Elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (--TPTREF) such as "Previous Dose" or "Previous Meal". This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by --ELTM, --TPTNUM, --TPT, --STINT, and --ENINT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Start Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the observation as being before, during, or after the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics.

label: End Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before, during or after the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: Start Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable --STTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: Start Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by STRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-15", "VISIT 1".

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the event as being before or after the reference time point defined by variable HOENTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the reference point referred to by HOENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25", "VISIT 2".

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Inclusion or exclusion criteria number from CRF.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Inclusion/Exclusion Criterion Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the criterion described in IETEST. The value in IETESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). IETESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "IN01", "EX01".

label: Inclusion/Exclusion Criterion
cdisc_notes: Verbatim description of the inclusion or exclusion criterion that was the exception for the subject within the study. IETEST cannot be longer than 200 characters.

label: Inclusion/Exclusion Category
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records across subjects.

label: Inclusion/Exclusion Subcategory
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of the exception criterion. Can be used to distinguish criteria for a sub-study or for to categorize as a major or minor exceptions. Examples: "MAJOR", "MINOR".

label: I/E Criterion Original Result
cdisc_notes: Original response to inclusion/exclusion criterion question, i.e., whether the inclusion or exclusion criterion was met.

label: I/E Criterion Result in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Response to inclusion/exclusion criterion result, in standard format.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the observation date/time of the inclusion/exclusion finding.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of the inclusion/exclusion criterion represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Collection
cdisc_notes: Study day of collection of the inclusion/exclusion exceptions, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics. This formula should be consistent across the submission.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Non-Host Organism ID
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for a non-host organism which should only be used when the organism is the subject of the TEST. This variable should be populated with an intuitive name based on the identity of the non-host organism as reported by a lab (e.g., "A/California/7/2009 (H1N1)"). It is not to be used as a qualifier of the result in the record on which it appears.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject. May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external specimen identifier. Example: "458975-01".

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier.

label: Immunogenicity Test/Exam Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in ISTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in ISTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). ISTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores.

label: Immunogenicity Test or Examination Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in ISTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Example: "Immunoglobulin E".

label: Test Operational Objective
cdisc_notes: Text description of the high-level purpose of the test at the operational level. If populated, valid values are "SCREEN", "CONFIRM", and "QUANTIFY".

label: Test Detail
cdisc_notes: Further description of ISTESTCD and ISTEST.

label: Category for Immunogenicity Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values across subjects. Example: "SEROLOGY".

label: Subcategory for Immunogenicity Test
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of ISCAT.

label: Results or Findings in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for ISORRES. Examples: "Index Value", "gpELISA", "unit/mL".

label: Reference Range Lower Limit in Orig Unit
cdisc_notes: Lower end of reference range for continuous measurement in original units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Reference Range Upper Limit in Orig Unit
cdisc_notes: Upper end of reference range for continuous measurement in original units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings copied or derived from ISORRES, in a standard format or in standard units. ISSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in ISSTRESN.

label: Numeric Results/Findings in Std. Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from ISSTRESC. ISSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for ISSTRESC and ISSTRESN. Examples: "Index Value", "gpELISA", "unit/mL".

label: Reference Range Lower Limit-Std Units
cdisc_notes: Lower end of reference range for continuous measurements for ISSTRESC/ISSTRESN in standardized units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Reference Range Upper Limit-Std Units
cdisc_notes: Upper end of reference range for continuous measurements in standardized units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Reference Range for Char Rslt-Std Units
cdisc_notes: For normal range values that are character in ordinal scale or if categorical ranges were supplied. Examples: "-1 to +1", "NEGATIVE TO TRACE".

label: Reference Range Indicator
cdisc_notes: Indicates where the value falls with respect to reference range defined by ISORNRLO and ISORNRHI, ISSTNRLO and ISSTNRHI, or by ISSTNRC. Examples: "NORMAL", "ABNORMAL", "HIGH", "LOW". \n Sponsors should specify in the study metadata (Comments column in the Define-XML document) whether ISNRIND refers to the original or standard reference ranges and results. \n Should not be used to indicate clinical significance.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a test was not done. Should be null if a result exists in ISORRES.

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Describes why a measurement or test was not performed. Used in conjunction with ISSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: Name or identifier of the laboratory or vendor who provided the test results.

label: Specimen Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the types of specimen used for a measurement. Example: "SERUM".

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: Free or standardized text describing the condition of the specimen. Examples: "HEMOLYZED", "ICTERIC", "LIPEMIC".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "ELISA", "ELISPOT".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that ISBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record. The value should be "Y" or null. Examples of records that might be derived for the submission datasets include those that represent the average of other records, do not come from the CRF, or are not as originally received or collected. If ISDRVFL="Y", then ISORRES may be null, with ISSTRESC and (if numeric) ISSTRESN having the derived value.

label: Lower Limit of Quantitation
cdisc_notes: Indicates the lower limit of quantitation for an assay. Units will be those used for ISSTRESU.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation.

label: End Date/Time of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the observation.

label: Study Day of Visit/Collection/Exam
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when specimen should be taken. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See ISTPTNUM and ISTPTREF. Examples: "Start", "5 min post".

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numerical version of ISTPT to aid in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time (in ISO 8601) relative to a planned fixed reference (ISTPTREF). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable. Represented as ISO 8601 duration. Examples: "-PT15M" to represent the period of 15 minutes prior to the reference point indicated by ISTPTREF, "PT8H" to represent the period of 8 hours after the reference point indicated by ISTPTREF.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Name of the fixed reference point referred to by ISELTM, ISTPTNUM, and ISTPT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time of the reference time point, ISTPTREF.

label: Binding Agent
cdisc_notes: Text description of the agent that is binding to the entity in the ISTEST variable. ISBDAGNT is used to indicate that there is a binding relationship between the entities in the ISTEST and ISBDAGNT variables, regardless of direction. \n ISBDAGNT is not a method qualifier. It should only be used when the actual interest of the measurement is the binding interaction between the 2 entities in ISTEST and ISBDAGNT. In other words, the combination of ISTEST and ISBDAGNT should describe the entity or the analyte being measured, without the need for additional variables. \n The binding agent may be (but is not limited to) a test article, a portion of the test article, a related compound, an endogenous molecule, an allergen, or an infectious agent.

label: Test Condition Agent
cdisc_notes: The textual description of the agent used to impose a test condition. Examples are different stimulating agents used in immunoassays such as those in the Interferon Gamma Response assay (e.g., Mycobacterium tuberculosis ESAT-6, CFP-10, TB 7.7, Mitogen).

label: Molecule Secreted by Cells
cdisc_notes: Text description of the entity secreted by the cells represented in ISTEST. The combination of ISTEST and ISMSCBCE should describe the entity or the analyte being measured, without the need for additional variables.

label: Test Condition
cdisc_notes: Identifies any planned condition imposed by the assay system on the specimen at the time the test is performed.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Specimen ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external specimen identifier. Example: specimen ID.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number on the Lab page.

label: Lab Test or Examination Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in LBTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in LBTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). LBTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "ALT", "LDH".

label: Lab Test or Examination Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. Note: Any test normally performed by a clinical laboratory is considered a lab test. The value in LBTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Alanine Aminotransferase", "Lactate Dehydrogenase".

label: Test Operational Objective
cdisc_notes: Text description of the high-level purpose of the test at the operational level.

label: Category for Lab Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records across subjects. Examples: "HEMATOLOGY", "URINALYSIS", "CHEMISTRY".

label: Subcategory for Lab Test
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of a test category. Examples: "DIFFERENTIAL", "COAGULATION", "LIVER FUNCTION", "ELECTROLYTES".

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for LBORRES. Example: "g/L".

label: Result Scale
cdisc_notes: Classifies the scale of the original result value; for example, whether the result is ordinal, nominal, quantitative, or narrative.

label: Result Type
cdisc_notes: Classifies the kind of result (i.e., property type) originally reported for the test. Examples include substance concentration, proportion, mass rate, and arbitrary concentration.

label: Collected Summary Result Type
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate the type of collected summary result. This includes source summary results collected on a CRF or provided by an external vendor (e.g., central lab). If the summary result is derived by the sponsor using individual source data records from SDTM, the derived summary result is represented in ADaM. If the summary result is produced and reported by the lab, the collected summary result is represented in SDTM.

label: Reference Range Lower Limit in Orig Unit
cdisc_notes: Lower end of reference range for continuous measurement in original units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Reference Range Upper Limit in Orig Unit
cdisc_notes: Upper end of reference range for continuous measurement in original units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Lower Limit of Detection
cdisc_notes: The lowest threshold (as originally received or collected) for reliably detecting the presence or absence of substance measured by a specific test. The value for the field will be as described in documentation from the instrument or lab vendor.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from LBORRES in a standard format or standard units. LBSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in LBSTRESN. For example, if a test has results "NONE", "NEG", and "NEGATIVE" in LBORRES and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in LBSTRESC as "NEGATIVE". For other examples, see Original and Standardized Results.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from LBSTRESC. LBSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized unit used for LBSTRESC or LBSTRESN.

label: Reference Range Lower Limit-Std Units
cdisc_notes: Lower end of reference range for continuous measurements for LBSTRESC/LBSTRESN in standardized units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Reference Range Upper Limit-Std Units
cdisc_notes: Upper end of reference range for continuous measurements in standardized units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Reference Range for Char Rslt-Std Units
cdisc_notes: For normal range values that are character in ordinal scale or if categorical ranges were supplied. Examples: "-1 to +1", "NEGATIVE TO TRACE".

label: Reference Range Indicator
cdisc_notes: Indicates where the value falls with respect to reference range defined by LBORNRLO and LBORNRHI, LBSTNRLO and LBSTNRHI, or by LBSTNRC. Examples: "NORMAL", "ABNORMAL", "HIGH", "LOW". Sponsors should specify in the study metadata (Comments column in the Define-XML document) whether LBNRIND refers to the original or standard reference ranges and results. LBNRIND is not used to indicate clinical significance.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate exam not done. Should be null if a result exists in LBORRES.

label: Reason Test Not Done
cdisc_notes: Describes why a measurement or test was not performed. Examples: "BROKEN EQUIPMENT", "SUBJECT REFUSED", or "SPECIMEN LOST". Used in conjunction with LBSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: The name or identifier of the laboratory that performed the test.

label: LOINC Code
cdisc_notes: Code for the lab test from the LOINC code system. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to map the terms utilizing the Define-XML external codelist attributes.

label: Specimen Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE".

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: The physical state or quality of a sample for an assessment. Examples: "HEMOLYZED", "ICTERIC", "LIPEMIC".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "EIA" (enzyme immunoassay), "ELECTROPHORESIS", "DIPSTICK".

label: Analysis Method
cdisc_notes: Analysis method applied to obtain a summarized result. Analysis method describes the method of secondary processing applied to a complex observation result (e.g., a calculation used to measure eGFR).

label: Test Method Sensitivity
cdisc_notes: The sensitivity of the test methodology with respect to observation, detection, or quantification.

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that LBBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Fasting Status
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify fasting status. Examples: "Y", "N".

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record. The value should be "Y" or null. Records that represent the average of other records, or do not come from the CRF, or are not as originally received or collected are examples of records that might be derived for the submission datasets. If LBDRVFL="Y", then LBORRES may be null, with LBSTRESC and (if numeric) LBSTRESN having the derived value.

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than one assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted evaluation. Expected values can include "Y", "N", or null. This is not intended to be an analysis flag to indicate acceptability for a given analysis.

label: Toxicity
cdisc_notes: Description of toxicity quantified by LBTOXGR. The sponsor is expected to provide the name of the scale and version used to map the terms, utilizing the external codelist element in the Define-XML document.

label: Standard Toxicity Grade
cdisc_notes: Records toxicity grade value using a standard toxicity scale (e.g., the NCI CTCAE). If value is from a numeric scale, represent only the number (e.g., "2" not "Grade 2"). The sponsor is expected to provide the name of the scale and version used to map the terms, utilizing the external codelist element in the Define-XML document.

label: Clinically Significant, Collected
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate whether a collected observation is clinically significant based on judgment.

label: Lower Limit of Quantitation
cdisc_notes: Indicates the lower limit of quantitation for an assay. Units will be those used for --STRESU.

label: Upper Limit of Quantitation
cdisc_notes: Indicates the upper limit of quantitation for an assay. Units will be those used for --STRESU.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected.

label: Date/Time of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Date/time of specimen collection represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: End Date/Time of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: End date/time of specimen collection represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Study day of specimen collection, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics. This formula should be consistent across the submission.

label: Study Day of End of Observation
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when specimen should be taken. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See LBTPTNUM and LBTPTREF. Examples: "Start", "5 min post".

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numerical version of LBTPT to aid in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time (in ISO 8601) relative to a planned fixed reference (LBTPTREF). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable. Represented as ISO 8601 duration. Examples: "-PT15M" to represent the period of 15 minutes prior to the reference point indicated by LBTPTREF, "PT8H" to represent the period of 8 hours after the reference point indicated by LBTPTREF.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Name of the fixed reference point referred to by LBELTM, LBTPTNUM, and LBTPT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time of the reference time point, LBTPTREF.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Evaluation interval associated with a QSTEST question represented in ISO 8601 character format. Example: "-P2Y" to represent an interval of 2 years in the question "Have you experienced any episodes in the past 2 years?".

label: Point in Time Flag
cdisc_notes: An indication that the specimen was collected at a single point in time. The value is "Y" or null. The intent of this variable in the LB domain is to aid mapping to LOINC codes in the dataset, when LOINC part "Time Aspect" = "Pt".

label: Planned Duration
cdisc_notes: Planned duration of specimen collection. If LBPTFL is "Y" then LBPDUR is null.

label: Binding Agent
cdisc_notes: The textual description of the agent that is binding to the entity in the LBTEST variable. The LBBDAGNT variable is used to indicate that there is a binding relationship between the entities in the LBTEST and LBBDAGNT variables, regardless of direction. \n LBBDAGNT is not a method qualifier. It should only be used when the actual interest of the measurement is the binding interaction between the 2 entities in LBTEST and LBBDAGNT. In other words, the combination of LBTEST and LBBDAGNT should describe the thing, the entity, or the analyte being measured, without the need for additional variables. \n The binding agent may be (but is not limited to) a test article, a portion of the test article, a related compound, or an endogenous molecule.

label: Test Condition
cdisc_notes: Identifies any planned condition imposed by the assay system on the specimen at the time the test is performed.

label: Number of Hours Subject Has Been Fasting
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Specimen Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE", "DNA", "RNA".

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized unit used for LBSTRESC or LBSTRESN.

label: Lab Test or Examination Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. Note any test normally performed by a clinical laboratory is considered a lab test. The value in LBTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Alanine Aminotransferase", "Lactate Dehydrogenase".

label: Lab Test or Examination Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in LBTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in LBTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). LBTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "ALT", "LDH".

cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Specimen ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external specimen identifier. Example: Specimen ID.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. Perhaps preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number on the Lab page.

label: Lab Test or Examination Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in LBTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in LBTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). LBTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "ALT", "LDH".

label: Lab Test or Examination Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. Note any test normally performed by a clinical laboratory is considered a lab test. The value in LBTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Alanine Aminotransferase", "Lactate Dehydrogenase".

cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records across subjects. Examples: "HEMATOLOGY", "URINALYSIS", "CHEMISTRY".

cdisc_notes: A further categorization of a test category such as "DIFFERENTIAL", "COAGULATION", "LIVER FUNCTION", "ELECTROLYTES".

cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

cdisc_notes: Lower end of reference range for continuous measurement in original units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Reference Range Upper Limit in Orig Unit
cdisc_notes: Upper end of reference range for continuous measurement in original units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from LBORRES in a standard format or standard units. LBSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in LBSTRESN. For example, if a test has results "NONE", "NEG", and "NEGATIVE" in LBORRES and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in LBSTRESC as "NEGATIVE". For other examples, see general assumptions.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from LBSTRESC. LBSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Reference Range Lower Limit-Std Units
cdisc_notes: Lower end of reference range for continuous measurements for LBSTRESC/LBSTRESN in standardized units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Reference Range Upper Limit-Std Units
cdisc_notes: Upper end of reference range for continuous measurements in standardized units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Reference Range for Char Rslt-Std Units
cdisc_notes: For normal range values that are character in ordinal scale or if categorical ranges were supplied (e.g., "-1 to +1", "NEGATIVE TO TRACE").

Sponsors should specify in the study metadata (Comments column in the Define-XML document) whether LBNRIND refers to the original or standard reference ranges and results. 3. Should not be used to indicate clinical significance.

cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Focus of Study-Specific Interest
cdisc_notes: Identification of a focus of study-specific interest on or within a subject or specimen as called out in the protocol for which a measurement, test, or examination was performed. The value in this variable should have inherent semantic meaning.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external specimen identifier (e.g., sample ID for a subject sample from which a microbial culture was generated).

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, it may be used to link genetic findings (in the PF domain) about a microbe to the original culture of that microbe (in MB), or to susceptibility records (in MS) if needed.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship.

label: Microbiology Test or Finding Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or finding described in MBTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in MBTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). MBTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "MCORGIDN" for Microbial Organism Identification "GMNCOC" for Gram Negative Cocci.

label: Microbiology Test or Finding Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in MBTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Microbial Organism Identification", "Gram Negative Cocci", "HIV-1 RNA".

label: Measurement, Test or Examination Detail
cdisc_notes: Further description of MBTESTCD and MBTEST. Example: "VIRAL LOAD" when MBTESTCD represents viral genetic material, such as "HCRNA", "QUANTIFICATION" when MBTESTCD represents any organism being quantified.

label: Category
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records.

label: Subcategory
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of MBCAT values.

label: Position of Subject During Observation
cdisc_notes: Position of the subject during a measurement or examination. Examples: "SUPINE", "STANDING", "SITTING".

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the microbiology measurement or finding as originally received or collected. Examples for "GRAM STAIN" findings: "+3 MODERATE", "+2 FEW", "<10". Examples for "CULTURE PLATE" findings: "KLEBSIELLA PNEUMONIAE", "STREPTOCOCCUS PNEUMONIAE".

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original unit for MBORRES. Example: "mcg/mL".

label: Reference Range Lower Limit in Orig Unit
cdisc_notes: Lower end of reference range for continuous measurement in original units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Reference Range Upper Limit in Orig Unit
cdisc_notes: Upper end of reference range for continuous measurement in original units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Result or Finding in Standard Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings copied or derived from MBORRES, in a standard format or standard units. MBSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in MBSTRESN. For example, if a test has results "+3 MODERATE", "MOD", and "MODERATE" in MBORRES and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in MBSTRESC as "MODERATE".

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from MBSTRESC. MBSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for MBSTRESC and MBSTRESN.

cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element which the specimen collection occurred.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the specimen was collected.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Date/time of specimen collection.

label: End Date/Time of Observation
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the observation.

label: Study Day of Visit/Collection/Exam
cdisc_notes: Study day of the specimen collection, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics. This formula should be consistent across the submission.

label: Study Day of End of Observation
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Collected Duration
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of an event, intervention, or finding. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when specimen should be taken. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See MBTPTNUM and MBTPTREF. Examples: "Start", "5 min post".

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of MBTPT used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time (in ISO 8601) relative to a planned fixed reference (MBTPTREF). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date time variable. Represented as an ISO 8601 duration. Examples: "-PT15M" to represent the period of 15 minutes prior to the reference point indicated by MBTPTREF, or "PT8H" to represent the period of 8 hours after the reference point indicated by MBTPTREF.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Name of the fixed reference point referred to by MBELTM, MBTPTNUM, and MBTPT. Example: "PREVIOUS DOSE".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point, MBTPTREF.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable --ENTPT.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 or other character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by --ENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25" or "VISIT 2".

label: Reason Central Lab Sample Not Collected
Reason Isolate not Sent to Central Lab
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external medical history identifier.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number on a Medical History CRF page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Reported Term for the Medical History
cdisc_notes: Verbatim or preprinted CRF term for the medical condition or event.

label: Modified Reported Term
cdisc_notes: If MHTERM is modified to facilitate coding, then MHMODIFY will contain the modified text.

label: Lowest Level Term
cdisc_notes: MedDRA Lowest Level Term.

label: Lowest Level Term Code
cdisc_notes: MedDRA Lowest Level Term code.

label: Dictionary-Derived Term
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived text description of MHTERM or MHMODIFY. Equivalent to the Preferred Term (PT in MedDRA). The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to map the terms utilizing the external codelist element in the Define-XML document.

label: Medical History Event Date Type
cdisc_notes: Specifies the aspect of the medical condition or event by which MHSTDTC and/or the MHENDTC is defined. Examples: "DIAGNOSIS", "SYMPTOMS", "RELAPSE", "INFECTION".

label: Preferred Term Code
cdisc_notes: MedDRA Preferred Term code.

label: High Level Term
cdisc_notes: MedDRA High Level Term from the primary path.

label: High Level Term Code
cdisc_notes: MedDRA High Level Term code from the primary path.

label: High Level Group Term
cdisc_notes: MedDRA High Level Group Term from the primary path.

label: High Level Group Term Code
cdisc_notes: MedDRA High Level Group Term code from the primary path.

label: Category for Medical History
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records. Examples: "CARDIAC", "GENERAL".

label: Subcategory for Medical History
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of the condition or event.

label: Medical History Event Pre-Specified
cdisc_notes: A value of "Y" indicates that this medical history event was prespecified on the CRF. Values are null for spontaneously reported events (i.e., those collected as free-text verbatim terms).

label: Medical History Occurrence
cdisc_notes: Used when the occurrence of specific medical history conditions is solicited, to indicate whether ("Y"/"N") a medical condition (MHTERM) had ever occurred. Values are null for spontaneously reported events.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: The status indicates that the prespecified question was not asked/answered.

label: Reason Medical History Not Collected
cdisc_notes: Describes the reason why data for a prespecified condition was not collected. Used in conjunction with MHSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Body System or Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived. Body system or organ class that is involved in an event or measurement from a standard hierarchy (e.g., MedDRA). When using a multi-axial dictionary such as MedDRA, this should contain the SOC used for the sponsor's analyses and summary tables which may not necessarily be the primary SOC.

label: Body System or Organ Class Code
cdisc_notes: MedDRA System Organ Class code corresponding to --BODSYS assigned for analysis.

label: Primary System Organ Class
cdisc_notes: MedDRA primary System Organ Class associated with the event.

label: Primary System Organ Class Code
cdisc_notes: MedDRA primary System Organ Class code.

label: Location of Event
cdisc_notes: Describes anatomical location relevant for the event. Example: "ARM" for skin rash.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Severity/Intensity
cdisc_notes: The severity or intensity of the event. Examples: "MILD", "MODERATE", "SEVERE".

label: Other Action Taken
cdisc_notes: Describes other actions taken as a result of the event that are unrelated to dose adjustments of study treatment.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the medical history event.

label: Date/Time of History Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of the medical history observation represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Start Date/Time of Medical History Event
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of the medical history event represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: End Date/Time of Medical History Event
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the medical history event.

label: Study Day of History Collection
cdisc_notes: Study day of medical history collection, measured as integer day. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics. This formula should be consistent across the submission.

label: Study Day of Start of Observation
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of start of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Observation
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Collected Duration
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of an event, intervention, or finding. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

label: End Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Describes the end of the event relative to the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point (represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics). \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: Start Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable --STTPT.

label: Start Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 or other character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by --STRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-15" or "VISIT 1".

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the event as being before or after the reference time point defined by variable MHENTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the reference point referred to by MHENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25", "VISIT 2".

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject. This is not the treatment group number.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external specimen identifier. Example: specimen barcode number.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be printed on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: line number from the MI Findings page.

label: Microscopic Examination Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in MITEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in MITESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). MITESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "HER2", "BRCA1", "TTF1".

label: Microscopic Examination Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in MITEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2", "Breast Cancer Susceptibility Gene 1", "Thyroid Transcription Factor 1".

label: Microscopic Examination Detail
cdisc_notes: Further description of the test performed in producing the MI result. This would be used to represent specific attributes, such as intensity score or percentage of cells displaying presence of the biomarker or compound.

label: Category for Microscopic Finding
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records.

label: Subcategory for Microscopic Finding
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of MICAT.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the histopathology measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original unit for MIORRES.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from MIORRES in a standard format or standard units. MISTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in MISTRESN.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from MISTRESC. MISTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized unit used for MISTRESC and MISTRESN.

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding. Examples: "MALIGNANT" or "BENIGN" for tumor findings.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate examination not done or result is missing. Should be null if a result exists in MIORRES or have a value of "NOT DONE" when MIORRES = "NULL".

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with MISTAT when value is NOT DONE. Examples: "SAMPLE AUTOLYZED", "SPECIMEN LOST".

label: Laboratory/Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: Name or identifier of the vendor (e.g., laboratory) that provided the test results.

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Subject of the observation. Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "TISSUE", "BLOOD", "BONE MARROW".

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: Free or standardized text describing the condition of the specimen. Example: "AUTOLYZED".

label: Specimen Collection Location
cdisc_notes: Location relevant to the collection of the specimen. Examples: "LUNG", "KNEE JOINT", "ARM", "THIGH".

label: Specimen Laterality within Subject
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for laterality of the location of the specimen in MILOC. Examples: "LEFT", "RIGHT", "BILATERAL".

label: Specimen Directionality within Subject
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for directionality of the location of the specimen in MILOC. Examples: "DORSAL", "PROXIMAL".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. This could include the technique or type of staining used for the slides. Examples: "IHC", "Crystal violet", "Safranin", "Trypan blue", or "Propidium iodide".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. The value should be "Y" or null. Note that MIBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline flag for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Example: "PATHOLOGIST", "PEER REVIEW", "SPONSOR PATHOLOGIST".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the specimen was collected.

label: Date/Time of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Date/time of specimen collection, in ISO 8601 format.

label: Study Day of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Study day of specimen collection, in integer days. The algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in the Demographics (DM) domain.
