Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject (or within a parameter, in the case of the Trial Summary domain). May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID or a medical image.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. Example: Preprinted line identifier on a Concomitant Medications page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship.

label: Short Name of Musculoskeletal Test
cdisc_notes: Short character value for MKTEST used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical format to a horizontal format. The value in MKTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). MKTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "TNDRIND", "SWLLIND", "SGJSNSCR".

label: Name of Musculoskeletal Test
cdisc_notes: Long name For MKTESTCD. Examples: "Tenderness Indicator", "Swollen Indicator", "Sharp/Genant JSN Score".

label: Category for Musculoskeletal Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Examples: "SWOLLEN/TENDER JOINT ASSESSMENT".

label: Subcategory for Musculoskeletal Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of MKCAT values.

label: Position of Subject
cdisc_notes: Position of the subject during a measurement or examination. Examples: "SUPINE", "STANDING", "SITTING".

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Unit for MKORRES.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from MKORRES in a standard format or in standard units. MKSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in MKSTRESN. For example, if various tests have results "NONE", "NEG", and "NEGATIVE" in MKORRES and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in MKSTRESC as "NEGATIVE".

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from MKSTRESC. MKSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for MKSTRESC and MKSTRESN.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question was not asked or a test was not done, or that a test was attempted but did not generate a result. Should be null if a result exists in MKORRES.

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with MKSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Examples: "INTERPHALANGEAL JOINT 1", "SHOULDER JOINT".

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "X-RAY", "MRI", "CT SCAN".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that MKBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record (e.g., a record that represents the average of other records such as a computed baseline). Should be "Y" or null.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "RADIOLOGIST".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in MKEVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1" or "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the assessment was made.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation.

label: Study Day of Visit/Collection/Exam
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See MKTPTNUM and MKTPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned Elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (MKTPTREF; e.g., "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL"). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval, represented as ISO duration.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by MKELTM, MKTPTNUM, and MKTPT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by MKTPTREF.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. Examples: a number preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier, record identifier defined in the sponsor's operational database.

label: Name of Meal
cdisc_notes: Verbatim food product name that is either preprinted or collected on a CRF.

label: Category for Meal
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of MLTRT values.

label: Subcategory for Meal
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of MLCAT values.

label: ML Pre-specified
cdisc_notes: Used when a specific meal is prespecified on a CRF. Values should be "Y" or null.

label: ML Occurrence
cdisc_notes: Used to record whether a prespecified meal occurred when information about the occurrence of a specific meal is solicited.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate when a question about the occurrence of a prespecified meal was not answered. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Meal Not Collected
cdisc_notes: Describes the reason a response to a question about the occurrence of a meal was not collected. Used in conjunction with MLSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Dose
cdisc_notes: Amount of MLTRT consumed. Not populated when MLDOSTXT is populated.

label: Dose Description
cdisc_notes: Amount description of MLTRT consumed, collected in text form. Not populated when MLDOSE is populated. Examples: "<1 per day", "200-400".

label: Dose Units
cdisc_notes: Units for MLDOSE, MLDOSTOT, or MLDOSTXT.

label: Dose Form
cdisc_notes: Dosage form for MLTRT. Example: "BAR, CHEWABLE".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the meal started.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the meal.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of the meal represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Start Date/Time of Meal
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of the meal represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: End Date/Time of Meal
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the meal represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Visit/Collection/Exam
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of the visit/collection expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of Start of Meal
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of start of the meal expressed in integer days relative to sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Meal
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of the meal expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Duration of Meal
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of the meal represented in ISO 8601 character format. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point. See MLTPTNUM and MLTPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time (in ISO 8601) relative to the planned fixed reference (MLTPTREF). This variable is useful when there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable. Represented as an ISO 8601 duration.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by MLELTM, MLTPTNUM, and MLTPT.

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by MLTPTREF in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Disease Milestone Instance Name
cdisc_notes: The name of a specific instance of a disease milestone type (MIDSTYPE) described in the Trial Disease Milestones dataset. This should be unique within a subject. Used only in conjunction with RELMIDS and MIDSDTC.

label: Temporal Relation to Milestone Instance
cdisc_notes: The temporal relationship of the observation to the disease milestone instance name in MIDS. Examples: "IMMEDIATELY BEFORE", "AT TIME OF", "AFTER".

label: Disease Milestone Instance Date/Time
cdisc_notes: The start date/time of the disease milestone instance name in MIDS, in ISO 8601 format.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Non-host Organism ID
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for a non-host organism which should only be used when the organism is the subject of the TEST. This variable should be populated with an intuitive name based on the identity of the non-host organism as reported by a lab (e.g., "A/California/7/2009 (H1N1)"). It is not to be used as a qualifier of the result in the record on which it appears.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject (or within a parameter, in the case of the Trial Summary domain). May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain. In SDTMIG v3.2 this was an Expected variable. In this version, the core designation has been changed to Permissible.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier (e.g., an identifier for the culture/isolate being tested for susceptibility).

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, it may be used to link genetic findings (in the PF domain) about a microbe to the original culture of that microbe (in MB), or to susceptibility records (in MS) if needed.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Short Name of Assessment
cdisc_notes: Short character value for MSTEST used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical format to a horizontal format. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in MSTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). MSTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "MIC" for Minimum Inhibitory Concentration; "MICROSUS" for Microbial Susceptibility.

label: Name of Assessment
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in MSTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Minimum Inhibitory Concentration", "Microbial Susceptibility".

label: Measurement, Test or Examination Detail
cdisc_notes: Further description of MSTESTCD and MSTEST.

label: Agent Name
cdisc_notes: The name of the agent for which resistance is tested. The agent specified may be based on genetic markers or direct phenotypic drug sensitivity testing. Examples: "Penicillin", name of study drug.

label: Agent Concentration
cdisc_notes: Numeric concentration of agent listed in MSAGENT.

label: Agent Concentration Units
cdisc_notes: Units for value of the agent concentration listed in MSCONC. Example: "mg/L".

label: Category
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of MSTEST values.

label: Subcategory
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of MSCAT values.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Unit for MSORRES. Examples: "ug/mL".

label: Result or Finding in Standard Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from MSORRES in a standard format or in standard units. MSSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in MSSTRESN. For example, if various tests have results "NONE", "NEG", and "NEGATIVE" in MSORRES and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in MSSTRESC as "NEGATIVE".

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from MSSTRESC. MSSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for MSSTRESC and MSSTRESN. Example: "mol/L".

label: Normal/Reference Range Indicator
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate the value is outside the normal range or reference range. May be defined by MSORNRLO and MSORNRHI or other objective criteria. Examples: "Y", "N", "HIGH", "LOW", "NORMAL". "ABNORMAL".

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding. In SDTMIG v3.2, MSRESCAT was used to categorize a numeric susceptibility result represented in MSORRES as either "SUSCEPTIBLE", "INTERMEDIATE", or "RESISTANT". However, results from some susceptibility tests may report only a categorical result and not a numeric result. Thus, in order for susceptibility results to be represented consistently, MSRESCAT should no longer be used for this purpose. In this version, the core designation has been changed from Expected to Permissible.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question was not asked or a test was not done, or a test was attempted but did not generate a result. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with MSSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: External File Path
cdisc_notes: Filename for an external file.

label: Laboratory/Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: Name or identifier of the vendor (e.g., laboratory) that provided the test results.

label: LOINC Code
cdisc_notes: Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes (LOINC) code for the topic variable such as a lab test.

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Example: "SPUTUM".

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: Defines the condition of the specimen. Example: "CLOUDY".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "EPSILOMETER", "MACRO BROTH DILUTION".

label: Analysis Method
cdisc_notes: Analysis method applied to obtain a summarized result. Analysis method describes the method of secondary processing applied to a complex observation result (e.g., an image or a genetic sequence).

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that MSBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Fasting Status
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify fasting status. Valid values include "Y", "N", "U", or null if not relevant.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record (e.g., a record that represents the average of other records such as a computed baseline). Should be "Y" or null.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "MICROSCOPIST".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in MSEVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1" or "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than 1 assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered, by an independent assessor, to be the accepted evaluation. Expected to be "Y" or null.

label: Lower Limit of Quantitation
cdisc_notes: Indicates the lower limit of quantitation for an assay. Units will be those used for MSSTRESU.

label: Upper Limit of Quantitation
cdisc_notes: Indicates the upper limit of quantitation for an assay. Units will be those used for MSSTRESU.

label: Repetition Number
cdisc_notes: The incidence number of a test that is repeated within a given timeframe for the same test. The level of granularity can vary (e.g., within a time point, within a visit). Examples: multiple measurements of blood pressure, multiple analyses of a sample.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the specimen was collected.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the specimen was collected.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation.

label: End Date/Time of Observation
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the observation.

label: Study Day of Visit/Collection/Exam
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Observation
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Duration
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of an event, intervention, or finding. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point (e.g., time of last dose). See MSTPTNUM and MSTPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (MSTPTREF; e.g., previous dose, previous meal). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval, represented as ISO duration.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by MSELTM, MSTPTNUM, and MSTPT. Example: "PREVIOUS DOSE".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by MSTPTREF.

label: Start Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the observation as being before, during, or after the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics.

label: End Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before, during or after the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding MSTESTCD. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months before the assessment.

label: Evaluation Interval Text
cdisc_notes: Evaluation interval associated with an observation, where the interval is not able to be represented in ISO 8601 format. Examples: "LIFETIME", "LAST NIGHT", "RECENTLY", "OVER THE LAST FEW WEEKS".

label: Start Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable MSSTTPT.

label: Start Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time, in ISO 8601 or other character format, of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by MSSTRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-15" or "VISIT 1".

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable MSENTPT.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 or other character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by MSENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25" or "VISIT 2".

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Focus of Study-Specific Interest
cdisc_notes: Identification of a focus of study-specific interest on or within a subject or specimen as called out in the protocol for which a measurement, test, or examination was performed, such as a drug application site (e.g., "Injection site 1", "Biopsy site 1", "Treated site 1") or a more specific focus (e.g., "OD" (right eye), "Upper left quadrant of the back"). The value in this variable should have inherent semantic meaning.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external procedure identifier.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number from the Procedure or Test page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link a procedure to the assessment results over the course of the study.

label: Link Group
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship.

label: Short Name of Nervous System Test
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in NVTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in NVTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). NVTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "SUVR", "N75LAT", "P100LAT","N145LAT".

label: Name of Nervous System Test
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in NVTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Standard Uptake Value Ratio", "N75 Latency", "P100 Latency", "N145 Latency".

label: Category for Nervous System Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Example: "VISUAL EVOKED POTENTIAL".

label: Subcategory for Nervous System Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of NVCAT values.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the procedure measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for NVORRES.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings copied or derived from NVORRES, in a standard format or standard units. NVSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in NVSTRESN.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from NVSTRESC. NVSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized unit used for NVSTRESC or NVSTRESN.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a test was not done, or a measurement was not taken. Should be null if a result exists in NVORRES.

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Describes why a measurement or test was not performed. Examples: "BROKEN EQUIPMENT", "SUBJECT REFUSED". Used in conjunction with NVSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Examples: "BRAIN", "EYE", "PRECUNEUS", "CINGULATE CORTEX".

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "EEG", "PET/CT SCAN ", "FDGPET".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that NVBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline flag for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record (e.g., a record that represents the average of other records such as a computed baseline). Should be "Y" or null.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "RADIOLOGIST".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in NVEVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST 1", "RADIOLOGIST 2".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the assessment was made.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Date of procedure or test.

label: Study Day of Visit/Collection/Exam
cdisc_notes: Study day of the procedure or test, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when measurement should be taken. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point (e.g., "TIME OF LAST DOSE"). See NVTPTNUM and NVTPTREF. Examples: "START", "5 MIN POST".

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numerical version of NVTPT to aid in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time (in ISO 8601) relative to a fixed time point reference (NVTPTREF). Not a clock time or a date time variable. Represented as an ISO 8601 duration. Examples: "-PT15M" to represent the period of 15 minutes prior to the reference point indicated by NVTPTREF, "PT8H" to represent the period of 8 hours after the reference point indicated by NVTPTREF.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Name of the fixed reference point referred to by NVELTM, NVTPTNUM, and NVTPT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by --TPTREF in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Focus of Study-Specific Interest
cdisc_notes: Identification of a focus of study-specific interest on or within a subject or specimen as called out in the protocol for which a measurement, test, or examination was performed.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship.

label: Link Group
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship.

label: Short Name of Ophthalmic Test or Exam
cdisc_notes: Short character value for OETEST used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical format to a horizontal format. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in OETESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). OETESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Example: "NUMLCOR".

label: Name of Ophthalmic Test or Exam
cdisc_notes: Long name for the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in OETEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Example: "Number of Letters Correct" for OETESTCD = "NUMLCOR".

label: Ophthalmic Test or Exam Detail
cdisc_notes: Further description of OETESTCD and OETEST.

label: Category for Ophthalmic Test or Exam
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Examples: "VISUAL ACUITY", "CONTRAST SENSITIVITY", "OCULAR COMFORT".

label: Subcategory for Ophthalmic Test or Exam
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of OECAT values. Example: "HIGH CONTRAST" or "LOW CONTRAST" when OECAT is "VISUAL ACUITY".

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected. Examples: "120", "<1, NORMAL", "RED SPOT VISIBLE".

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original unit for OEORRES. Examples: "mm", "um".

label: Normal Range Lower Limit-Original Units
cdisc_notes: Lower end of normal range or reference range for results stored in OEORRES.

label: Normal Range Upper Limit-Original Units
cdisc_notes: Upper end of normal range or reference range for results stored in OEORRES.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings copied or derived from OEORRES, in a standard format or in standard units. OESTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in OESTRESN.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from OESTRESC. OESTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for OESTRESC and OESTRESN. Examples: "mm", "um".

label: Normal Range Lower Limit-Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Lower end of normal range or reference range for standardized results (e.g., OESTRESC, OESTRESN) represented in standardized units (OESTRESU).

label: Normal Range Upper Limit-Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Upper end of normal range or reference range for standardized results (e.g., OESTRESC, OESTRESN) represented in standardized units (OESTRESU).

label: Normal Range for Character Results
cdisc_notes: Normal range or reference range for results stored in OESTRESC that are character in ordinal or categorical scale. Example: "Negative to Trace".

label: Normal/Reference Range Indicator
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate the value is outside the normal range or reference range. May be defined by OEORNRLO and OEORNRHI or other objective criteria. Examples: "Y", "N"; "HIGH", "LOW"; "NORMAL", "ABNORMAL".

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding or medical status per interpretation of test results. Examples: "POSITIVE", "NEGATIVE". The variable OERESCAT is not meant to replace the use of OENRIND for cases where normal ranges are provided.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question was not asked or a test was not done, or a test was attempted but did not generate a result. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with OESTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: External File Path
cdisc_notes: Filename for an external file, such as one for a retinal OCT image.

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Examples: "EYE" for a finding record relative to the complete eye, "RETINA" for a measurement or assessment of only the retina.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution (i.e., arrangement of, apportioning of). Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Example: "ETDRS EYE CHART" for OETESTCD = "NUMLCOR". The different methods may offer different functionality or granularity, affecting the set of results and associated meaning.

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that OEBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record (e.g., a record that represents the average of other records such as a computed baseline). Should be "Y" or null.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "INVESTIGATOR".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in OEEVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than one assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered, by an independent assessor, to be the accepted evaluation. Expected to be "Y" or null.

label: Repetition Number
cdisc_notes: The incidence number of a test that is repeated within a given timeframe for the same test. The level of granularity can vary (e.g., within a time point, within a visit). Examples: multiple measurements of blood pressure, multiple analyses of a sample.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the assessment was made.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date/time of the observation.

label: Study Day of Visit/Collection/Exam
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of observation/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (OETPTREF; e.g., "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL"). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval, represented as ISO duration.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by OETPT, OETPTNUM, and OEELTM.

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time of the reference time point, OETPTREF.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Non-host Organism Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for a non-host organism. NHOID should be populated with an intuitive name based on the identity of the organism as reported by the lab. It must be unique for each unique organism as defined by the specific values of the organism's entire known taxonomy described by pairs of OIPARMCD and OIVAL .

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to given to ensure uniqueness within a parameter within an organism (NHOID) within dataset.

label: Non-host Organism ID Element Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the taxon being described. Examples: "GROUP", "GENTYP", "SUBTYP".

label: Non-host Organism ID Element Name
cdisc_notes: Name of the taxon being described. Examples: "Group", "Genotype", "Subtype".

label: Non-host Organism ID Element Value
cdisc_notes: Value for the taxon in OIPARMCD/OIPARM for the organism identified by NHOID.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain to support relationships within the domain and between domains.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external specimen identifier.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Pharmacokinetic Test Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the analyte or specimen characteristic. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in PCTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). PCTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "ASA", "VOL", "SPG".

label: Pharmacokinetic Test Name
cdisc_notes: Name of the analyte or specimen characteristic. Note any test normally performed by a clinical laboratory is considered a lab test. The value in PCTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Acetylsalicylic Acid", "Volume", "Specific Gravity".

label: Test Category
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records. Examples: "ANALYTE", "SPECIMEN PROPERTY".

label: Test Subcategory
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of a test category.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for PCORRES. Example: "mg/L".

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from PCORRES in a standard format or standard units. PCSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in PCSTRESN. For example, if a test has results "NONE", "NEG", and "NEGATIVE" in PCORRES, and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in PCSTRESC as "NEGATIVE". For other examples, see general assumptions.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from PCSTRESC. PCSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized unit used for PCSTRESC and PCSTRESN.

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding. Example: "MALIGNANT" or "BENIGN" for tumor findings.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a result was not obtained. Should be null if a result exists in PCORRES.

label: Reason Test Not Done
cdisc_notes: Describes why a result was not obtained, such as "SPECIMEN LOST". Used in conjunction with PCSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: External File Path
cdisc_notes: Filename for an external file, such as one for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: Name or identifier of the laboratory or vendor who provides the test results.

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE".

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: Free or standardized text describing the condition of the specimen. Examples: "HEMOLYZED", "ICTERIC", "LIPEMIC".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Examples: "RECTAL" for temperature, "ARM" for blood pressure.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "HPLC/MS", "ELISA". This should contain sufficient information and granularity to allow differentiation of various methods that might have been used within a study.

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Fasting Status
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify fasting status.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record. The value should be "Y" or null. Records that represent the average of other records, which do not come from the CRF, are examples of records that would be derived for the submission datasets. If PCDRVFL = "Y", then PCORRES may be null with PCSTRESC, and PCSTRESN (if the result is numeric) having the derived value.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "RADIOLOGIST".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than one assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted evaluation. Expected values can include "Y", "N", or null. This is not intended to be an analysis flag to indicate acceptability for a given analysis.

label: Lower Limit of Quantitation
cdisc_notes: Indicates the lower limit of quantitation for an assay. Units should be those used in PCSTRESU.

label: Upper Limit of Quantitation
cdisc_notes: Indicates the upper limit of quantitation for an assay. Units should be those used in PCSTRESU.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected.

label: Date/Time of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Date/time of specimen collection represented in ISO 8601 character format. If there is no end time, then this will be the collection time.

label: End Date/Time of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: End date/time of specimen collection represented in ISO 8601 character format. If there is no end time, the collection time should be stored in PCDTC, and PCENDTC should be null.

label: Actual Study Day of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Study day of specimen collection, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Observation
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when specimen should be taken. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See PCTPTNUM and PCTPTREF. Examples: "Start", "5 min post".

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numerical version of PCTPT to aid in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time (in ISO 8601) relative to a planned fixed reference (PCTPTREF; e.g., "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL"). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date time variable.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Name of the fixed reference point used as a basis for PCTPT, PCTPTNUM, and PCELTM. Example: "MOST RECENT DOSE".

label: Date/Time of Reference Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time of the reference time point described by PCTPTREF.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Evaluation Interval associated with a PCTEST record represented in ISO 8601 character format. Example: "-PT2H" to represent an evaluation interval of 2 hours prior to a PCTPT.

label: Last IP Taken Before any Food That Day
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to link together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. Perhaps preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number on a CRF.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Body System Examined Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of a part of the body examined in a physical examination. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in PETESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). PETESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "HEAD", "ENT". If the results of the entire physical examination are represented in one record, value should be "PHYSEXAM".

label: Body System Examined
cdisc_notes: Long name of a part of the body examined in a physical examination. The value in PETEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Head", "Ear/Nose/Throat". If the results of the entire physical examination are represented in one record, value should be "Physical Examination".

label: Modified Reported Term
cdisc_notes: If the value of PEORRES is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Category for Examination
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Example: "GENERAL".

label: Subcategory for Examination
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of --CAT values.

label: Body System or Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Body system or organ class (e.g., MedDRA SOC) that is involved for a finding from the standard hierarchy for dictionary-coded results.

label: Verbatim Examination Finding
cdisc_notes: Text description of any abnormal findings. If the examination was completed and there were no abnormal findings, the value should be "NORMAL". If the examination was not performed on a particular body system, or at the subject level, then the value should be null, and "NOT DONE" should appear in PESTAT.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for PEORRES.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: If there are findings for a body system, then either the dictionary preferred term (if findings are coded using a dictionary) or PEORRES (if findings are not encoded) should appear here. If PEORRES is null, PESTRESC must be null.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate exam not done. Must be null if a result exists in PEORRES/PESTRESC.

label: Reason Not Examined
cdisc_notes: Describes why an examination was not performed or why a body system was not examined. Example: "SUBJECT REFUSED". Used in conjunction with PESTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Location of Physical Exam Finding
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Example: "ARM" for skin rash.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterallity. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "PALPATION", "PERCUSSION".

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. Should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: A baseline defined by the sponsor (could be derived in the same manner as PELOBXFL or ABLFL, but is not required to be). The value should be "Y" or null. Note that PEBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline flag for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Example: "INVESTIGATOR".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than one assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted evaluation. Expected values can include "Y", "N", or null. This is not intended to be an analysis flag to indicate acceptability for a given analysis.

label: Severity/Intensity
cdisc_notes: Describes the severity or intensity of a particular finding. Examples: "MILD", "MODERATE", "SEVERE".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the observation date/time of the physical exam finding.

label: Date/Time of Examination
cdisc_notes: Date and time of the physical examination represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Examination
cdisc_notes: Study day of physical exam, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See --TPTNUM and --TPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Type of CT Scan Performed, Specify
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject (or within a parameter, in the case of the Trial Summary domain). May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain. Also used to link together a block of related records in the Trial Summary Information (Section 3.3).

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID, or UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. Example: pre-printed line identifier on a Concomitant Medications page.

label: Assay ID
cdisc_notes: Definition: A unique identifier for a test as maintained by a lab.

label: Short Name of Measurement, Test, or Exam
cdisc_notes: Short character value for --TEST used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical format to a horizontal format. The short value can be up to 8 characters. Examples: "PLAT", "SYSBP", "RRMIN", "EYEEXAM".

label: Name of Measurement, Test, or Exam
cdisc_notes: Long name For --TESTCD. Examples: Platelets, Systolic Blood Pressure, Summary (Min) RR Duration, Eye Examination.

label: Category
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Examples: "HEMATOLOGY", "URINALYSIS", "CHEMISTRY", "HAMD 17", "SF36 V2.0 ACUTE", "EGFR MUTATION ANALYSIS".

label: Subcategory
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of --CAT values. Example: "WBC DIFFERENTIAL".

label: Body System or Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Body System or Organ Class that is involved for a finding from the standard hierarchy for dictionary-coded results. Example: MedDRA SOC.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected. Examples: "120", "<1", "POS".

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Unit for --ORRES and --ORREF. Examples: "in", "LB", "kg/L".

label: Result or Finding in Standard Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from --ORRES in a standard format or in standard units. --STRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in --STRESN. For example, if various tests have results "NONE", "NEG", and "NEGATIVE" in --ORRES and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in --STRESC as "NEGATIVE".

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from --STRESC. --STRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for --STRESC, --STRESN, --STREFC, and --STREFN. Example: "mol/L".

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question was not asked or a test was not done, or a test was attempted but did not generate a result. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with --STAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: External File Path
cdisc_notes: Filename for an external file, such as one for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Laboratory/Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: Name or identifier of the vendor (e.g., laboratory) that provided the test results.

label: LOINC Code
cdisc_notes: Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes (LOINC) code for the topic variable such as a lab test.

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE", "DNA", "RNA".

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: Defines the condition of the specimen. Example: "CLOUDY".

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Examples: "RECTAL" for temperature, "ARM" for blood pressure.

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "EIA" (enzyme immunoassay), "ELECTROPHORESIS", "DIPSTICK".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally-derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date or start date and time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected (see Section Trial Arms).

label: Date/Time of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation.

label: Study Day of Specimen Collection
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See --TPTNUM and --TPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned Elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (--TPTREF) such as "Previous Dose" or "Previous Meal". This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by --ELTM, --TPTNUM, --TPT, --STINT, and --ENINT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: Sequence Number

label: Link ID

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Parameter Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the pharmacokinetic parameter. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in PPTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). PPTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "AUCALL", "TMAX", "CMAX".

label: Parameter Name
cdisc_notes: Name of the pharmacokinetic parameter. The value in PPTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "AUC All", "Time of CMAX", "Max Conc".

label: Parameter Category
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records. For PP, this should be the name of the analyte in PCTEST whose profile the parameter is associated with.

label: Parameter Subcategory
cdisc_notes: Categorization of the model type used to calculate the PK parameters. Examples: "COMPARTMENTAL", "NON-COMPARTMENTAL".

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for PPORRES. Example: "ng/L". See PP Assumption 3.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from PPORRES in a standard format or standard units. PPSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in PPSTRESN.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from PPSTRESC. PPSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding. Example: "MALIGNANT" or "BENIGN" for tumor findings.

label: Completion Status
