label: Reason Parameter Not Calculated
cdisc_notes: Describes why a parameter was not calculated, such as "INSUFFICIENT DATA". Used in conjunction with PPSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: External File Path
cdisc_notes: Filename for an external file, such as one for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. If multiple specimen types are used for a calculation (e.g., serum and urine for renal clearance), then this field should be left blank. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Examples: "RECTAL" for temperature, "ARM" for blood pressure.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "EIA" (enzyme immunoassay), "ELECTROPHORESIS", "DIPSTICK".

label: Analysis Method
cdisc_notes: Analysis method applied to obtain a summarized result. Analysis method describes the method of secondary processing applied to a complex observation result. Example: A named formula used to calculate AUC, such as "LIN-LOG TRAPEZOIDAL METHOD". \n Sponsor-defined formulas can also be represented by this variable. Example: Calculating ratio AUCs where the PPANMETH may be "DRUG METABOLITE 1 TO DRUG PARENT" or "DRUG METABOLITE 2 TO METABOLITE 1".

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "RADIOLOGIST".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than one assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted evaluation. Expected values can include "Y", "N", or null. This is not intended to be an analysis flag to indicate acceptability for a given analysis.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected.

label: Date/Time of Parameter Calculations
cdisc_notes: Nominal date/time of parameter calculations.

label: Study Day of Parameter Calculations
cdisc_notes: Study day of the collection, in integer days. The algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in the Demographics (DM) domain.

label: Study Day of End of Observation
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See --TPTNUM and --TPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned Elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (--TPTREF) such as "Previous Dose" or "Previous Meal". This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: The description of a time point that acts as a fixed reference for a series of planned time points.

label: Date/Time of Reference Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time of the reference time point from the PC records used to calculate a parameter record. The values in PPRFTDTC should be the same as that in PCRFTDTC for related records.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: Planned Start of Assessment Interval
cdisc_notes: The start of a planned evaluation or assessment interval relative to the time point reference.

label: Planned End of Assessment Interval
cdisc_notes: The end of a planned evaluation or assessment interval relative to the time point reference.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID, or UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. Example: preprinted line identifier on a CRF, record identifier defined in the sponsor's operational database.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Used to facilitate identification of relationships between records.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to facilitate identification of relationships between records.

label: Reported Name of Procedure
cdisc_notes: Name of procedure performed, either preprinted or collected on a CRF.

label: Modified Treatment Name
cdisc_notes: If the value for --TRT is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Standardized Procedure Name
cdisc_notes: Standardized or dictionary-derived name of PRTRT. If the codelist "PROCEDUR" is not used, the sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to map the terms in the external codelist element in the Define-XML document. If an intervention term does not have a decode value, then PRDECOD will be null.

label: Category
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of procedure values.

label: Subcategory
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of PRCAT values.

label: Pre-specified
cdisc_notes: Used when a specific procedure is pre-specified on a CRF. Values should be "Y" or null.

label: Occurrence
cdisc_notes: Used to record whether a prespecified procedure occurred when information about the occurrence of a specific procedure is solicited.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate when a question about the occurrence of a pre-specified intervention was not answered. Should be null or have a value of NOT DONE.

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with --STAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Indication
cdisc_notes: Denotes the indication for the procedure (e.g., why the procedure was performed).

label: Class
cdisc_notes: Class for a medication or treatment, often obtained from a coding dictionary.

label: Class Code
cdisc_notes: Used to represent code for --CLAS.

label: Dose
cdisc_notes: Amount of PRTRT administered. Not populated when PRDOSTXT is populated.

label: Dose Description
cdisc_notes: Dosing information collected in text form. Examples: "<1", "200-400". Not populated when PRDOSE is populated.

label: Dose Units
cdisc_notes: Units for PRDOSE, PRDOSTOT, or PRDOSTXT.

label: Dose Form
cdisc_notes: Dose form for PRTRT.

label: Dosing Frequency per Interval
cdisc_notes: Usually expressed as the number of doses given per a specific interval.

label: Total Daily Dose
cdisc_notes: Total daily dose of --TRT using the units in --DOSU. Used when dosing is collected as Total Daily Dose.

label: Intended Dose Regimen
cdisc_notes: Text description of the intended schedule or regimen for the procedure.

label: Route of Administration
cdisc_notes: Route of administration for PRTRT.

label: Location of Procedure
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of a procedure.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality.

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality.

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the procedure.

label: Start Date/Time of Procedure
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of the procedure represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: End Date/Time of Procedure
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the procedure represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Collection
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of Start of Procedure
cdisc_notes: Study day of start of procedure expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Procedure
cdisc_notes: Study day of end of procedure expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Duration of Procedure
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of a procedure represented in ISO 8601 character format. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived from start and end date/times.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a procedure should be performed. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See PRTPTNUM and PRTPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numerical version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time in ISO 8601 format relative to a planned fixed reference (PRTPTREF). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval, represented as ISO duration.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by PRELTM, PRTPTNUM, and PRTPT.

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by PRTRTREF in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Start Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: Start Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable PRSTTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: Start Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by PRSTRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-15", "VISIT 1".

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable PRENTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by PRENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25", "VISIT 2".

label: Body System or Organ Class Code
Other Specify, Loc. of Radiological Exam
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID, or UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Question number on a questionnaire.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Question Short Name
cdisc_notes: Topic variable for QS. Short name for the value in QSTEST, which can be used as a column name when converting the dataset from a vertical format to a horizontal format. The value in QSTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). QSTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. \n Controlled terminology for QSTESTCD is published in separate codelists for each questionnaire. See https://www.cdisc.org/standards/semantics/terminology for values for QSTESTCD. Examples: "ADCCMD01", "BPR0103".

label: Question Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the question or group of questions used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in QSTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. \n Controlled terminology for QSTEST is published in separate codelists for each questionnaire. See https://www.cdisc.org/standards/semantics/terminology for values for QSTEST. Example: "BPR01 - Emotional Withdrawal".

label: Category of Question
cdisc_notes: Used to specify the questionnaire in which the question identified by QSTEST and QSTESTCD was included. Examples: "ADAS-COG", "MDS-UPDRS".

label: Subcategory for Question
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of the questions within the category. Examples: "MENTAL HEALTH" , "DEPRESSION", "WORD RECALL".

label: Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Finding as originally received or collected (e.g., "RARELY", "SOMETIMES"). When sponsors apply codelist to indicate that code values are statistically meaningful standardized scores (which are defined by sponsors or by valid methodologies, e.g., SF36 questionnaires), QSORRES will contain the decode format; QSSTRESC and QSSTRESN may contain the standardized code values or scores.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for QSORRES, such as minutes or seconds or the units associated with a visual analog scale.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the finding for all questions or subscores copied or derived from QSORRES, in a standard format or standard units. QSSTRESC should store all findings in character format; if findings are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in QSSTRESN. If question scores are derived from the original finding, then the standard format is the score. Examples: "0", "1". \n When sponsors apply codelist to indicate the code values are statistically meaningful standardized scores (which are defined by sponsors or by valid methodologies, e.g., SF36 questionnaires), QSORRES will contain the decode format; QSSTRESC and QSSTRESN may contain the standardized code values or scores.

label: Numeric Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from QSSTRESC. QSSTRESN should store all numeric results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized unit used for QSSTRESC or QSSTRESN.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question was not done or was not answered. Should be null if a result exists in QSORRES.

label: Reason Not Performed
cdisc_notes: Describes why a question was not answered. Used in conjunction with QSSTAT when value is "NOT DONE". Example: "SUBJECT REFUSED".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Examples: "RECTAL" for temperature, "ARM" for blood pressure.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination.

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. Should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that QSBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record. The value should be "Y" or null. Records that represent the average of other records or questionnaire subscores that do not come from the CRF are examples of records that would be derived for the submission datasets. If QSDRVFL = "Y", then QSORRES may be null with QSSTRESC and (if numeric) QSSTRESN having the derived value.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Examples: "STUDY SUBJECT", "CAREGIVER", "INVESTIGATOR".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than one assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted evaluation. Expected values can include "Y", "N", or null. This is not intended to be an analysis flag to indicate acceptability for a given analysis.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the observation date/time of the physical exam finding.

label: Date/Time of Finding
cdisc_notes: Date of questionnaire.

label: Study Day of Finding
cdisc_notes: Study day of finding collection, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when questionnaire should be administered. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point (e.g., "TIME OF LAST DOSE"). See QSTPTNUM and QSTPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numerical version of QSTPT to aid in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time (in ISO 8601) relative to a planned fixed reference (QSTPTREF). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date time variable. Represented as an ISO 8601 duration. Examples: "-PT15M" to represent the period of 15 minutes prior to the reference point indicated by QSTPTREF, "PT8H" to represent the period of 8 hours after the reference point indicated by QSTPTREF.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Name of the fixed reference point referred to by QSELTM, QSTPTNUM, and QSTPT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time of the reference time point, QSTPTREF.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Evaluation interval associated with a QSTEST question represented in ISO 8601 character format. Example: "-P2Y" to represent an interval of 2 years in the question "Have you experienced any episodes in the past 2 years?".

label: Evaluation Interval Text
cdisc_notes: Evaluation interval associated with an observation, where the interval is not able to be represented in ISO 8601 format. Examples: "LIFETIME", "LAST NIGHT", "RECENTLY", "OVER THE LAST FEW WEEKS".

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sponsor Device Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier for a device.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject. May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external procedure identifier.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship.

label: Link Group
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship.

label: Short Name of Respiratory Test
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical format to a horizontal format. The value in RETESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). RETESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "FEV1", "FVC".

label: Name of Respiratory Test
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in RETEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Forced Expiratory Volume in 1 Second", "Forced Vital Capacity".

label: Modified Result Term
cdisc_notes: If the value of --ORRES is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Category for Respiratory Test
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize observations across subjects.

label: Subcategory for Respiratory Test
cdisc_notes: A further categorization.

label: Position of Subject During Observation
cdisc_notes: Position of the subject during a measurement or examination. Examples: "SUPINE", "STANDING", "SITTING".

label: Body System or Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Body System or Organ Class that is involved for a finding from the standard hierarchy for dictionary-coded results. Example: MedDRA SOC.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the procedure measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for REORRES and REORREF.

label: Reference Result in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Reference result for continuous measurements in original units. Should be collected only for continuous results.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from REORRES in a standard format or in standard units. RESTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in RESTRESN.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from RESTRESC. RESTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized unit used for RESTRESC, RESTRESN and RESTREFN.

label: Character Reference Result
cdisc_notes: Reference value for the result or finding copied or derived from --ORREF in a standard format.

label: Numeric Reference Result in Std Units
cdisc_notes: Reference result for continuous measurements in standard units. Should be populated only for continuous results.

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding. Example: "MALIGNANT" or "BENIGN" for tumor findings.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a test was not done or a measurement was not taken. Should be null if a result exists in REORRES.

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Describes why a measurement or test was not performed. Examples: "BROKEN EQUIPMENT", "SUBJECT REFUSED". Used in conjunction with RESTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: External File Path
cdisc_notes: Filename for an external file, such as one for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Laboratory/Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: Name or identifier of the vendor (e.g., laboratory) that provided the test results.

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE", "DNA", "RNA".

label: Anatomical Region
cdisc_notes: Defines the specific anatomical or biological region of a tissue, organ specimen or the region from which the specimen is obtained, as defined in the protocol, such as a section or part of what is described in the --SPEC variable. Examples: "CORTEX", "MEDULLA", "MUCOSA".

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: Defines the condition of the specimen. Example: "CLOUDY".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Examples: "LUNG", "BRONCHUS".

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Side of the body used to collect measurement. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method used to create the result.

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally-derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be Y or null. Note that REBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline flag for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Fasting Status
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify fasting status. Valid values include "Y", "N", "U", or null if not relevant.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record. Should be "Y" or null. Records that represent the average of other records, or that do not come from the CRF, or are not as originally collected or received are examples of records that would be derived for the submission datasets. If REDRVFL = "Y", then REORRES could be null, with RESTRESC and (if numeric) RESTRESN having the derived value.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "RADIOLOGIST".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in REEVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than one assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted evaluation. Expected values can include "Y", "N", or null. This is not intended to be an analysis flag to indicate acceptability for a given analysis.

label: Severity/Intensity
cdisc_notes: Describes the severity or intensity of a particular finding. Examples: "MILD", "MODERATE", "SEVERE".

label: Repetition Number
cdisc_notes: The instance number of a test that is repeated within a given time frame for the same test. The level of granularity can vary (e.g., within a time point, within a visit). Example: multiple measurements of pulmonary function.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the assessment was made.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Date/time of procedure or test.

label: End Date/Time of Observation
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the observation.

label: Study Day of Visit/Collection/Exam
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Test
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Collected Duration
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of an event, intervention, or finding. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point (e.g., "TIME OF LAST DOSE"). See RETPTNUM and RETPTREF. Examples: "START", "5 MINUTES POST".

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of RETPT to aid in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (RETPTREF). Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval, represented as ISO duration. Examples: "-PT15M" to represent 15 minutes prior to the reference time point indicated by RETPTREF, "PT8H" to represent 8 hours after the reference time point represented by RETPTREF.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by REELTM, RETPTNUM, and RETPT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by RETPTREF.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable --ENTPT.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 or other character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by --ENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25" or "VISIT 2".

label: Inadequate Results Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a result is not considered acceptable/adequate according to the assessment protocol. The value should be Y or null. If additional qualification of the result is collected then this could be recorded in a Supplemental Qualifier variable.

label: Last Asthma Maintenance Medic. Date Time
Last COPD Maintenance Medic. Date Time
Oxygen Treatment Prior to Study Start
QC Status for the Image Analysis
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Related Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Abbreviation for the domain of the parent record(s).

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Identifying Variable
cdisc_notes: Name of the identifying variable in the general-observation-class dataset that identifies the related record(s). Examples: --SEQ, --GRPID.

label: Identifying Variable Value
cdisc_notes: Value of identifying variable described in IDVAR. If --SEQ is the variable being used to describe this record, then the value of --SEQ would be entered here.

label: Relationship Type
cdisc_notes: Identifies the hierarchical level of the records in the relationship. Values should be either "ONE" or "MANY". Used only when identifying a relationship between datasets (as described in Section 8.3, Relating Datasets).

label: Relationship Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique value within USUBJID that identifies the relationship. All records for the same USUBJID that have the same RELID are considered related/associated. RELID can be any value the sponsor chooses, and is only meaningful within the RELREC dataset to identify the related/associated domain records.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Specimen ID
cdisc_notes: Specimen identifier, unique within USUBJID.

label: Specimen Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE", "SOFT TISSUE".

label: Specimen Parent
cdisc_notes: Identifies the REFID of the parent of a specimen to support tracking its genealogy.

label: Specimen Level
cdisc_notes: Identifies the generation number of the sample where the collected sample is considered the first generation.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product. Either USUBJID or POOLID must be populated.

label: Pool Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to identify a pool of subjects. If POOLID is entered, POOLDEF records must exist for each subject in the pool and USUBJID must be null. Either USUBJID or POOLID must be populated.

label: Related Subject or Pool Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to identify a related subject or pool of subjects. RSUBJID will be populated with either the USUBJID of the related subject or the POOLID of the related pool.

label: Subject Relationship
cdisc_notes: Describes the relationship of the subject identified in USUBJID or the pool identified in POOLID to the subject or pool identified in RSUBJID.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject (or within a parameter, in the case of the Trial Summary domain). May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain. Also used to link together a block of related records in the Trial Summary dataset.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier (e.g., lab specimen ID, UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image).

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. Example: Preprinted line identifier on a CRF.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship.

label: Short Name of Reproductive Test
cdisc_notes: Short character value for RPTEST used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical format to a horizontal format. The short value can be up to 8 characters. Examples: "CHILDPOT", "BCMETHOD", "MENARAGE".

label: Name of Reproductive Test
cdisc_notes: Long name For RPTESTCD. Examples: "Childbearing Potential", "Birth Control Method", "Menarche Age".

label: Object of the Observation
cdisc_notes: Used in domains modeled as Findings About Events or Findings About Interventions. Describes the event or intervention whose property is being measured in --TESTCD/--TEST. Example: an event of vomiting which has findings, where --OBJ = "VOMIT" and the volume of VOMIT is being measured where --TESTCD = "VOLUME".

label: Modified Result Term
cdisc_notes: If the value of --ORRES is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Category for Reproductive Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Example: "No use case to date, but values would be relative to reproduction tests grouping".

label: Subcategory for Reproductive Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of RPCAT values. Example: "No use case to date, but values would be relative to reproduction tests grouping".

label: Position of Subject During Observation
cdisc_notes: Position of the subject during a measurement or examination. Examples: "SUPINE", "STANDING", "SITTING".

label: Body System or Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Body System or Organ Class that is involved for a finding from the standard hierarchy for dictionary-coded results. Example: MedDRA SOC.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected. Examples: "120", "<1", "POS".

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Unit for RPORRES. Examples: "in", "LB", "kg/L".

label: Normal Range Lower Limit-Original Units
cdisc_notes: Lower end of normal range or reference range for results stored in --ORRES.

label: Normal Range Upper Limit-Original Units
cdisc_notes: Upper end of normal range or reference range for results stored in --ORRES.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings copied or derived from RPORRES, in a standard format or in standard units. RPSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in RPSTRESN. For example, if various tests have results "NONE", "NEG", and "NEGATIVE" in RPORRES, and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in RPSTRESC as "NEGATIVE".

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from RPSTRESC. RPSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for RPSTRESC and RPSTRESN. Example: "mol/L".

label: Normal Range Lower Limit-Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Lower end of normal range or reference range for standardized results (e.g., --STRESC, --STRESN) represented in standardized units (--STRESU).

label: Normal Range Upper Limit-Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Upper end of normal range or reference range for standardized results (e.g., --STRESC, --STRESN) represented in standardized units (--STRESU).

label: Normal Range for Character Results
cdisc_notes: Normal range or reference range for results stored in --STRESC that are character in ordinal or categorical scale. Example: "Negative to Trace".

label: Normal/Reference Range Indicator
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate the value is outside the normal range or reference range. May be defined by --ORNRLO and --ORNRHI or other objective criteria. Examples: "Y", "N"; "HIGH", "LOW"; "NORMAL", "ABNORMAL".

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding. Example: "MALIGNANT" or "BENIGN" for tumor findings.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question was not asked or a test was not done, or a test was attempted but did not generate a result. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with RPSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: External File Path
cdisc_notes: Filename for an external file, such as one for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Laboratory/Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: Name or identifier of the vendor (e.g., laboratory) that provided the test results.

label: LOINC Code
cdisc_notes: Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes (LOINC) code for the topic variable such as a lab test.

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE", "DNA", "RNA".

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: Defines the condition of the specimen. Example: "CLOUDY".

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Examples: "RECTAL" for temperature, "ARM" for blood pressure.

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "EIA" (enzyme immunoassay), "ELECTROPHORESIS", "DIPSTICK".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that RPBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Fasting Status
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify fasting status. Valid values include "Y", "N", "U", or null if not relevant.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record. The value should be "Y" or null. Records which represent the average of other records or which do not come from the CRF are examples of records that would be derived for the submission datasets. If RPDRVFL = "Y", then RPORRES may be null, with RPSTRESC and (if numeric) RPSTRESN having the derived value.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "RADIOLOGIST".

label: Toxicity
cdisc_notes: Description of toxicity quantified by --TOXGR such as NCI CTCAE Short Name. Examples: "HYPERCALCEMIA", "HYPOCALCEMIA". Sponsor should specify which scale and version is used in the Sponsor Comments column of the Define-XML document.

label: Toxicity Grade
cdisc_notes: Records toxicity grade using a standard toxicity scale (such as the NCI CTCAE). Sponsor should specify which scale and version is used in the Sponsor Comments column of the Define data definition document. Example: 2.

label: Severity/Intensity
cdisc_notes: Describes the severity or intensity of a particular finding. Examples: "MILD", "MODERATE", "SEVERE".

label: Lower Limit of Quantitation
cdisc_notes: Indicates the lower limit of quantitation for an assay. Units will be those used for --STRESU.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the assessment was made.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation.

label: Start Date/Time of Observation
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of an observation.

label: End Date/Time of Observation
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the observation.

label: Study Day of Visit/Collection/Exam
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of Start of Observation
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of start of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Observation
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Duration
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of an event, intervention, or finding represented in ISO 8601 character format. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time in ISO 8601 character format relative to a planned fixed reference (RPTPTREF; e.g., "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL"). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval, represented as ISO duration.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by RPELTM, RPTPTNUM, and RPTPT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by RPTPTREF in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Start Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the observation as being before, during, or after the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics.

label: End Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before, during or after the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: Start Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable --STTPT.

label: Start Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 or other character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by --STRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-15" or "VISIT 1".

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable --ENTPT.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 or other character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by --ENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25" or "VISIT 2".

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness within a dataset for a subject. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external identifier.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: An identifier used to link the response assessment to the related measurement record in another domain which was used to determine the response result. LNKID values group records within USUBJID.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: A grouping identifier used to link the response assessment to a group of measurement/assessment records which were used in the assessment of the response. LNKGRP values group records within USUBJID.

label: Assessment Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the TEST in RSTEST. The value in RSTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). RSTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "TRGRESP", "NTRGRESP", "OVRLRESP", "SYMPTDTR", "CPS0102". \n There are separate codelists used for RSTESTCD where the choice depends on the value of RSCAT. Codelist "ONCRTSCD" is used for oncology response criteria (when RSCAT is a term in codelist "ONCRSCAT"). Examples: TRGRESP, "NTRGRESP, "OVRLRESP". For Clinical Classifications (when RSCAT is a term in codelist "CCCAT"), QRS Naming Rules apply. These instruments have individual dedicated terminology codelists.

label: Assessment Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the response assessment. The value in RSTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. \n There are separate codelists used for RSTEST where the choice depends on the value of RSCAT. Codelist "ONCRTS" is used for oncology response criteria (when RSCAT is a term in codelist "ONCRSCAT"). Examples: "Target Response", "Non-target Response", "Overall Response", "Symptomatic Deterioration". For Clinical Classifications (when RSCAT is a term in codelist "CCCAT"), QRS Naming Rules apply. These instruments have individual dedicated terminology codelists.

label: Modified Result Term
cdisc_notes: If the value of --ORRES is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Category for Assessment
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records across subjects. Examples: "RECIST 1.1", "CHILD-PUGH CLASSIFICATION". There are separate codelists used for RSCAT where the choice depends on whether the related records are about an oncology response criterion or another clinical classification. \n RSCAT is required for clinical classifications other than oncology response criteria.

label: Subcategory
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of RSCAT values.

label: Position of Subject During Observation
cdisc_notes: Position of the subject during a measurement or examination. Examples: "SUPINE", "STANDING", "SITTING".

label: Body System or Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Body System or Organ Class that is involved for a finding from the standard hierarchy for dictionary-coded results. Example: MedDRA SOC.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the response assessment as originally received, collected, or calculated.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Unit for RSORRES.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for the response assessment, copied, or derived from RSORRES in a standard format or standard units. RSSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format. \n For Clinical Classifications, this may be a score.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from --STRESC. --STRESN should store all numeric test results or findings. For Clinical Classifications, this may be a score.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for RSSTRESC and RSSTRESN.

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding. Example: "MALIGNANT" or "BENIGN" for tumor findings.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate the response assessment was not performed. Should be null if a result exists in RSORRES.

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Describes why a response assessment was not performed. Examples: "All target tumors not evaluated", "Subject does not have non-target tumors". Used in conjunction with RSSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: External File Path
cdisc_notes: Filename for an external file, such as one for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: The name or identifier of the vendor that performed the response assessment. This column can be left null when the investigator provides the complete set of data in the domain.

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE", "DNA", "RNA".

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: Defines the condition of the specimen. Example: "CLOUDY".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Examples: "RECTAL" for temperature, "ARM" for blood pressure.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination.

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null. \n When a clinical classification is assessed at multiple times, including baseline, RSLOBXFL should be included in the dataset.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that --BLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Fasting Status
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify fasting status. Valid values include "Y", "N", "U", or null if not relevant.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record (e.g., a record that represents the average of other records such as a computed baseline). Should be "Y" or null.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "RADIOLOGIST". \n RSEVAL is expected for oncology response criteria. It can be left null when the investigator provides the complete set of data in the domain. However, the column should contain no null values when data from one or more independent assessors is included, meaning that the rows attributed to the investigator should contain a value of "INVESTIGATOR".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in RSEVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2". See assumptions in Section 6.3.9.3.1, Disease Response Use Case.

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than 1 independent assessor (e.g., "RADIOLOGIST 1", "RADIOLOGIST 2", "ADJUDICATOR") provides an evaluation of response, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted evaluation.

label: Severity/Intensity
cdisc_notes: Describes the severity or intensity of a particular finding. Examples: "MILD", "MODERATE", "SEVERE".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the assessment was made.

label: Date/Time of Assessment
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of the assessment represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Assessment
cdisc_notes: Study day of the assessment, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics.

label: Collected Duration
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of an event, intervention, or finding. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See RSTPTNUM and RSTPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time in ISO 8601 character format relative to a planned fixed reference (RSTPTREF; e.g., "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL"). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval, represented as ISO duration.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by RSELTM, RSTPTNUM, and RSTPT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by RSTPTREF in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding RSTESTCD, represented in ISO 8601 character format. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval.

label: Evaluation Interval Text
cdisc_notes: Evaluation interval associated with an observation, where the interval is not able to be represented in ISO 8601 format. Examples: "LIFETIME", "LAST NIGHT", "RECENTLY", "OVER THE LAST FEW WEEKS".

label: Start Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable RSSTTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: Start Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by RSSTRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-15", "VISIT 1".

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable RSENTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by RSENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25", "VISIT 2".

label: Inv Opinion Agrees with RECIST Response

Lugano Reason PET/PET-CT Resp NE, Other

RECIL Reason CT/MRI Resp NE, Other

Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Subject Characteristic Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in SCTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in SCTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). SCTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "MARISTAT", "NATORIG".

label: Subject Characteristic
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in SCTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Marital Status", "National Origin".

label: Category for Subject Characteristic
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records.

label: Subcategory for Subject Characteristic
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of the subject characteristic.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the subject characteristic as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original unit in which the data were collected. The unit for SCORRES.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings copied or derived from SCORRES, in a standard format or standard units. SCSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in SCSTRESN. For example, if a test has results "NONE", "NEG", and "NEGATIVE" in SCORRES, and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in SCSTRESC as "NEGATIVE".

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from SCSTRESC. SCSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized unit used for SCSTRESC or SCSTRESN.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that the measurement was not done. Should be null if a result exists in SCORRES.

label: Reason Not Performed
cdisc_notes: Describes why the observation has no result. Example: "Subject refused". Used in conjunction with SCSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Laboratory/Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: Name or identifier of the vendor (e.g., laboratory) that provided the test results.

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE", "DNA", "RNA".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "EIA" (enzyme immunoassay), "ELECTROPHORESIS", "DIPSTICK".

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than one assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted evaluation. Expected values can include "Y", "N", or null. This is not intended to be an analysis flag to indicate acceptability for a given analysis.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time at which the assessment was made.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of the subject characteristic represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Examination
cdisc_notes: Study day of collection, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. Should be assigned to be consistent chronological order.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain. Also used to link together a block of related records in the Trial Summary Information (Section 3.3).

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Element Code
cdisc_notes: 1. ETCD (the companion to ELEMENT) is limited to 8 characters and does not have special character restrictions. These values should be short for ease of use in programming, but it is not expected that ETCD will need to serve as a variable name. \n 2. If an encountered element differs from the planned element to the point that it is considered a new element, then use "UNPLAN" as the value for ETCD to represent this element.

label: Description of Element
cdisc_notes: The name of the element. If ETCD has a value of "UNPLAN", then ELEMENT should be null.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the subject's assigned trial arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the element in the planned sequence of elements for the arm to which the subject was assigned.

label: Start Date/Time of Element
cdisc_notes: Start date/time for an element for each subject.

label: End Date/Time of Element
cdisc_notes: End date/time for an element for each subject.

label: Description of Unplanned Element
cdisc_notes: Description of what happened to the subject during an unplanned element. Used only if ETCD has the value of "UNPLAN".

label: Study Day of Start of Element
cdisc_notes: Study day of start of element relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Day of End of Element
cdisc_notes: Study day of end of element relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of subject records. Should be assigned to be consistent chronological order.

label: Disease Milestone Instance Name
cdisc_notes: Name of the specific disease milestone. For types of disease milestones that can occur multiple times, the name will end with a sequence number. Example: "HYPO1".

label: Disease Milestone Type
cdisc_notes: The type of disease milestone. Example: "HYPOGLYCEMIC EVENT".

label: Start Date/Time of Milestone
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of milestone instance (if milestone is an intervention or event) or date of milestone (if Milestone is a finding).

label: End Date/Time of Milestone
cdisc_notes: End date/time of disease milestone instance.

label: Study Day of Start of Milestone
cdisc_notes: Study day of start of disease milestone instance, relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Day of End of Milestone
cdisc_notes: Study day of end of disease milestone instance, relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external specimen identifier. Example: "Specimen ID".

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Skin Response Test or Exam Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in SRTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in SRTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). SRTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores.

label: Skin Response Test or Examination Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in SRTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Example: "Wheal Diameter".

label: Object of the Observation
cdisc_notes: Used to describe the object or focal point of the findings observation that is represented by --TEST. Examples: the dose of the immunogenic material or the allergen associated with the response (e.g., "Johnson Grass IgE 0.15 BAU mL").

label: Category for Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values across subjects.

label: Subcategory for Test
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of SRCAT values.

label: Results or Findings in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Results of measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for SRORRES. Example: "mm".

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings copied or derived from SRORRES, in a standard format or in standard units. SRSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in SRSTRESN.

label: Numeric Results/Findings in Std. Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from SRSTRESC. SRSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for SRSTRESC and SRSTRESN. Example: "mm".

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate exam not done. Should be null if a result exists in SRORRES.

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Describes why a measurement or test was not performed. Used in conjunction with SRSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: Name or identifier of the laboratory or vendor who provided the test results.

label: Specimen Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the types of specimen used for a measurement. Example: "SKIN".

label: Location Used for Measurement
cdisc_notes: Location relevant to the collection of the measurement.

label: Laterality
