cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing laterality of intervention administration. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of test or examination. Examples: "ELISA", "EIA", "MICRONEUTRALIZATION ASSAY", "PLAQUE REDUCTION NEUTRALIZATION ASSAY".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. The value should be "Y" or null. Note that SRBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline flag for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of person who provided evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Should be null for records that contain collected or derived data. Examples: "INVESTIGATOR", "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "VENDOR".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time of the observation. Examples: "SCREENING", "TREATMENT", and "FOLLOW-UP".

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation represented in ISO 8601.

label: Study Day of Visit/Collection/Exam
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to sponsor- defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when measurement should be taken. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See SRTPTNUM and SRTPTREF. Examples: "START", "5 MIN POST".

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numerical version of SRTPT to aid in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time (in ISO 8601) relative to a fixed time point reference (SRTPTREF). Not a clock time or a date time variable. Represented as an ISO 8601 duration. Examples: "-PT15M" to represent the period of 15 minutes prior to the reference point indicated by EGTPTREF, "PT8H" to represent the period of 8 hours after the reference point indicated by SRTPTREF.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Name of the fixed reference point referred to by SRELTM, SRTPTNUM, and SRTPT. Example: "INTRADERMAL INJECTION".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time of the reference time point, SRTPTREF.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number from the Procedure or Test page.

label: Status Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the status assessment described in SSTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in SSTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). SSTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Example: "SURVSTAT".

label: Status Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the status assessment used to obtain the finding. The value in SSTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Example: "Survival Status".

label: Category for Assessment
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize observations across subjects.

label: Subcategory for Assessment
cdisc_notes: A further categorization.

label: Result or Finding Original Result
cdisc_notes: Result of the status assessment finding as originally received or collected.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings copied or derived from SSORRES, in a standard format.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a status assessment was not done. Should be null if a result exists in SSORRES.

label: Reason Assessment Not Performed
cdisc_notes: Describes why an assessment was not performed. Example: "Subject refused". Used in conjunction with SSSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Should be null for records that contain collected or derived data. Examples: "CAREGIVER", "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "FRIEND".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the subject status assessment.

label: Date/Time of Assessment
cdisc_notes: Date and time of the subject status assessment represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Assessment
cdisc_notes: Study day of the subject status assessment, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics.

label: Reason For Not Searching Other, Specify
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number on a Tobacco & Alcohol Use CRF page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Reported Name of Substance
cdisc_notes: Substance name. Examples: "CIGARETTES", "COFFEE".

label: Modified Substance Name
cdisc_notes: If SUTRT is modified, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Standardized Substance Name
cdisc_notes: Standardized or dictionary-derived text description of SUTRT or SUMODIFY if the sponsor chooses to code the substance use. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to map the terms utilizing the external codelist element in the Define-XML document.

label: Category for Substance Use
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records. Examples: "TOBACCO", "ALCOHOL", or "CAFFEINE".

label: Subcategory for Substance Use
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of substance use. Examples: "CIGARS", "CIGARETTES", "BEER", "WINE".

label: SU Pre-Specified
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate whether ("Y"/null) information about the use of a specific substance was solicited on the CRF.

label: SU Occurrence
cdisc_notes: When the use of specific substances is solicited, SUOCCUR is used to indicate whether ("Y"/"N") a particular prespecified substance was used. Values are null for substances not specifically solicited.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: When the use of prespecified substances is solicited, the completion status indicates that there was no response to the question about the prespecified substance. When there is no prespecified list on the CRF, then the completion status indicates that substance use was not assessed for the subject.

label: Reason Substance Use Not Collected
cdisc_notes: Describes the reason substance use was not collected. Used in conjunction with SUSTAT when value of SUSTAT is "NOT DONE".

label: Substance Use Class
cdisc_notes: Substance use class. May be obtained from coding. When coding to a single class, populate with class value. If using a dictionary and coding to multiple classes, then follow Section 4.2.8.3, Multiple Values for a Non-result Qualifier Variable, or omit SUCLAS.

label: Substance Use Class Code
cdisc_notes: Code corresponding to SUCLAS. May be obtained from coding.

label: Substance Use Consumption
cdisc_notes: Amount of SUTRT consumed. Not populated if SUDOSTXT is populated.

label: Substance Use Consumption Text
cdisc_notes: Substance use consumption amounts or a range of consumption information collected in text form. Not populated if SUDOSE is populated.

label: Consumption Units
cdisc_notes: Units for SUDOSE, SUDOSTOT, or SUDOSTXT. Examples: "oz", "CIGARETTE", "PACK", "g".

label: Dose Form
cdisc_notes: Dose form for SUTRT. Examples: "INJECTABLE", "LIQUID", "POWDER".

label: Use Frequency Per Interval
cdisc_notes: Usually expressed as the number of repeated administrations of SUDOSE within a specific time period. Example: "Q24H" (every day).

label: Total Daily Consumption
cdisc_notes: Total daily use of SUTRT using the units in SUDOSU. Used when dosing is collected as total daily dose. If a sponsor needs to aggregate the data over a period other than daily, then the aggregated total could be recorded in a supplemental qualifier variable.

label: Route of Administration
cdisc_notes: Route of administration for SUTRT. Examples: "ORAL", "INTRAVENOUS".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the substance use started. Null for substances that started before study participation.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time of the substance use. Null for substances that started before study participation.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation.

label: Start Date/Time of Substance Use
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of the substance use represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: End Date/Time of Substance Use
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the substance use represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Collection
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of Start of Substance Use
cdisc_notes: Study day of start of substance use relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Study Day of End of Substance Use
cdisc_notes: Study day of end of substance use relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC.

label: Duration of Substance Use
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of substance use in ISO 8601 format. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived from start and end date/times.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See --TPTNUM and --TPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Start Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Describes the start of the substance use relative to the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point (represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics). If information such as "PRIOR" was collected, this information may be translated into SUSTRF. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Describes the end of the substance use with relative to the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point (represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics). If information such as "PRIOR", "ONGOING", or "CONTINUING" was collected, this information may be translated into SUENRF. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: Start Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the substance as being before or after the reference time point defined by variable SUSTTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7 , Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: Start Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the reference point referred to by SUSTRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-15", "VISIT 1".

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the substance as being before or after the reference time point defined by variable SUENTPT. \n Not all values of the codelist are allowable for this variable. See Section 4.4.7 , Use of Relative Timing Variables.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the reference point referred to by SUENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25", "VISIT 2".

label: Most Recent Substance Intake Date Time
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Study identifier of the parent record(s).

label: Related Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain of the parent record(s).

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product. This is the value of USUBJID in the parent record(s).

label: Identifying Variable
cdisc_notes: Identifying variable in the dataset that identifies the related record(s). Examples: --SEQ, --GRPID.

label: Identifying Variable Value
cdisc_notes: Value of identifying variable of the parent record(s).

label: Qualifier Variable Name
cdisc_notes: The short name of the qualifier variable, which is used as a column name in a domain view with data from the parent domain. The value in QNAM cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). QNAM cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. This will often be the column name in the sponsor's operational dataset.

label: Qualifier Variable Label
cdisc_notes: This is the long name or label associated with QNAM. The value in QLABEL cannot be longer than 40 characters. This will often be the column label in the sponsor's original dataset.

label: Data Value
cdisc_notes: Result of, response to, or value associated with QNAM. A value for this column is required; no records can be in SUPP-- with a null value for QVAL.

label: Origin
cdisc_notes: Because QVAL can represent a mixture of collected (on a CRF), derived, or assigned items, QORIG is used to indicate the origin of this data. Examples: "CRF", "Assigned", "Derived". See Section 4.1.8, Origin Metadata.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Should be null for records that contain objectively collected or derived data. Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "STATISTICIAN", "DATABASE ADMINISTRATOR", "CLINICAL COORDINATOR".

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain most relevant to the observation. The domain abbreviation is also used as a prefix for variables to ensure uniqueness when datasets are merged.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject (or within a parameter, in the case of the Trial Summary domain). May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Should be an integer.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date or start date and time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected (see Section Trial Arms).

label: Start Date/Time of Observation
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of an observation represented in IS0 8601 character format.

label: End Date/Time of Observation
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the observation represented in IS0 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Start of Observation
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of start of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Observation
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Pre-specified
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate whether the visit was planned (i.e., visits specified in the TV domain). Value is "Y" for planned visits, null for unplanned visits.

label: Contact Mode
cdisc_notes: The way in which the visit was conducted. Examples: "IN PERSON", "TELEPHONE CALL", "IVRS".

label: Occurrence
cdisc_notes: Used to record whether a planned visit occurred. The value is null for unplanned visits.

label: Reason for Occur Value
cdisc_notes: The reason for the value in SVOCCUR. If SVOCCUR="N", SVREASOC is the reason the visit did not occur.

label: Description of Unplanned Visit
cdisc_notes: Description of what happened to the subject during an unplanned visit. Only populated for unplanned visits.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Planned Arm Code
cdisc_notes: ARMCD is limited to 20 characters and does not have special character restrictions. The maximum length of ARMCD is longer than that for other "short" variables to accommodate the kind of values that are likely to be needed for crossover trials. For example, if ARMCD values for a 7-period crossover were constructed using 2-character abbreviations for each treatment and separating hyphens, the length of ARMCD values would be 20.

label: Description of Planned Arm
cdisc_notes: Name given to an arm or treatment group.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the order of the element within the arm.

label: Element Code
cdisc_notes: ETCD (the companion to ELEMENT) is limited to 8 characters and does not have special character restrictions. These values should be short for ease of use in programming, but it is not expected that ETCD will need to serve as a variable name.

label: Description of Element
cdisc_notes: The name of the element. The same element may occur more than once within an arm.

label: Branch
cdisc_notes: Condition subject met, at a "branch" in the trial design at the end of this element, to be included in this arm (e.g., "Randomization to DRUG X").

label: Transition Rule
cdisc_notes: If the trial design allows a subject to transition to an element other than the next element in sequence, then the conditions for transitioning to those other elements, and the alternative element sequences, are specified in this rule (e.g., "Responders go to washout").

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Name of the trial epoch with which this element of the arm is associated.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Planned Arm Code
cdisc_notes: ARMCD is limited to 20 characters and does not have special character restrictions. The maximum length of ARMCD is longer than that for other "short" variables to accommodate the kind of values that are likely to be needed for crossover trials. For example, if ARMCD values for a seven-period crossover were constructed using two-character abbreviations for each treatment and separating hyphens, the length of ARMCD values would be 20.

label: Description of Planned Arm
cdisc_notes: Name given to an Arm or treatment group.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the order of the Element within the Arm.

label: Treatment Period
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the order of the Element within the Arm.

label: Element Code
cdisc_notes: ETCD (the companion to ELEMENT) is limited to 8 characters and does not have special character restrictions. These values should be short for ease of use in programming, but it is not expected that ETCD will need to serve as a variable name.

label: Treatment Code
cdisc_notes: ETCD (the companion to ELEMENT) is limited to 8 characters and does not have special character restrictions. These values should be short for ease of use in programming, but it is not expected that ETCD will need to serve as a variable name.

label: Description of Element
cdisc_notes: The name of the Element. The same Element may occur more than once within an Arm.

label: Branch
cdisc_notes: Condition subject met, at a "branch" in the trial design at the end of this Element, to be included in this Arm (e.g., "Randomization to DRUG X").

label: Transition Rule
cdisc_notes: If the trial design allows a subject to transition to an Element other than the next Element in sequence, then the conditions for transitioning to those other Elements, and the alternative Element sequences, are specified in this rule (e.g., "Responders go to washout").

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Name of the Trial Epoch with which this Element of the Arm is associated.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Sequence of Planned Assessment Schedule
cdisc_notes: A number given to ensure ordinal sequencing of the planned assessment schedules within a trial.

label: Anchor Variable Name
cdisc_notes: A reference to the date variable name that provides the start point from which the planned disease assessment schedule is measured. This must be a referenced from the ADaM ADSL dataset (e.g., "ANCH1DT"). Note: TDANCVAR will contain the name of a reference date variable.

label: Offset from the Anchor
cdisc_notes: A fixed offset from the date provided by the variable referenced in TDANCVAR. This is used when the timing of planned cycles does not start on the exact day referenced in the variable indicated in TDANCVAR. The value of this variable will be either zero or a positive value and will be represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Planned Assessment Interval
cdisc_notes: The planned interval between disease assessments represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Planned Assessment Interval Minimum
cdisc_notes: The lower limit of the allowed range for the planned interval between disease assessments represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Planned Assessment Interval Maximum
cdisc_notes: The upper limit of the allowed range for the planned interval between disease assessments represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Maximum Number of Actual Assessments
cdisc_notes: This variable must represent the maximum number of actual assessments for the analysis that this disease assessment schedule describes. In a trial where the maximum number of assessments is not defined explicitly in the protocol (e.g., assessments occur until death), TDNUMRPT should represent the maximum number of disease assessments that support the efficacy analysis encountered by any subject across the trial at that point in time.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Element Code
cdisc_notes: ETCD (the companion to ELEMENT) is limited to 8 characters and does not have special character restrictions. These values should be short for ease of use in programming, but it is not expected that ETCD will need to serve as a variable name.

label: Description of Element
cdisc_notes: The name of the element.

label: Rule for Start of Element
cdisc_notes: Describes condition for beginning element.

label: Rule for End of Element
cdisc_notes: Describes condition for ending element. Either TEENRL or TEDUR must be present for each element.

label: Planned Duration of Element
cdisc_notes: Planned duration of element in ISO 8601 format. Used when the rule for ending the element is applied after a fixed duration.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Incl/Excl Criterion Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name IETEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in IETESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). IETESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. The prefix "IE" is used to ensure consistency with the IE domain.

label: Inclusion/Exclusion Criterion
cdisc_notes: Full text of the inclusion or exclusion criterion. The prefix "IE" is used to ensure consistency with the IE domain.

label: Inclusion/Exclusion Category
cdisc_notes: Used for categorization of the inclusion or exclusion criteria.

label: Inclusion/Exclusion Subcategory
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of the exception criterion. Can be used to distinguish criteria for a sub-study or to categorize as major or minor exceptions. Examples: "MAJOR", "MINOR".

label: Inclusion/Exclusion Criterion Rule
cdisc_notes: Rule that expresses the criterion in computer-executable form. See Assumption 4.

label: Protocol Criteria Versions
cdisc_notes: The number of this version of the Inclusion/Exclusion criteria. May be omitted if there is only 1 version.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain, which must be TM.

label: Disease Milestone Type
cdisc_notes: The type of disease milestone. Example: "HYPOGLYCEMIC EVENT".

label: Disease Milestone Definition
cdisc_notes: Definition of the disease milestone.

label: Disease Milestone Repetition Indicator
cdisc_notes: Indicates whether this is a disease milestone that can occur only once ("N") or a type of disease milestone that can occur multiple times ("Y").

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness within a dataset for a subject. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external identifier.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link the assessment result records to the individual tumor/lesion identification record in TU domain.

label: Link Group
cdisc_notes: Used to group and link all of the measurement/assessment records used in the assessment of the response record in the RS domain.

label: Tumor/Lesion Assessment Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the TEST in TRTEST. TRTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "TUMSTATE", "DIAMETER", "LESSCIND", "LESRVIND". See assumption 3.

label: Tumor/Lesion Assessment Test Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in TRTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Tumor State", "Diameter", "Volume", "Lesion Success Indicator", "Lesion Revascularization Indicator". See assumption 3.

label: Modified Result Term
cdisc_notes: If the value of --ORRES is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Category
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Examples: "HEMATOLOGY", "URINALYSIS", "CHEMISTRY", "HAMD 17", "SF36 V2.0 ACUTE", "EGFR MUTATION ANALYSIS".

label: Subcategory
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of --CAT values. Example: "WBC DIFFERENTIAL".

label: Position of Subject During Observation
cdisc_notes: Position of the subject during a measurement or examination. Examples: "SUPINE", "STANDING", "SITTING".

label: Body System or Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Body System or Organ Class that is involved for a finding from the standard hierarchy for dictionary-coded results. Example: MedDRA SOC.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the tumor/lesion measurement/assessment as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for TRORRES. Example: "mm".

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings copied or derived from TRORRES, in a standard format or standard units. TRSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in TRSTRESN.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from TRSTRESC. TRSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized unit used for TRSTRESN.

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding. Example: "MALIGNANT" or "BENIGN" for tumor findings.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a scan/image/physical exam was not performed or a tumor/lesion measurement was not taken. Should be null if a result exists in TRORRES.

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Describes why a scan/image/physical exam was not performed or a tumor/lesion measurement was not taken. Examples: "SCAN NOT PERFORMED", "NOT ASSESSABLE: IMAGE OBSCURED TUMOR". Used in conjunction with TRSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: External File Path
cdisc_notes: Filename for an external file, such as one for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Laboratory/Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: The name or identifier of the vendor that performed the tumor/lesion measurement or assessment. This column can be left null when the investigator provides the complete set of data in the domain.

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE", "DNA", "RNA".

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: Defines the condition of the specimen. Example: "CLOUDY".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Examples: "RECTAL" for temperature, "ARM" for blood pressure.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Method Used to Identify the Tumor/Lesion
cdisc_notes: Method used to measure the tumor/lesion/location of interest. Examples: "MRI", "CT SCAN", "PET SCAN", "Coronary angiography".

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally-derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. Should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that TRBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline flag for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Fasting Status
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify fasting status. Valid values include "Y", "N", "U", or null if not relevant.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record (e.g., a record that represents the average of other records such as a computed baseline). Should be "Y" or null.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in TREVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2". See assumption 6.

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than 1 independent assessor (e.g., "RADIOLOGIST 1", "RADIOLOGIST 2", "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE") provide independent assessments at the same time point, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted assessment.

label: Severity/Intensity
cdisc_notes: Describes the severity or intensity of a particular finding. Examples: "MILD", "MODERATE", "SEVERE".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the element in the planned sequence of elements for the arm to which the subject was assigned.

label: Date/Time of Tumor/Lesion Measurement
cdisc_notes: The date of the scan/image/physical exam. TRDTC does not represent the date that the image was read to identify tumors/lesions. TRDTC also does not represent the VISIT date.

label: Study Day of Tumor/Lesion Measurement
cdisc_notes: Study day of the scan/image/physical exam, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics.

label: Collected Duration
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of an event, intervention, or finding. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See --TPTNUM and --TPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned Elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (--TPTREF) such as "Previous Dose" or "Previous Meal". This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by --ELTM, --TPTNUM, --TPT, --STINT, and --ENINT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by --TPTREF.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable --ENTPT.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 or other character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by --ENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25" or "VISIT 2".

label: Number of Lesions in Measurement, Split
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness within a parameter. Allows inclusion of multiple records for the same TSPARMCD.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a group of related records.

label: Trial Summary Parameter Short Name
cdisc_notes: TSPARMCD (the companion to TSPARM) is limited to 8 characters and does not have special character restrictions. These values should be short for ease of use in programming, but it is not expected that TSPARMCD will need to serve as variable names. Examples: "AGEMIN", "AGEMAX".

label: Trial Summary Parameter
cdisc_notes: Term for the trial summary parameter. The value in TSPARM cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Planned Minimum Age of Subjects", "Planned Maximum Age of Subjects".

label: Parameter Value
cdisc_notes: Value of TSPARM. Example: "ASTHMA" when TSPARM value is "Trial Indication". TSVAL can only be null when TSVALNF is populated. Text over 200 characters can be added to additional columns TSVAL1-TSVALn. See Assumption 8.

label: Parameter Value
cdisc_notes: Value of TSPARM. Example: "ASTHMA" when TSPARM value is "Trial Indication". TSVAL cannot be null - a value is required for the record to be valid. Text over 200 characters can be added to additional columns TSVAL1-TSVALn.

label: Parameter Value
cdisc_notes: Value of TSPARM. Example: "ASTHMA" when TSPARM value is "Trial Indication". TSVAL cannot be null - a value is required for the record to be valid. Text over 200 characters can be added to additional columns TSVAL1-TSVALn.

label: Parameter Value
cdisc_notes: Value of TSPARM. Example: "ASTHMA" when TSPARM value is "Trial Indication". TSVAL cannot be null - a value is required for the record to be valid. Text over 200 characters can be added to additional columns TSVAL1-TSVALn.

label: Parameter Value Null Flavor
cdisc_notes: Null flavor for the value of TSPARM, to be populated only if TSVAL is null.

label: Parameter Value Code
cdisc_notes: This is the code of the term in TSVAL. For example, "6CW7F3G59X" is the code for gabapentin; "C49488" is the code for Y. The length of this variable can be longer than 8 to accommodate the length of the external terminology.

label: Name of the Reference Terminology
cdisc_notes: The name of the reference terminology from which TSVALCD is taken. For example; CDISC CT, SNOMED, ISO 8601.

label: Version of the Reference Terminology
cdisc_notes: The version number of the reference terminology, if applicable.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness within a dataset for a subject. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain. Can be used to group split or merged tumors/lesions which have been identified.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Internal or external identifier (e.g., medical image ID number).

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link identified tumor/lesion/location of interest to the assessment results (in TR domain) over the course of the study.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship.

label: Tumor/Lesion ID Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the TEST in TUTEST. TUTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters nor can start with a number. TUTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Example: "TUMIDENT". See assumption 3.

label: Tumor/Lesion ID Test Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test for the tumor/lesion identification. The value in TUTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Example: "Tumor identification". See assumption 3.

label: Modified Result Term
cdisc_notes: If the value of --ORRES is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Category
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Examples: "HEMATOLOGY", "URINALYSIS", "CHEMISTRY", "HAMD 17", "SF36 V2.0 ACUTE", "EGFR MUTATION ANALYSIS".

label: Subcategory
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of --CAT values. Example: "WBC DIFFERENTIAL".

label: Position of Subject During Observation
cdisc_notes: Position of the subject during a measurement or examination. Examples: "SUPINE", "STANDING", "SITTING".

label: Body System or Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Body System or Organ Class that is involved for a finding from the standard hierarchy for dictionary-coded results. Example: MedDRA SOC.

label: Tumor/Lesion ID Result
cdisc_notes: Result of the tumor/lesion identification. The result of tumor/lesion identification is a classification of the identified tumor/lesion. Example: When TUTESTCD = "TUMIDENT", values of TUORRES might be "TARGET", "NON-TARGET", "NEW", or "BENIGN ABNORMALITY".

label: Tumor/Lesion ID Result Std. Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings copied or derived from TUORRES in a standard format.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from --STRESC. --STRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding. Example: "MALIGNANT" or "BENIGN" for tumor findings.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question was not asked or a test was not done, or a test was attempted but did not generate a result. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with --STAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: External File Path
cdisc_notes: Filename for an external file, such as one for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Laboratory/Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: The name or identifier of the vendor that performed the tumor/lesion Identification. This column can be left null when the investigator provides the complete set of data in the domain.

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE", "DNA", "RNA".

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: Defines the condition of the specimen. Example: "CLOUDY".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Location of the Tumor/Lesion
cdisc_notes: Used to specify the anatomical location of the identified tumor/lesion (e.g., "LIVER"). \n Note: When anatomical location is broken down and collected as distinct pieces of data that when combined provide the overall location information (e.g., laterality/directionality/distribution), then additional anatomical location qualifiers should be used. See assumption 3.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality (e.g., "LEFT", "RIGHT", "BILATERAL").

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality (e.g., "UPPER", "INTERIOR").

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, or apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MULTIPLE".

label: Method of Identification
cdisc_notes: Method used to identify the tumor/lesion. Examples: "MRI", "CT SCAN".

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. Should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that TUBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline flag for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Fasting Status
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify fasting status. Valid values include "Y", "N", "U", or null if not relevant.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record (e.g., a record that represents the average of other records such as a computed baseline). Should be "Y" or null.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR". \n This column can be left null when the investigator provides the complete set of data in the domain. However, the column should contain no null values when data from 1 or more independent assessors is included. For example, the rows attributed to the investigator should contain a value of "INVESTIGATOR".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2". See assumption 9.

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than 1 independent assessor (e.g., "RADIOLOGIST 1", "RADIOLOGIST 2", "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE") provide independent assessments at the same time point, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted assessment.

label: Severity/Intensity
cdisc_notes: Describes the severity or intensity of a particular finding. Examples: "MILD", "MODERATE", "SEVERE".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the assessment was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the assessment was made.

label: Date/Time of Tumor/Lesion Identification
cdisc_notes: TUDTC variable represents the date of the scan/image/physical exam. TUDTC does not represent the date that the image was read to identify tumors. TUDTC also does not represent the VISIT date.

label: Study Day of Tumor/Lesion Identification
cdisc_notes: Study day of the scan/image/physical exam, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics.

label: Collected Duration
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of an event, intervention, or finding. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See --TPTNUM and --TPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned Elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (--TPTREF) such as "Previous Dose" or "Previous Meal". This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by --ELTM, --TPTNUM, --TPT, --STINT, and --ENINT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by --TPTREF.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable --ENTPT.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 or other character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by --ENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25" or "VISIT 2".

label: Date of Scan Equivocal Lesion Detected
New Lesion First Observed at this Visit
Method of Test or Examination, Other
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Description of clinical encounter. This is often defined in the protocol. Used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY as a text description of the clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Due to its sequential nature, used for sorting.

label: Planned Arm Code
cdisc_notes: 1. ARMCD is limited to 20 characters and does not have special character restrictions. The maximum length of ARMCD is longer than for other "short" variables to accommodate the kind of values that are likely to be needed for crossover trials. For example, if ARMCD values for a 7-period crossover were constructed using 2-character abbreviations for each treatment and separating hyphens, the length of ARMCD values would be 20. \n 2. If the timing of visits for a trial does not depend on which arm a subject is in, then ARMCD should be null.

label: Description of Planned Arm
cdisc_notes: 1. Name given to an arm or treatment group. \n 2. If the timing of visits for a trial does not depend on which arm a subject is in, then Arm should be left blank.

label: Visit Start Rule
cdisc_notes: Rule describing when the visit starts, in relation to the sequence of elements.

label: Visit End Rule
cdisc_notes: Rule describing when the visit ends, in relation to the sequence of elements.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject. May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier (e.g., lab specimen ID, universally unique identifier (UUID) for a medical image).

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. Example: Preprinted line identifier.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship.

label: Short Name of Urinary Test
cdisc_notes: Short character value for URTEST used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical format to a horizontal format. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in URTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). URTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "COUNT", "LENGTH", "RBLDFLW".

label: Name of Urinary Test
cdisc_notes: Long name For URTESTCD. Examples: "Count", "Length", "Renal Blood Flow".

label: Urinary Test Detail
cdisc_notes: Further description of URTESTCD and URTEST.

label: Category for Urinary Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values.

label: Subcategory for Urinary Test
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of URCAT values.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Unit for URORRES.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings copied or derived from URORRES, in a standard format or in standard units. URSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in URSTRESN.

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from URSTRESC. URSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for URSTRESC and URSTRESN.

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question was not asked or a test was not done, or a test was attempted but did not generate a result. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with URSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement.

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination.

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. The value should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: A baseline defined by the sponsor The value should be "Y" or null. Note that URBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline flag for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record (e.g., a record that represents the average of other records such as a computed baseline). Should be "Y" or null.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "RADIOLOGIST".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in UREVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of a clinical encounter.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT. Should be an integer.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm for the element in which the observation was made.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time at which the observation was made.

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation.

label: Study Day of Visit/Collection/Exam
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point (e.g., time of last dose). See URTPTNUM and URTPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (URTPTREF; e.g., "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL"). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval, represented as ISO duration.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by URELTM, URTPTNUM, and URTPT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by URTPTREF.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID, or UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. May be preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Vital Signs Test Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short name of the measurement, test, or examination described in VSTEST. It can be used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical to a horizontal format. The value in VSTESTCD cannot be longer than 8 characters, nor can it start with a number (e.g., "1TEST" is not valid). VSTESTCD cannot contain characters other than letters, numbers, or underscores. Examples: "SYSBP", "DIABP", "BMI".

label: Vital Signs Test Name
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in VSTEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Systolic Blood Pressure", "Diastolic Blood Pressure", "Body Mass Index".

label: Category for Vital Signs
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records.

label: Subcategory for Vital Signs
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of a measurement or examination.

label: Vital Signs Position of Subject
cdisc_notes: Position of the subject during a measurement or examination. Examples: "SUPINE", "STANDING", "SITTING".

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the vital signs measurement as originally received or collected.

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Original units in which the data were collected. The unit for VSORRES. Examples: "in", "LB", "beats/min".

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from VSORRES in a standard format or standard units. VSSTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in VSSTRESN. For example, if a test has results "NONE", "NEG", and "NEGATIVE" in VSORRES, and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in VSSTRESC as "NEGATIVE".

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from VSSTRESC. VSSTRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized unit used for VSSTRESC and VSSTRESN.

label: Normal Range Lower Limit-Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Lower end of normal range or reference range for standardized results (e.g., --STRESC, --STRESN) represented in standardized units (--STRESU).

label: Normal Range Upper Limit-Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Upper end of normal range or reference range for standardized results (e.g., --STRESC, --STRESN) represented in standardized units (--STRESU).

label: Normal/Reference Range Indicator
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate the value is outside the normal range or reference range. May be defined by --ORNRLO and --ORNRHI or other objective criteria. Examples: "Y", "N"; "HIGH", "LOW"; "NORMAL", "ABNORMAL".

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a vital sign measurement was not done. Should be null if a result exists in VSORRES.

label: Reason Not Performed
cdisc_notes: Describes why a measurement or test was not performed. Examples: "BROKEN EQUIPMENT", "SUBJECT REFUSED". Used in conjunction with VSSTAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Location of Vital Signs Measurement
cdisc_notes: Location relevant to the collection of vital signs measurement. Example: "ARM" for blood pressure.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "EIA" (enzyme immunoassay), "ELECTROPHORESIS", "DIPSTICK".

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Operationally derived indicator used to identify the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC. Should be "Y" or null.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that VSBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record. The value should be "Y" or null. Records that represent the average of other records or that do not come from the CRF are examples of records that would be derived for the submission datasets. If VSDRVFL = "Y," then VSORRES may be null, with VSSTRESC and (if numeric) VSSTRESN having the derived value.

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than one assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted evaluation. Expected values can include "Y", "N", or null. This is not intended to be an analysis flag to indicate acceptability for a given analysis.

label: Toxicity
cdisc_notes: Description of toxicity quantified by VSTOXGR. The sponsor is expected to provide the name of the scale and version used to map the terms, utilizing the external codelist element in the Define-XML document.

label: Standard Toxicity Grade
cdisc_notes: Records toxicity grade value using a standard toxicity scale (e.g., NCI CTCAE). If value is from a numeric scale, represent only the number (e.g., "2", not "Grade 2"). The sponsor is expected to provide the name of the scale and version used to map the terms, utilizing the external codelist element in the Define-XML document.

label: Clinically Significant, Collected
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate whether a collected observation is clinically significant based on judgment.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of the visit based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Planned Order of Element within Arm
cdisc_notes: Number that gives the planned order of the element within the arm.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date/time at which the assessment was made.

label: Date/Time of Measurements
cdisc_notes: Date and time of the vital signs assessment represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Vital Signs
cdisc_notes: Study day of vital signs measurements, measured as integer days. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when measurement should be taken. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point (e.g., time of last dose). See VSTPTNUM and VSTPTREF. Examples: "START", "5 MIN POST".

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numerical version of VSTPT to aid in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned elapsed time (in ISO 8601) relative to a planned fixed reference (VSTPTREF). This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date time variable. Represented as an ISO 8601 Duration. Examples: "-PT15M" to represent the period of 15 minutes prior to the reference point indicated by VSTPTREF, "PT8H" to represent the period of 8 hours after the reference point indicated by VSTPTREF.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Name of the fixed reference point referred to by VSELTM, VSTPTNUM, and VSTPT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time of the reference time point, VSTPTREF.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: Dose of Last Invest. Product Intake
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject (or within a parameter, in the case of the Trial Summary domain). May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain. Also used to link together a block of related records in the Trial Summary Information (Section 3.3).

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID, or UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. Example: pre-printed line identifier on a Concomitant Medications page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Name of Intervention
cdisc_notes: The topic for the intervention observation, usually the verbatim name of the treatment, drug, medicine, or therapy given during the dosing interval for the observation.

label: Standardized Intervention Name
cdisc_notes: Standardized or dictionary-derived name of the topic variable, --TRT, or the modified topic variable (--MODIFY), if applicable. Equivalent to the generic drug name in WHO Drug, or a term in SNOMED, ICD9, or other published or sponsor-defined dictionaries.

label: Category for Intervention
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values.

label: Subcategory for Intervention
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of --CAT values.

label: Pre-specified
cdisc_notes: Used when a specific intervention is pre-specified on a CRF. Values should be "Y" or null.

label: Occurrence
cdisc_notes: Used to record whether a pre-specified intervention occurred when information about the occurrence of a specific intervention is solicited.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate when a question about the occurrence of a pre-specified intervention was not answered. Should be null or have a value of NOT DONE.

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with --STAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: Indication
cdisc_notes: Denotes the indication for the intervention (e.g., why the therapy was taken or administered).

label: Class
cdisc_notes: Class for a medication or treatment, often obtained from a coding dictionary.

label: Class Code
cdisc_notes: Used to represent code for --CLAS.

label: Dose
cdisc_notes: Amount of --TRT given. Not populated when --DOSTXT is populated.

label: Dose Description
cdisc_notes: Dosing information collected in text form. Examples: <1 per day, 200-400. Not populated when --DOSE is populated.

label: Dose Units
cdisc_notes: Units for --DOSE, --DOSTOT, or --DOSTXT. Examples: "ng", "mg", "mg/kg".

label: Dose Form
cdisc_notes: Dose form for the treatment. Examples: "TABLET", "CAPSULE".

label: Dosing Frequency Per Interval
cdisc_notes: Usually expressed as the number of doses given per a specific interval. Examples: "Q2H", "QD", "PRN".

label: Total Daily Dose using DOSU
cdisc_notes: Total daily dose of --TRT using the units in --DOSU. Used when dosing is collected as Total Daily Dose.

label: Intended Dose Regimen
cdisc_notes: Text description of the (intended) schedule or regimen for the Intervention. Example: "TWO WEEKS ON, TWO WEEKS OFF".

label: Route of Administration
cdisc_notes: Route of administration for the intervention. Examples: "ORAL", "INTRAVENOUS".

label: Lot Number
cdisc_notes: Lot number for the intervention described in --TRT.

label: Location of Intervention
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of an intervention, such as an injection site. Example: ARM for an injection.

label: Treatment Vehicle
cdisc_notes: Vehicle for administration of treatment, such as a liquid in which the treatment drug is dissolved. Example: "SALINE".

label: Treatment Vehicle Amount
cdisc_notes: Amount of the prepared product (treatment + vehicle) administered or given. Note: should not be diluent amount alone.

label: Treatment Vehicle Amount Unit
cdisc_notes: Units for the prepared product (treatment + vehicle). Examples: "mL", "mg".

label: Reason for Dose Adjustment
cdisc_notes: Describes reason or explanation of why a dose is adjusted. Examples "ADVERSE EVENT", "INSUFFICIENT RESPONSE", "NON-MEDICAL REASON".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date or start date and time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected (see Section Trial Arms).

label: Date/Time of Intervention
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation.

label: Start Date/Time of Intervention
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of an observation.

label: End Date/Time of Intervention
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the observation.

label: Study Day of Intervention
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of Start of Intervention
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of start of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Intervention
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Duration of Intervention
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of an event, intervention, or finding. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See --TPTNUM and --TPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned Elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (--TPTREF) such as "Previous Dose" or "Previous Meal". This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by --ELTM, --TPTNUM, --TPT, --STINT, and --ENINT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Start Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the observation as being before, during, or after the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics.
