label: End Relative to Reference Period
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before, during or after the sponsor-defined reference period. The sponsor-defined reference period is a continuous period of time defined by a discrete starting point and a discrete ending point represented by RFSTDTC and RFENDTC in Demographics.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: Start Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the start of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable --STTPT.

label: Start Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 or other character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by --STRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-15" or "VISIT 1".

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable --ENTPT.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 or other character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by --ENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25" or "VISIT 2".

label: Reason Scanner Not Used at Baseline
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject (or within a parameter, in the case of the Trial Summary domain). May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1. Should be assigned to be in a consistent chronological order.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID, or UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. Example: pre-printed line identifier on a Concomitant Medications page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link a feature (such as a tumor) initially recorded as a result in one findings domain to assessments of that feature in another findings domain. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For Example: A single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessment performed at each study visit.

label: Reported Term for the Event
cdisc_notes: Topic variable for an event observation, which is the verbatim or pre-specified name of the event.

label: Category for the Event
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values.

label: Subcategory for the Event
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of --CAT values.

label: Pre-Specified
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate whether the Event in YBTERM was pre-specified. Value is "Y" for pre-specified events and null for spontaneously reported events.

label: Occurrence Indicator
cdisc_notes: Used when the occurrence of specific events is solicited, to indicate whether or not a clinical event occurred. Values are null for spontaneously reported events.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date or start date and time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected (see Section Trial Arms).

label: Date/Time of Event Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation represented in IS0 8601 character format.

label: Start Date/Time of Event
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of an observation represented in IS0 8601 character format.

label: Study Day of Event Collection
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of Start of Event
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of start of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Event Sent to Committee for Furth Assmnt
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject (or within a parameter, in the case of the Trial Summary domain). May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1. Should be assigned to be in a consistent chronological order.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. Example: pre-printed line identifier on a Concomitant Medications page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link a feature (such as a tumor) initially recorded as a result in one findings domain to assessments of that feature in another findings domain. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For Example: A single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessment performed at each study visit.

label: Reported Term for the Medication Error
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the event.

label: Lowest Level Term
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived text description of the lowest level term.

label: Lowest Level Term Code
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived code for the lowest level term.

label: Dictionary-Derived Term
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived text description of YMTERM or YMMODIFY. Equivalent to the Preferred Term. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to map the terms utilizing the external codelist element in the Define-XML document.

label: Preferred Term Code
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived code for the preferred term.

label: High Level Term
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived text description of the high level term for the primary system organ class (SOC).

label: High Level Term Code
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived code for the high level term for the primary SOC.

label: High Level Group Term
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived text description of the high level group term for the primary SOC.

label: High Level Group Term Code
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived code for the high level group term for the primary SOC.

label: Category for Medication Error Event
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records. Examples: IMP,Device,IMP and Device

label: Subcategory for Medication Error Event
cdisc_notes: Used to define a Sub category of related records.

label: Medication Error Event Pre-specified
cdisc_notes: Used when the occurrence of specific events is solicited, to indicate whether or not a medication error event occurred. Values are null for spontaneously reported events.

label: Medication Error Event Occurance
cdisc_notes: Used when the occurrence of specific events is solicited, to indicate whether or not a medication error event occurred. Values are null for spontaneously reported events.

label: Body System or Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Dictionary derived. Body system or organ class used by the sponsor from the coding dictionary (e.g., MedDRA). When using a multi-axial dictionary such as MedDRA, this should contain the SOC used for the sponsor's analyses and summary tables, which may not necessarily be the primary SOC.

label: Body System or Organ Class Code
cdisc_notes: Dictionary derived. Code for the body system or organ class used by the sponsor. When using a multi-axial dictionary such as MedDRA, this should contain the SOC used for the sponsor's analyses and summary tables, which may not necessarily be the primary SOC.

label: Primary System Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived text description of the primary SOC.

label: Primary System Organ Class Code
cdisc_notes: Dictionary-derived code for the primary SOC.

label: Serious Event
cdisc_notes: Is this a serious event? Valid values are "Y" and "N".

label: Action Taken
cdisc_notes: Describes of Error Occurrence, Action Taken.

label: Causality
cdisc_notes: Records the investigator's opinion as to the causality of the event to the treatment. ICH does not establish any required or recommended terms for non-device relatedness. ICH E2A and E2B examples include (up-cased here for alignment to SDTM conventions) terms such as "NOT RELATED", "UNLIKELY RELATED", "POSSIBLY RELATED", "RELATED", but these example terms do not establish any conventions or expectations. Controlled terminology may be defined in the future. Check with regulatory authority for population of this variable.

label: Start Date/Time of Medication Error
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of the Medication Error event represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: End Date/Time of Medication Error
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the Medication Error event represented in ISO 8601 character format.

label: Desc of Potent Error and Possible Harm
Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within a domain. May be any valid number.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Used to tie together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject.

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID, or UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined reference number. Perhaps preprinted on the CRF as an explicit line identifier or defined in the sponsor's operational database. Example: Line number on a CRF.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Adjudication Test
cdisc_notes: Verbatim name of the test or examination used to obtain the measurement or finding. The value in FATEST cannot be longer than 40 characters. Examples: "Severity/Intensity", "Occurrence". Note that controlled terminology is in several therapeutic area-specific codelists.

label: Object of Interest
cdisc_notes: Used to describe the object or focal point of the findings observation that is represented by --TEST. Examples: the term (such as Acne) describing a clinical sign or symptom that is being measured by a Severity test, or an event such as VOMIT, where the volume of Vomit is being measured by a VOLUME test.

label: Category
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of related records. Examples: "GERD", "PRE-SPECIFIED AE".
az_notes: Used to define if the adjudication is a first adjudication or re-adjudication. Per FDA guidelines: Used to define if the adjudication is a first adjudication or re-adjudication.

label: Subcategory
cdisc_notes: A further categorization of FACAT.
az_notes: Used to define a further categorization of --CAT values. Used to identify the type of Adjudicated Event

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the test as originally received or collected.

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from FAORRES in a standard format or standard units. FASTRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in FASTRESN. For example, if a test has results "NONE", "NEG", and "NEGATIVE" in FAORRES, and these results effectively have the same meaning; they could be represented in standard format in FASTRESC as "NEGATIVE".

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Should be null for records that contain collected or derived data. Examples: "INVESTIGATOR", "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "VENDOR".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than one assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted evaluation. Expected values can include "Y", "N", or null. This is not intended to be an analysis flag to indicate acceptability for a given analysis.
az_notes: Identifies records that have been determined to be the accepted assessment by an independent assessor. This flag would be provided by an independent assessor and when multiple evaluators (e.g., "Adjudicator 1", "Adjudicator 2", and "CEC Adjudication Committee") provide assessment or evaluations at the same time point or an overall evaluation.

label: Unique Repetition Number
az_notes: This is optional variable used group each block of records resulting from the assessment of a single adjudicator per adjudication event.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the date/time of the observation. Examples: "SCREENING", "TREATMENT", "FOLLOW-UP".
az_notes: Epoch associated with the start date or start date and time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected (see Section Trial Arms).

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of findings assessment represented in ISO 8601 character format.
az_notes: Collection date and time of an observation represented in IS0 8601 character format.

label: Start Date/Time of Observation
cdisc_notes: Start date/time of the observation.
az_notes: Start date/time of an observation represented in IS0 8601 character format.

label: End Date/Time of Observation
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the observation.

label: Study Day of Collection
cdisc_notes: 1. Study day of collection, measured as integer days. 2. Algorithm for calculations must be relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC variable in Demographics. This formula should be consistent across the submission.
az_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of Start of Observation
az_notes: Actual study day of start of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Observation
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject (or within a parameter, in the case of the Trial Summary domain). May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain. Also used to link together a block of related records in the Trial Summary Information (Section 3.3).

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID, or UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. Example: pre-printed line identifier on a Concomitant Medications page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Resource Utilization Short Name
cdisc_notes: Short character value for --TEST used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical format to a horizontal format. The short value can be up to 8 characters. Examples: "PLAT", "SYSBP", "RRMIN", "EYEEXAM".

label: Resource Utilization Name
cdisc_notes: Long name For --TESTCD. Examples: Platelets, Systolic Blood Pressure, Summary (Min) RR Duration, Eye Examination.

label: Modified Term for Result
cdisc_notes: If the value of --ORRES is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Category for Resource Utilization
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Examples: "HEMATOLOGY", "URINALYSIS", "CHEMISTRY", "HAMD 17", "SF36 V2.0 ACUTE", "EGFR MUTATION ANALYSIS".

label: Subcategory for Resource Utilization
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of --CAT values. Example: "WBC DIFFERENTIAL".

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected. Examples: "120", "<1", "POS".

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Unit for --ORRES and --ORREF. Examples: "in", "LB", "kg/L".

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from --ORRES in a standard format or in standard units. --STRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in --STRESN. For example, if various tests have results "NONE", "NEG", and "NEGATIVE" in --ORRES and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in --STRESC as "NEGATIVE".

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from --STRESC. --STRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for --STRESC, --STRESN, --STREFC, and --STREFN. Example: "mol/L".

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding. Example: "MALIGNANT" or "BENIGN" for tumor findings.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question was not asked or a test was not done, or a test was attempted but did not generate a result. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with --STAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: External Filename
cdisc_notes: Filename for an external file, such as one for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Laboratory/Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: Name or identifier of the vendor (e.g., laboratory) that provided the test results.

label: Specimen Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE", "DNA", "RNA".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Method of Test or Examination
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "EIA" (enzyme immunoassay), "ELECTROPHORESIS", "DIPSTICK".

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Last Observation Before Exposure Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that ZCBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null. Note that ZCBLFL is retained for backward compatibility. The authoritative baseline for statistical analysis is in an ADaM dataset.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record (e.g., a record that represents the average of other records such as a computed baseline). Should be "Y" or null.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "RADIOLOGIST".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record (e.g., a record that represents the average of other records such as a computed baseline). Should be "Y" or null.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "RADIOLOGIST".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than one assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted evaluation. Expected values can include "Y", "N", or null. This is not intended to be an analysis flag to indicate acceptability for a given analysis.

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

label: Planned Study Day of Visit
cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

label: Epoch
cdisc_notes: Epoch associated with the start date or start date and time of the observation, or the date/time of collection if start date/time is not collected (see Section Trial Arms).

label: Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: Collection date and time of an observation.

label: End Date/Time of Collection
cdisc_notes: End date/time of the observation.

label: Study Day of Collection
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of visit/collection/exam expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Study Day of End of Collection
cdisc_notes: Actual study day of end of observation expressed in integer days relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC in Demographics.

label: Duration
cdisc_notes: Collected duration of an event, intervention, or finding. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

label: Planned Time Point Name
cdisc_notes: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point, such as time of last dose. See --TPTNUM and --TPTREF.

label: Planned Time Point Number
cdisc_notes: Numeric version of planned time point used in sorting.

label: Planned Elapsed Time from Time Point Ref
cdisc_notes: Planned Elapsed time relative to a planned fixed reference (--TPTREF) such as "Previous Dose" or "Previous Meal". This variable is useful where there are repetitive measures. Not a clock time or a date/time variable, but an interval.

label: Time Point Reference
cdisc_notes: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by --ELTM, --TPTNUM, --TPT, --STINT, and --ENINT. Examples: "PREVIOUS DOSE", "PREVIOUS MEAL".

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by --TPTREF.

label: Evaluation Interval
cdisc_notes: Duration of interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD. Usually used with --DTC to describe an interval of this duration that ended at the time represented in --DTC. Example: "-P2M" to represent a period of the past 2 months as the evaluation interval for a question from a questionnaire.

label: End Relative to Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Identifies the end of the observation as being before or after the sponsor-defined reference time point defined by variable --ENTPT.

label: End Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Description or date/time in ISO 8601 or other character format of the sponsor-defined reference point referred to by --ENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25" or "VISIT 2".

label: Study Identifier
cdisc_notes: Unique identifier for a study.

label: Domain Abbreviation
cdisc_notes: Two-character abbreviation for the domain.

label: Unique Subject Identifier
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications or submissions involving the product.

label: Sequence Number
cdisc_notes: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject (or within a parameter, in the case of the Trial Summary domain). May be any valid number (including decimals) and does not have to start at 1.

label: Group ID
cdisc_notes: Optional group identifier, used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain. Also used to link together a block of related records in the Trial Summary Information (Section 3.3).

label: Reference ID
cdisc_notes: Optional internal or external identifier such as lab specimen ID, or UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Sponsor-Defined Identifier
cdisc_notes: Sponsor-defined identifier. Example: pre-printed line identifier on a Concomitant Medications page.

label: Link ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. For example, a single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

label: Link Group ID
cdisc_notes: Identifier used to link related records across domains. This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. For example, multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response to therapy determination record.

label: Short Name of Organ Measurement
cdisc_notes: Short character value for --TEST used as a column name when converting a dataset from a vertical format to a horizontal format. The short value can be up to 8 characters. Examples: "PLAT", "SYSBP", "RRMIN", "EYEEXAM".

label: Name of Organ Measurement
cdisc_notes: Long name For --TESTCD. Examples: Platelets, Systolic Blood Pressure, Summary (Min) RR Duration, Eye Examination.

label: Modified Term for Result
cdisc_notes: If the value of --ORRES is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.

label: Category for Organ Measurement
cdisc_notes: Used to define a category of topic-variable values. Examples: "HEMATOLOGY", "URINALYSIS", "CHEMISTRY", "HAMD 17", "SF36 V2.0 ACUTE", "EGFR MUTATION ANALYSIS".

label: Subcategory for Organ Measurement
cdisc_notes: Used to define a further categorization of --CAT values. Example: "WBC DIFFERENTIAL".

label: Position of Subject During Observation
cdisc_notes: Position of the subject during a measurement or examination. Examples: "SUPINE", "STANDING", "SITTING".

label: Body System or Organ Class
cdisc_notes: Body System or Organ Class that is involved for a finding from the standard hierarchy for dictionary-coded results. Example: MedDRA SOC.

label: Result or Finding in Original Units
cdisc_notes: Result of the measurement or finding as originally received or collected. Examples: "120", "<1", "POS".

label: Original Units
cdisc_notes: Unit for --ORRES and --ORREF. Examples: "in", "LB", "kg/L".

label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
cdisc_notes: Contains the result value for all findings, copied or derived from --ORRES in a standard format or in standard units. --STRESC should store all results or findings in character format; if results are numeric, they should also be stored in numeric format in --STRESN. For example, if various tests have results "NONE", "NEG", and "NEGATIVE" in --ORRES and these results effectively have the same meaning, they could be represented in standard format in --STRESC as "NEGATIVE".

label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Used for continuous or numeric results or findings in standard format; copied in numeric format from --STRESC. --STRESN should store all numeric test results or findings.

label: Standard Units
cdisc_notes: Standardized units used for --STRESC, --STRESN, --STREFC, and --STREFN. Example: "mol/L".

label: Result Category
cdisc_notes: Used to categorize the result of a finding. Example: "MALIGNANT" or "BENIGN" for tumor findings.

label: Completion Status
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate that a question was not asked or a test was not done, or a test was attempted but did not generate a result. Should be null or have a value of "NOT DONE".

label: Reason Not Done
cdisc_notes: Reason not done. Used in conjunction with --STAT when value is "NOT DONE".

label: External Filename
cdisc_notes: Filename for an external file, such as one for an ECG waveform or a medical image.

label: Laboratory/Vendor Name
cdisc_notes: Name or identifier of the vendor (e.g., laboratory) that provided the test results.

label: Specimen Material Type
cdisc_notes: Defines the type of specimen used for a measurement. Examples: "SERUM", "PLASMA", "URINE", "DNA", "RNA".

label: Specimen Condition
cdisc_notes: Defines the condition of the specimen. Example: "CLOUDY".

label: Specimen Usability for the Test
cdisc_notes: Describes the usability of the specimen for the test. The value will be "N" if the specimen is not usable, and null if the specimen is usable.

label: Location Used for the Measurement
cdisc_notes: Anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement. Examples: "RECTAL" for temperature, "ARM" for blood pressure.

label: Laterality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing laterality. Examples: "RIGHT", "LEFT", "BILATERAL".

label: Directionality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing directionality. Examples: "ANTERIOR", "LOWER", "PROXIMAL".

label: Portion or Totality
cdisc_notes: Qualifier for anatomical location or specimen further detailing the distribution, which means arrangement of, apportioning of. Examples: "ENTIRE", "SINGLE", "SEGMENT", "MANY".

label: Method of Organ Measurement
cdisc_notes: Method of the test or examination. Examples: "EIA" (enzyme immunoassay), "ELECTROPHORESIS", "DIPSTICK".

label: Lead Identified to Collect Measurements
cdisc_notes: Lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument. Examples: "LEAD I", "LEAD V2", "LEAD CM5".

label: Consciousness State
cdisc_notes: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement. Examples: "CONSCIOUS", "SEMI-CONSCIOUS", "UNCONSCIOUS".

label: Baseline Flag
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify a baseline value. Should be "Y" or null.

label: Fasting Status
cdisc_notes: Indicator used to identify fasting status. Valid values include "Y", "N", "U", or null if not relevant.

label: Derived Flag
cdisc_notes: Used to indicate a derived record (e.g., a record that represents the average of other records such as a computed baseline). Should be "Y" or null.

label: Evaluator
cdisc_notes: Role of the person who provided the evaluation. Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). Examples: "ADJUDICATION COMMITTEE", "INDEPENDENT ASSESSOR", "RADIOLOGIST".

label: Evaluator Identifier
cdisc_notes: Used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role recorded in --EVAL. Examples: "RADIOLOGIST1", "RADIOLOGIST2".

label: Accepted Record Flag
cdisc_notes: In cases where more than one assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response, this flag identifies the record that is considered to be the accepted evaluation. Expected values can include "Y", "N", or null. This is not intended to be an analysis flag to indicate acceptability for a given analysis.

label: Severity
cdisc_notes: Describes the severity or intensity of a particular finding. Examples: "MILD", "MODERATE", "SEVERE".

label: Visit Number
cdisc_notes: 1. Clinical encounter number. 2. Numeric version of VISIT, used for sorting.

label: Visit Name
cdisc_notes: 1. Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter. 2. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY.

cdisc_notes: Planned study day of VISIT based upon RFSTDTC in Demographics. Should be an integer.

cdisc_notes: End date/time of the observation.

label: Date/Time of Reference Time Point
cdisc_notes: Date/time for a fixed reference time point defined by --TPTREF.
